inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1916.)
VALLANĀḌU AGARAM, SRIVAIKUNTAM TALUK,
TINNAVELLY DISTRICT.
AÑJĀNIṈRA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH, WEST AND
SOUTH WALL OF CENTRAL SHRINE
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVANA CHAKRVARTIN VIKRAMA
PĀṆḌYA DĒVA-14TH YEAR (2+12).
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete record. It mentions Vallanāḍu alias
Jayaṅgoṇḍa Pāṇḍiyanallūr. Seems to record the allotment of shares of
nañjey, Puñjey, nattam lands besides pond, house-sites to the following
brāhmanās viz., Maṅgalūr-Sōmanāthabhaṭṭaṉ, Sentirattu-Jñana Eṭṭukūra-
śridharabhaṭṭaṉ, Śrī Puḷḷūr Uṭbāhu-Sundarabhaṭṭaṉ, Karuñjey Narasiṅgaṉ,
Perumayyalūr Dharmarājaṉ, Kaṇḍiyūr Tiruvēṅgaḍa uḍaiya-rājanārayana
Brahmarāyaṉ etc., of various gōtra and sūtras.

edition.l:
svastiśrī komāṟavarmmarāṉa tribhuvaṉaccakkaṟavattikaḷ śrī
vikkiṟamapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 vatu etir 12 vatu tai mātam 12
vallanāṭāṉa ceyaṅkoṇṭa pāṇṭiyanallūr melaikkuṟucciyil
tirunāmattāl vaitta [ vikra] mapāṇḍyaccaturvvēdima ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1916.)
VALLANĀḌU AGARAM, SRIVAIKUNTAM TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
AÑJĀNIṈRA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE
[MĀṞAVARMAN] ALIAS TRIBHUVANA-CHAKRAVARTIN
KŌṈĒRIMAIKOṆḌĀṈ SUNDARAPĀṆḌYADĒVA-(2+9) YEAR/
ŚAKA 1460 = 1538 A.D.
inscription.p:
Badly damaged. Seems to register a gift of tax free land to a
brāhmana, viz., Harinā[rāyaṇa]bhaṭṭaṉ of Bharatvājagōtram, probably for
performing pūja in the temple.

edition.l:
[svastiśrī komāṟavarmarāna] tribhuvana cakravatti konermai koṇṭān
śrī

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1916.)
VALLANĀḌU AGARAM, SRIVAIKUNTAM TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
CHAKRAPĀṆI PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH, WEST AND
NORTH, TIERS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
CHAKRAVARTTIGAL ŚRI VIKRAMAPĀṆḌYA DĒVAR-5TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Fragments. Seems to record the grant of land for supply of one
āḻākku of ghee to the god.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1916.)
VALLANĀḌU AGARAM, SRIVAIKUNTAM TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
CHAKRAPĀṆI PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE
MĀRA ALIAS TRIBH. KŌṈĒRIMAIKOṆḌĀṈ PARĀKRAMA PĀṆḌYA
DĒVA.+28TH YEAR (1535 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription is partly built in the wall. It registers the gift of
land as Tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam for supplying sacred offerings such as Tirumālai
(garlands), Tiruparivaṭṭam (cloth) and Tiruviḷakkeṇṇai (castor oil) on the
occasion of the service, Parākrama Pāṇḍyaṉ sandi named after the king in
the temple of Āḻiṅgapperumāḷ Nayiṉār at Vallanāḍu [Jayaṅ]goṇḍa
Pāṇḍyanallūr.

edition.p: Built in at the end of bottom and damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1916.)
VALLANĀḌU AGARAM, SRIVAIKUNTAM TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
CHAKRAPĀṆI PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE FOUR SIDES OF THE
BALIPI6ṬHA IN FRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE
•••••••••• 10TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is highly damaged, and also fragmentary. It mentions
the name of the division Vallanāḍu alias Jayaṅgoṇḍa Pāṉ[ḍiyapuram*]. One
of the fragmentary inscriptions mentions the king, Tribhuvaṇachakravartigal
Śri Vikramapāṇḍyadēvar Jayaṅgoṇḍa Pāṇḍipurattu Śri Kulaśēkharadēvar,
respectively. Another one refers to a land donation to burn lamps before
the deity etc.

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1916.)
SUGANDALAI, TIRUCHCHENDUR TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
IḌAIVIṈAITĪRTA-PERUMĀL TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHU. VĪRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA,-11TH YEAR,
[13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 4 mā of tax free (iṟaiyili) land
by the mahāsabhā of the village for offering sacred food in the name of
Śrī Sēnāpati Āḻvār to the temple of Tirunārāyaṇa-viṇṇagar-Emberumāṉ at
Śivaṉdalai alias Tirunārāyaṇa-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam on the occasion of the
birth day of Dāmodaraṉ alias Āṉaikkarul-cheydapirāṉ-dāsaṉ. Also mentions
the signatories like Aḻagiyamaṇavāḻnambi, Kākkularnilapirāṉ Dāmodaraṉ
alias Āṉaikkarulcheydapirāṉ, Sāttaṉ etc. It also mention the name of the
engraver Sāttaṉ alias Aḻagaṉ Veṅkādaṉ alias Kuḍanāṭṭu Āchārya at the
end of the record.

emperumāṉ koyil

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1916.)
SUGANDALAI, TIRUCHCHENDUR TALUK,
TINNEVELLY DISTRICT.
IḌAIVIṈAITĪRTA-PERUMĀL TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription contains the message that at the request of
Kāliṅgarāyaṉ, the king renovated the temple of Tirunārayaṇa-viṇṇagar-
Emberumāṉ, and setup the image of Garjārchchaṇai Āḻvār in it. Also it
mentions the signatories like Toṇḍaimāṉ and Pallavarāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHU. SUND[A*]RA-PĀṆḌYA DĒVA-5TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land for rebuilding the
compound wall and installation of the deity Tiruttiyūrmuṭṭattu
sōmaviḷāgamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār by Māḷava-chakravartti, who also gave some
tax-free land.

edition.l: svastiśrī śrī komāṟapanmarāṉa [ tribhuvaṉacakra]
ya dēvaṟki yāṇṭu 5- vatu tulānāyaṟṟu tinan civa tina na

l.ta: nām civantampi cā ••••• kku nāḷ añcum

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
••••••••••
inscription.p:
Beginning lost. This inscription contains the right of worship in the
temple of Sōmaviḷagamuḍaiyār, which was bestowed upon certain person
named Nichchaiyigaṇḍa-Bhaṭṭaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
•••••••••• 2+29TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription at the left side portion is built in wall. It seems to
register that the land was donated by a certain Gāṅgēyaṉ. Also mentions
the village Tiruttiyūr-muṭṭam.

edition.p: Built in at the left side.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
TRIBHUVANA CHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA.
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription refers to a village named as Hṛinya
(Hiranya simhamaṅgalam). Also refers to gift of some land for the
preparation of Vyañjana (sidedish) and supply of the same to the temple.

l.ta: la nallūr vaḷaiyil cuṟṟu muṟṟum nam[mu]pāti ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
JAṬĀVARMAṈ ALIAS TRIBHU. PARĀKRAMA PĀṆḌYADĒVA-30TH
YEAR [15TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a village for the performance of
the service called Raṇavīraṉsandi in the temple of Tiruchchaṉṉavaṉa-
muḍaiya-Nāyaṉ[ā]r at Iḍaikkaḍambaṅguḷam alias Iraṇyaśiṅgamaṅgalam,
hamlet situated on the east of Tiruttiyūr muṭṭam by Gāṅgēyaṉ, who held
the birudas Iravikulamaṉṉaṉ, Ēkāṅgavīraṉ, Urubanārayaṇaṉ, Śiraṅganādar,
and Iraṇavīrapperumāḷ for warding off the evil effect of the combination
of Sittirai (Chitra) and Saturday.

l.ta: yiṟ cuṟṟu muṟṟilum māṉa po ••••• ḷukkuppu
ṟam maṉittar iṟaiyili niṅka ••••• ḻan te
ttuk kalveṭṭiṉa paṭi iṉṉāḷ mutal naṭatti ••••••••••
ṟuppāl uḷḷa oṉṟu pātiyum āka ūr muṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHU, PARĀKRAMA PĀṆḌYA DĒVA,
2+28 = 30TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of land as kuḍinīngā-kāṇi made in
accordance with a previous grant of Mummuḍichōḻar Māḷavamāṇikkam
alias Uḍaiyār Māḷavachakravartin, for maintaining a lamp in the same
temple, by the chief mentioned in No. 12.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA-13+10=25TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the gift of taxes, by Māḷavachakravartin
mentioned in No. 13, for making offerings in the temples of
Tiruchchaṉṉavaṉamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Ilaikkaḍambaṅguḷam alias
Hiranyasiṁhmaṅgalam, hamlet situated on the east of Piḷār alias
Sundarapāṇḍyanallūr in Tiruttiyūrmuṭṭam.

edition.l: 10- t tāvatu simhanāyaṟṟu orunāḷ tirut
ḷa kaṭamai āntarāyamum 16- cāṇ
ṭamaikkālāl 309- yum āntarāya
kkum aṟpikai[k] kuṟuvai varaku 1- pātiyum

l.ta:
ttāluḷḷa kaṇakkup peṟu veṭṭi muṭ[ṭu]āḷum cilvari peruvariyu
maṟṟum epperp

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIKRAMAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-
11TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land by a person for removing
silt in the tank annually by a person named, Poyyāmoḻi-Āḷuḍaiyāṉ. The
land was measured by Seventeen-jāṇ kōl.

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA-
13+12TH = 25TH YEAR [13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land for making offerings to the goddess
Tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai-Nāchchiyār set up by the chief Māḷavachakravartin
mentioned in No. 13. He also gifted all the taxes like Kaḍamai, Antrāyam,
Silvari, Peruvari Veṭṭi-muṭṭavāl etc., for the supply of sacred food offering
to the deity in the temple of Tiruchchaṉṉavaṉamuḍaiyār at
Ilaikkaḍambaṅkuḷam alias Hiraṉyavamaṅgalam a hamlet of pillar alias
Sundarapāṇḍyanallūr in Tiruttiyūr muttam.

edition.l: ṇṭu 13- vatin etir 12- vatu mēṣanā
yaṟṟu 11- ntiyati nāḷ tiruttiyūr muṭṭa

l.ta: kaḷukku nām nir vārttu kuṭutta pari
l uḷḷa aḷavu nel uḷpaṭa kaṭamai antarā

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
MĀṞAVA[RMAN] ALIAS TRIBHUVA[NA CHAKRAVARTIN]-
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA, WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE [ALL]
COUNTRIES-23RD YEAR.
inscription.p:
Beginning lost. This inscription records the gift of land to the temple
of Tiruchchaṉṉavaṉa muḍaiyār, by the assembly of Sundarapāṇḍya-
chatūrvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Muttū[ṟṟu kū]ṟṟam for the supply of
sacred food to the temple.

edition.l:
ṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulacekaratevaṟku yāṇṭu 23- vatu
vruścikanā

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHU. KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA-6TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of land by the ūrār of the village
Ilañjēripaṟṟu near Poyyil in Kīḻkuṇḍāru, a sub-division of
Kēralaśiṅgavaḷanāḍu, to Māṇikkaṉ Saṉṉaṉ alias Saṇḍamāruḍa-vayirākkirār,
an Āṇḍār of Tiruchchaṉṉavānam, a sub-division in Tiruppumaṇḍapura after
deducting all the taxes like silvari, peruvari veṭṭimuṭṭāval, for the purpose of
guarding maṇḍapa.

edition.l:

kīḻkuṇṭāṟṟup poyyilippāl illañceri

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
MĀṞAVA[RMAN] ALIAS TRIBHUVA[NA CHAKRAVARTIN]-
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA, WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE [ALL]
COUNTRIES 38th YEAR [14TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land for making offerings to the
dieity in the same temple, by a person Sandhippiḷḷai alias Viṉāyaga
bhagavaṉ, the resident of Kaḻanivāyiḻ a hamlet of Maṟittulagaṅgātta
Pāṇḍyapuram in Maṟittulangaṅgātta Pāṇḍya Vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l: cekaratevaṟku yāṇṭu 38- vatu pruścika nāyaṟṟu triyō

l.ta: ka •••ṉṉa inṉāyaṉār tiruvaiy[kā] • • t tirunāḷukku mu
koṇṭa pi ••••• vayakkal kuṭimuḻutum uppāṟṟil nā
ttu kallilum cempilum veṭṭik kuṭutteṉ ippaṭi
kku naṅkaṉ cattippiḷḷaiyāṉa viṉāyaka[paka]vaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭi

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
•••••••••• TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN •••••••••• WHO
WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL COUNTRIES-[18]TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is in damaged condition. It records a gift of land as
tax-free kārāṇmai for offering to the deity in the shrine of
Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-muḍaiya-Nāchchiyār of the same temple, by
Śivanindakālaṉ Tirukkāṉappēruḍai[yā]ṉ alias Māḷavachakravarti who also
figures as one of the signatories. The other signatories mentioned are
Kulśēkhara viḻupparaiyaṉ, Śivanindakāla mūvēndavēḷāṉ alias Uttamapāṇḍya
Brahmarāyaṉ and Gurukularāyaṉ etc.

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAPĀṆḌYA
-10TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscriptions records the gift of money and land after deducting
the taxes like Pañjupili Poṉvari, Paṟamai etc by Tirukkāṉappēruḍaiyāṉ
Śivaṉindakālaṉ alias Māḷavachakravartiṉ, for the maintenance of Brāhmanas
of Sāttamaṅgalam in Tiruttiyūrmuṭṭam and also to conduct special worship
in the same temple every year on his birthday. It also mentions the
signatories such as Māḷavachakravarti, Gurukulataraiyaṉ and Uttamapāṇḍyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
PARĀKRA[MA] PĀṆḌYA DĒVA ••••• 18TH YEAR, (15TH CENTURY
A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land for burning lamp inthe
temple by the chief Raṇavīraperumāḷ alias Gāṅgēyaṉ, for eliminating bad
effect of some omen noticed while burning lamp.

edition.l:
śrī parākra[ma] pāṇṭiya tevaṟku āṇṭu 1[8] āvatu •••••••••• ā[ni]- 22 §
caṉṉavaṉa muṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku ciraṅkanā •••••

iraṇa[virappe]rumā[ḷ]āṉa kā[ṅ]keyaṉeṉ ••••• acaṉa kālattil
tiruviḷakku niṉṟa to

acaṉa tiruviḷakku •••••••••• iṟaiyili kārāṇmaiyāka cantirātittaval

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE. MĀṞAVARMAṈ
ALIAS TRIBHUVA[NA CHAKRAVARTIN]-KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA,
WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL COUNTRIES, 26th YEAR [13TH
CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of barren land by Chakravarti
Sōmaṉ Koṟṟaṉ and others of the ūrār of Kurumpaṟṟaṅguḍi (near) Illañjēri
in Maṟittulagaṅgāttapāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple, and subsequently they
made provision for irrigation facilities. At the end of the record mentions
the signatories like Avaiyaṉ, Pokkaṉ-ponnaṉ. Srirāmadēvaṉ, Alaṅgāraṉ Iḷaiya
Perumāḷ-Eḍuttakai-Aḻagiyār etc.

edition.l:
śrī maṟkittikku mel śrī komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉac cakravattika[i]kaḷ
emmaṇṭalamum koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulacekara tevaṟku yāṇṭu 26- vatu
paṅkūṉi mātam 24- ntiṉ tiruttiyūr muṭṭattu tiruccaṉṉavaṉattu
uṭaiyār tiruccaṉṉava[ṉam*] uṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil ādicaṇḍēśvara
nāyaṉārkku maṟittulakaṅkātta pāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu illañceri[kku]ṭi[ku]
kuṟumpaṟṟaṅkuṭi ūrukkuc camainta [vāri]cakravattiyum comaṉ koṟṟaṉum
pokkaṉ po

kellai nūṟaṉ viṭaṅkaṉ keṇikku kiḻai pokkaṉ poṉṉar

poruḷ

ippaṇam irupatum āvaṇakkaḷatte eṅkaḷūr kaṭamaikku
uṭaiyār virāṭarāyar vacam kuṭuppittu viṟṟuk kuṭutto
ivvellaikkuṭpaṭṭa

nilattukkum keṇikkum ituve vilaiyolaiyum po[ru*]ḷ

cilavolai kāṇavuṅ kāṭṭavum kaṭavatallavākavum oru kālāvatu

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
•••••••••• ALIAS TRIBHU[VA*]NACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAPĀṆḌYA-
10TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
The beginning of the inscription is lost. It records the gift of land,
by the same chief mentioned in No. 21, for the supply of offerings to
Subrahmaṇya-piḷḷaiyār and Kshētrapāla-pillaiyār. Signatories like
Sundarapāṇḍyaṉ, Uttamapāṇḍyaṉ etc., are mentioned at the end of the
record.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA
[KULAŚĒ]KHARADĒVA •••••••••• 9th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The beginning of inscription is lost. It records an order of
[Ka]ṇḍiyūrnāḍāḻvā[ṉ]. Also states that some gift was made by Tirukkāṇḍan
alias Aḻagaṉ-periyadēvaṉ to celebrate a festival.

periyatevaṉ āka ipperi •••••

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN-
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA, WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL
COUNTRIES, 17th YEAR (= 1285 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Seems to refer to the gift of land to the temple
after excempting all the taxes. Besides, it also mentions Tāṉattā[r] the
officer of the temple. Signatory Gurukularāyaṉ is mentioned. It also
mentions in the fragmentary part II, a person named Māḷavamānikka
Tirukkāṉappērudaiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE ON THE EAST WALL OF
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE
SHRINETRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYADĒVA,
‘WHO WAS PLEASED TO RESTORE THE CHŌḺA COUNTRY’
20+1 = 21ST YEAR (= 1237 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land measured by 18 feet sānkōl
to a Sivabrāhmaṇa of the temple with instruction to remit a fixed
quantity of paddy and kāśu for the maintenance of the temple at
Tiruttiyūr-Muṭṭam, by Māḷavamāṇikkam-Tirukāṇappēruḍaiyāṉ alias Mālava-
chakravarti. It was originally given to the donor by a Sivabrāhmaṇa of
Semboṉmāri in Talaiyūr-nāḍu. The taxes like Silvari, Perivari, Vettimuṭṭāvāl,
Kīḻveṭṭi and Paramai etc were exempted before donating the same land.

cuṉtarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF
CENTRAL SHRINE.
MĀ[ṞA]VARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN-
KU[LASĒKHARADĒVA], WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL
COUNTRIES, 18th YEAR (= 1234 A.D.)
inscription.p:
A portion at the right end of this inscription is damaged. Registers
the gift by the king of taxes (kaḍamai) on a village to the temple of
Saṉṉavaṉamuḍaiya Nāyaṇār at Tiruttiyūr muṭṭam alias Sundarapāṇḍya
chatūrvēdimaṅgalam in Talaiyūrnāḍu. It also mentions the taxes imposed
and collected according to the yield from the lands such as 1(1/4) on Puñjai
and 25 kāśu from the Nattam land etc.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
[MĀ]ṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN-
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA-[8] YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of land to the temple by the ūrār
of Ilañjēri near Poyil in Kiḻkuṇḍāṟu, a subdivision of Kēralasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu,
and donated it to the temple for burning lamp. While mentioning the
boundaries, a person named Chaiyarayaṉ-Paṟaiyan of Adaḷaiyūr nāḍu is also
mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH AND EAST
WALL OF SAME MAṆḌAPA.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN-
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA, WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL
COUNTRIES, 38th YEAR (= 1296 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land by the ūrārs of
Viyādaraiyaṉkōṭṭai for making offerings to the image of Tiruvādavūr-
Nāyaṇār set up in the same temple by a certain Sundara Pāṇḍya-Bhaṭṭaṉ.
Signatories like Sūraṉ-chinnan, Kanakk-Kālingataraiyaṉ etc are mentioned in
this fragmentary inscription.

l.ta:
śrīmakkittikku meṟ śrī komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ
emmaṇṭala muṅkoṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulacekara tevaṟkku yāṇṭu

vatu
ai(p)paci śrī tiruttiyū(r) mu(ṭ)ṭattu tiruccaṉṉavaṉattu uṭaiyā(r)
tiruccaṉṉavaṉamuṭaiya nāyiṉār koyilil tevakaṉmi cikāriyañcevār

kaṇkāṇi cevārkaḷukku i(n)nāṭu viyā taraiyaṉ
koṭṭai •••••••••• ḻaiñcca ū(ra)varom piṭipāṭu paṇṇi
kuṭuttaparicāvatu i(n)ta nāyiṉār koyilil muppatu vaṭṭattu
civappirāmaṇaril ceya

yāṉ imametaruvāṉāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya paṭṭaṉ innāyaṉār koyilil

innāyaṉāṟku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭa viñcaṉattukkum iṟaiyili
kārāḻmai āka nirvārttu eṅkaḷur puravil kaḻittu viṭṭamai(kku)

ūravarom ippaṭikku añcāta kaṇṭapparaiyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
muṭitāṅki rāmaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN-
[KU]LASĒKHA[RADĒVA], WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL
COUNTRIES, 6th year (1274 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land as tax-free kārāṇmai by
the nāṭṭavar of Muṭṭa-nāḍu to the Nāyaṉār at Tiruvādavūr for the supply
of food offerings by the Nāṭṭavar of Tiruttiyūmuṭṭanāḍu during the
Tirumārgaḻi festival. Also mentions that some amount of rice to be
provided to the Brāhmiṉ Sundarapāṇḍyabhaṭṭan, who recited mantra to
awaken the lord (Nāyaṉār), besides supplying rice to the people lived in
the area of thirty circles (Muppaduvaṭṭam).

l.ta:
vattikaḷ
emmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrī[ku]laceka[ra] •••••••••• āḷun tiyatiyum

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A PILLAR IN THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA ••••• 11TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
It states that a gift of land was made by a lady named
Poyyāmoḻiyāḻuḍaiyāḷ for the annual improvement of a land which she had
brought under cultivation and given to the temple of Tiruchchannavaṉa-
muḍaiya-Nāyaṇār at Tiruttiyūr-Muṭṭam in Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam.

edition.l: ppaṭi [bhumi]yāvatu kuṭivayalil āḻuṭaiya (īncomplete)

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME PILLAR
TRI[BHUVANA] CHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA
YEAR-LOST.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to register the gift of land by Mu[m*]muḍichōḻaṉ
Māḷavamānikkam alias Māḷavachakravartin to a brahmana residing at
Sund[a]rapāṇḍya Chatūrvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1916.)
SAṈṈAVAṈAM, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SĀLYAVANĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
MĀṞA[VARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN]-
KULAŚĒKHA[RADĒVA], [WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL]
COUNTRIE, 18th YEAR (1386 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the worship of an image in the sub-shrine
of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭa-Nāchchiyār, which was installed in the temple of
Tiruchchaṉṉavaṉa-muḍaiya-[Nāyaṉār], by the king's aunt (Attaiyāḻvār).
Mentions Kaṇḍiyūrnāḍāḻvāṉ and Vikrama-Pāṇḍya-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ.

l.ta:
l śrīkomāṟa •••••••••• ttikaḷ ••••• maṇṭalamu •••
••••• ya kulace

n(āṭā)ḻvāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON A PILLAR IN THE ROCK CUT
CAVE, SUNDRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA-10+2=12TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records an order of Vikkīramasiṅgadēvaṉ assigning
some taxes for providing offerings and lamps to a maṭha called Mudaliyār-
tirumaḍam.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON A SECOND PILLAR IN THE SAME
CAVE,
TRIBHU[VA*]NACHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA-9+1=10TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land for making offerings to the
temple of Śivadha[r]mīśvaramuḍaiya mahādēva at Tirukkōḷakkuḍi in
Puramaḷaināḍu, by Kaṇḍaṉ Sundaravalli alias Tuvarāpativēlaṉ of
Kaṇṇamaṅgalam in Chōḻapāṇḍyavaḷanāḍu.

l.ta: ka nirvārttu kalveṭṭi tirucūlakal nāṭṭi kuṭutten kaṇṭan

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE THIRD PILLAR IN THE SAME
CAVE, SUNDRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA-20+2 = 22ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers an order of Vikramaśingadēvaṇ, to collect
the taxes due from certain shepherds and made to the temple of
Śivadharmīśvara-muḍaiya-Nāyaṉār of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi for buring lamps and
offerings. The above gift was made for the health of the king [Uḻaguḍaiya-
Nāyaṉār]. Signatories like Vikramasiṅgadēvaṉ, Teṉṉavataṟaiyaṉ and Nagula-
Nāḍāḻvaṉ are mentioned.

l.ta: rum koḷaṉ koḷaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum • • ḷan āticaṉ
munpu piḷḷaiyār kuṭutta ko • ru piṉṉar

k kallilum cempilum veṭṭik koḷka i• vik

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON A SAME PILLAR.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA, WHO WAS
PLEASED TO TAKE ALL COUNTRIES-7TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of land situated in
Chōḻapāṇḍyavaḷanāḍu, as Tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple at Tirukōlakkuḍi
in Puramalaināḍu by a native of Kāraiyūr in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta: nāṉ muṉ nāḷ ceṉ • • ṉāṭṭu iṟai
ṭamum kuḷamum kuḷaparippum maramum kiṇaṟum maṟṟum

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE FOURTH PILLAR IN THE
SAME CAVE, TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIṈ KULŌTTUṄ[GA
CHŌ]ḺADĒVA-14TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to register the gift of 10 mā of land to the deity
Tirupaḷḷiyaṟai-Nāchchiyār, probably for its maintenance in same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME
CAVE, TRI[BHU]VAṈACHAKRAVARTIṈ KU[LŌTTU]ṄGA
CHŌḺADĒVA-14TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. It mentions Po[nnama]rāpati situated in
Pu[ṟa]malaināḍu, a sub-division of Rāj[ē]ndrachōḻavaḷanāḍu, which was a
district of Rājarājapāṇḍināḍu.

edition.p: The next lines are completely damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME
CAVE.
inscription.p:
Unfinished and damaged. Describes that the god was worshipped in
the three yugas Kṛita, Trēta and Dvābāra by Kṛitānta (i.e., Yama),
Kumbhabhava (i.e., Agastya) etc.

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME
CAVE,
TRI[BHU]VANACHAKRAVARTIN KU[LŌ]TTUṄGACHŌḺADĒVA-14TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the name of the temple Śivadharmīśvara-
muḍaiya-Mahadē[va] at Tirukkōḷa[kku]ḍi in [Pu]rama[laināḍu], a subdivision
of Rājēndrachōḻa vaḷanāḍu, which was a district of Rājarājapāṇḍi nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE ROCK-
CUT CELL OF MALAIMŪRTHI-VINĀYAKA, BELOW THE SAME
CAVE,
TRI[BHU]VAṈACHAKRAVARTIṈ RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA-7TH YEAR
(1170 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land as dēvadānam to the
temple of Śivadharmīśvara[mu*]ḍaiya Mahādēvar by Kōlaṉ
Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟa muḍaiyāṉ alias Nishadharājaṉ of Poṉṉamarāvati in
Puramalaināḍu.

l.ta: yāṇṭu eḻāvatu rājarājapāṇṭi nāṭṭu rājentira coḻa
nikki niṉṟa nilam aṭaṅka cūḻnta perunānkellaiuḷ a[ka]
[p]paṭṭa viḷainilam oḻiviṉṟiye nirvāttu kallu veṭṭi ku

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN
FRONT OF THE CAVE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1[4]4[7] (1525 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land as kāṇiyāṭchi to Taṭṭāṉ
Kaṉṉimalai Āchāri, the gold smith-cum-blacksmith of Āttūr for maintaining
services rendered in the temple at Tirukkōlakkuḍi.

l.ta: llā niṉṟa pāttipa varuṣam māci mātam
ṭa nāyiṉār koyil śrī paṇṭāra kā
paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu ivūr kollan āttūrile •••ṭukkac
ceṉṟa iṭattilē tirukkai vellan makaṉ ulakan ūcci ••••••••••
•••••••••• koyil tāṉattārum pūccaiya nāyakkar āḷ te
••••• ṉum ākac ceṉṟu ūrārai aṉaittu na ••••• kka
••••• ṭuttu tāṉattārai tāṉ caṅkai teṭam aṭittu

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 151[9] (1596-97 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that since the sacred car of the temple got
damaged, a new one was made and replaced in its place by the
(merchants) Nagarattar of Iḷaiyāṭṭakkuḍi. It also further mentions that the
income of the village of Perunallūr about 50 poṉ and 500 kalam of paddy
was set apart to meet the annual maintenence cost of the car festival. (See
No. 49 below for related information).

l.ta: cellā niṉṟa tuṉmuki varuṣam āṉi mātam
yāṟku muṉṉāḷ tāṅkaḷ pūṟuvam vaitta tirutter •••••

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a grant made by the merchants (nagarattār)
residing in the street called Sundarapāṇḍyaṉ perunderu at Tirukkōlakkuḍi
in Puṟamalaināḍu a hamlet Sundaraśolapuram alias Dēsiyugandapaṭṭinam in
Tenkōnāḍu, a sub-division of Ollaiyūrkūṟṟam in Kaḍaladaiyādilaṅgaikoṇḍa
chōḻavaḷanāḍu, for which they had the privilege of receiving the tīrtha and
sacred ashes in the temple.

l.ta: m puṟam piṭam nācciyār āka yivarkaḷ maṟṟum kaṭala
tiruccāntu cātti muṭintu varikku paṇam poṉṉilu

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA: RIGHT OF ENTRANCE NARASA NAYAKA-ŚAKA
142(4) (1502 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of dēvadāṉa village of
Kiḻmaṇanallūr together with Embalkuṟichchi in Tenkōnāḍu, for making
offering to the temple of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi-āṇḍa-nāyaṇār in Ponnamarāpati-
nāḍu for the Tiruvōṇam festival. Tammaya-nāyaka, the agent of
Tipparasa-uḍaiyar made the gift for the latter's merit. The inscription
mentions that Narasanāyaka was governing the country on behalf of
Narasinga Bhujabalarāja Danmarāya.

l.ta: pāṣakku tappuva irāyirakaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭa nāṭu koṇṭu koṇ
ṭanāṭu koṭātavaṉ metiṉi mīcurakaṇṭaṉ kaṭṭāri cā
kamum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ ciṟimatu tipparaca
āṇṭa nayiṉārkku uṣarpūcai amutu ceyyum kaṭṭaḷai
kku ayyaṉ[ta]tippara uṭaiyarkku taṉmamāka iṟṟai nāḷ
kuṟicci nañcai puñcai puṟakkaṭi uḷpaṭa camuttappiṟāttiyaḷum
kuḷpaṭṭa ental kuṟicci nañcai puñcai puṟakkaṭi

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL,
16TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
Built in at the right side. This inscription registers a grant of land
evidently by Sāluva-Nāyaka and Appappiḷḷai to two private individuals
Sakkadēvar, Vēṭṭuvakkāṭṭan and his brother Sirukāttavan of Kūḍalūr for
performing the service of Pāḍikāval at Tirupputtur It also mentions the
rehabitation of the old kuḍi who deserted the place.

l.ta:
puṟamalaināṭṭut tirukkoḷakkuṭi āṇṭanayinār
koyiṟ ciṟi paṇṭārak kāriyattukku kaṭavarom

cāḷuva nāyakkarum appappiḷḷaiyum tirupputtūrkku

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1459 (1536-37 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Built in at the right side. Records the re-settlement of Tandirimār at
this place permanently. Some incentive was made in the form of tax
concession for all the years by the temple trustees. Signatories Narasingha-
brahmādarāyar, Ālāasundara bhaṭṭar, Tiyam-Uyyanerikāṭṭinār etc., are
mentioned in the inscription. This seems to be related to the above
inscription.

mutalāka eṟku mūṉṟu paṇamum kaiylai irukku oṉ • •

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1423 (1501 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a sale of land named as Vāsagapparayan of
Kuḍikāḍu as Tirunāmattukkāni to the temple of Tirukkōlakkuḍi-Āṇḍa-
Nāyaṉār by the Maravas of Vēlānguḍi in Pūṅgunra-nāḍu in order to pay
the taxes due by them on their holdings.

edition.l: svastiśrī cakāttam- 1423- ṉ mel cellā

kaḷ ūrār kaiyyil nāṅkaḷ muṉṉāḷ koṇṭuṭaiya ucita

ṉ uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu ippaṭikku ippiṟamāṉam eḻu-

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1[4]73 (1581 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of temple car (tiruttēr) to the
temple at Tirukkōlakkuḍi by certain Nagarattār of Iḷaiyāttakkuḍi alias
Kulaśēkharapuram in Kalvāśal-nāḍu, in return for they were honoured
with Parivaṭṭam, Tirumālai in the same temple.

edition.l:
kācil 10- § puṟamalaināṭṭu tirukkoḻakkuṭi āṇṭa nayiṉār koyil
śrī paṇ

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1429 (1506-07 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that an agreement was made between the
temple authorities of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi and certain residents of Iḷaiyattakkuḍi
alias Kulaśēkharapuram in Kalvāsal-nāḍu regarding the celebration of
festivals and other services for the deity Śambandapperumāḷ, which they
had set up in the temple.

edition.l: cakāttam 1429- l mel cellā niṉ

kkuṭaiyār aṭiyāṟku nallā[r*] uḷḷiṭṭārum āvuṭaiyār
uḷḷiṭṭāru[m*]
āka ivvaṉaivarum intat tirukkoyilukku campantap perumāḷ
tirunāḷukkum aṟṟaik kuṟaiyaṟuppāka tirume pūccum tiruparivaṭṭa
mum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭavai uṇṭāṉa • ṟaikaḷum naṭatti
kaṭṭi cantirātittavaṟ cella tanmaka naṭantu ittirunāḷukku
cantirātitavaṟ cella kallilum cempilum veṭṭik ko
ṭṭāṟku ippaṭikku naraciṅka piramātarāyar eḻuttu kaiyavira

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 53 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1377 (1455-56 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription also informs a similar agreement regarding the
celebration of festivals of the deity of Chandrasēkhara which was setup in
the same temple by Tiruveppruaradaiyāṉ, Inbavaṇapperumāḷ,
Tirumuṇḍalagiyār etc. the residents of Iḷaiyāttakkuḍi alias Kulaśēkharapuram
in Kalvāsalnāḍu.

svasti[śrī] cakāttam 1377- l cellā niṉṟa īśvara varuṣam aṟpaci mātam
6 §

ik

maramum vay • •

inscription.head:
No. 54.
(A.R. No. 54 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription records the construction of a maṇḍapa by a certain
Maḻavarāyaṉ, a native of Tachchaṉūr in Kīḻvēmba-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 55.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SRIVALLA[BHA*]DĒVA-26TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the gift of paṭṭaṇappakuti on the merchandise
edible items like uppu (salt), Mañjal (Turmeric), Venkāyam (onion) by the
trading communities Nagarattār and Padinen Visaiyattār to the temple. In
return for this service, the temple honoured them with Tirunīr to the
nagarattar of Sundara-Chōlapuram, alias Dēsiyugantam of Ulavāuyir
Kūṟṟam in Tēr-nāḍu. Ālasundira bhaṭṭar Sundarapaṇḍya Brahmādirāyar etc.,
are mentioned as signatories.

edition.l: varkku yāṇṭu 26- vatu kumpa nāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu tiṅkkiḻa

inscription.head:
No. 56.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1437 (1514-15 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the renovation of the procession street of
the temple of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi-āṇḍa-nāyaṉār by the Nagarattār of
Sundarapāṇḍyanperunderu, for celebrating a festival.

edition.l:
supamastu cakāttam 1437- itaṉ mel cellā niṉṟa pāvaka varuṣam
vaikāci mātam 22 puṟamalai

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that the merchants (nagarattār) of
Sundarachōḻapuram in Tiruttēr-nāḍu supplied tiruvaśigai, for four images in
the same temple.

edition.l:
§ svastiśrī maṉmata varuṣam puraṭṭāci mātam 18- § tirukkoḷakkuṭi pāṇṭa
tampirāṉār

āka inta nālu tiruvātikaiyum ivarkaḷ taṉ[ma*]māka niṉṟa ve

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-7TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the gift of tax free Nañjey, Puñjai lands
located at Kumāttakkuḍi in Poṉṉamaravati-nāḍu for offerings, on the
occasion of Pāvanaṅgāraṉ-sandi instituted by the officer Parākramapāṇḍya
Māvilivānāḍi-rāyaṉ in the temple of Śivadharmīśvramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at
Tirukkōlakkuḍi in Puramalai-nāḍu. One of the signatories is mentioned as
Venṟumuḍisūḍiṉaṉ-Enakkunalla-Perumāḷ alias Sundarapāṇḍya Pallavarāyaṉ of
Sēvūr in Arumbūr-kūṟṟam. The king remitted taxes like Tariyiṟai, Sekkīṟai,
Taṭṭoli-Taṭṭarapāṭṭam, Ērivari, iṉavari, Kulamāṇpāṭṭam, Poṉvari, Antarāryam
Kārttigai Pachchai etc.

l.ta:
ram muṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattāṟku
taṅkaḷ nāyaṉāṟku makkaṇāya

mapāṇṭiya māvili vāṇātarāyaṉ kaṭṭiṉa pāvaṉaṅka kāṟaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription states that a certain Kaḷakarāya of
Sundarachōḻapuram alias Dēsiyugandapaṭṭiṇam in Tenkōnāḍu, a sub-division
of Olliyūr-kuṟṟam, set up, an image Pallakku-sokkar in the temple of
Tirukkōlakkuḍi-Āṇḍa-Nayiṉār and arranged for a festival for which he
received in return certain temple honours. Lines 17-29 seem to belong to a
different inscription referring to the order of the nāṭṭavar of
Ponnamarāpati-nāḍu sent to the same village.

l.ta: kuṭi āṇṭa nayiṉār koyil śrī paṇṭāra kāriyattukka
ṭavarroti ollaiyūr kūṟṟa[t*]tu teṉkoṉāṭṭu cuntara coḻa
kārāyarkku piṭipāṭu paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi innayiṉār tiruk
koyilukku ivar taṉ[ma*]māka pallakku cokkar eḷuntaruḷa pa

ṟukka kaṭavarkaḷ eṉṟum oṉṟupaṭa naṭatti potakkaṭavarkaḷ
m vantappaṭiyāle nāṅkaḷum nāṭṭuṭaṉe kūṭṭi eṅkaḷ
mariyāti kaṭamai iṟuppatāka canti[rāti*]tta varai celvatā
ka kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom poṉṉamarāpati nāṭṭu nāṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
MADURAI SULTAN (RĀJĀKKAL TAMBIRĀṈ)-[Hijra] 761 (1359 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This is incomplete. Refers to the destruction of the village
Sūraikkuḍi by some military captain (of the king ?). The temple trustees
seem to have given some tax concession to the Ūravar of Kōṭṭiyūr and
Viraiyachchilai respectively in Poṉṉamarāvati nāḍu.

l.ta: pirāṉār cūraikkuṭi aḻiyac ceyvatāka mañcilicu elicu
cāṉe ācam tāttāṉe muvācam tāṉucāṉe irācāttikā
paṭiyāle ivvaṭikaḷ kaṇ aruvukku irācarācaṉ tampirā
varakkāṭṭi aruḷiṉapaṭi muṉṉāḷ nammuṭaiyapaṇ namakku iṟuttu
ponta viraiyāccilai uḷḷiṭṭa ūrum koṭṭiyūr uḷḷiṭṭa
ūrum taṅkaḷ nāṭṭuṭaṉe kūṭṭi taṅkaḷ maryāti kaṭamai i
ṟukka kaṭavārkaḷ eṉṟum oṉṟupaṭa naṭatti potakaṭavārkaḷ
m vantapaṭiyāle nāṅkaḷum nāṭṭuṭaṉe kūṭṭi eṅkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, LEFT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1432 (1510 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the assigning of the duty of Kāval rights
of certain villages like Vēlaṅguḍi and Suṇḍaikāttūr in Pūṅkuṉṟanāḍu, to
three groups of watchmen (pādikāppāṟ), including Sivandeḻundārāṉa
Sundarapāṇḍiyaṉ of Kaḻaṉivāsalin kulaśēkharapuram alias Ilaiyāttaṅguḍi in
Kalvāsalnādu, Sivanda-kālaḻagiyārāṉa-chēdirāyar and Pāṇḍyaperumāḷ-piḷḷaiyār
of Eḻagapperunderu alias Vīrapāṇḍiyapuram.

l.ta:

yāṉa kulacekarapurattu kaḻaṉivācaluṭaiyāṇ civanteḻuntārāṉa

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE).
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDRA-
PĀṆḌYADĒVA-YEAR, 7TH, 13TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 100 kuḻi of land as
Tirunāmattukkāṇi for celebrating a festival, before the image of
Nāchchiyār of Udaiyāṇḍār set up in the shrine of Piḍāriyār Aḻagiya-
Nāchchiyār by Arumbākkiḻāṉ Tiruvan Sambandan alias Pāṇḍiyataraiyan
who purchased it from the temple and regranted the same. Mentions a
coin called Pudukkuḷigai. Signatories like Ellāndalaiyāṉa-pāṇḍya-Brahmārāyaṉ
and Vīrapāṇḍya-Brahmarāyar.

peṟṟa uttirāṭattu nāḷ puṟamalai nāṭṭu tirukkoḷakkuṭi
uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya[ṉ] nāyaṉār tirukkoyil śrī

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE).
JAḌĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA-10+1 = 11TH YEAR, 13TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sale of the Kārāṇkiḻmaikkāṇi right of
some tirunāmattukāṇi land in the village of Muḷḷikkuḍi alias Muḍivalaṅgu-
Perumālnallūr which was a dēvadāna village in Kāṉā-nāḍu, to Vīrapāṇḍya-
Kālingarāya, a native of Kalyānasundaranallūr, to cultivate the grains like
paddy, millet, pepper, gingili, sugarcane, betel leaves, turmeric, ginger etc,
and supply to the temple at Tiruppuvaṇam in Andanāḍu. The two
signatories mentioned in No. 62 also appears in the present inscription.

uṭaiyanāyaṉār tirukkoyili śrī ru

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE).
JAḌĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
VĪRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA-31ST YEAR, 14TH CENTURY (1365 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of a Kāṇiyātchi right of a temple
land to a native of Kuḷamaṅgalam in Mēlai-iraṇiyamuṭṭanāḍu, by the
temple trustees of Aḻagar. Refers to the restoration of peace and orderly
rule by the efforts of Kampaṇa-uḍaiyar after destroying the rule of
Tulukkaṉ (Muslims).

aḻakar tiruviṭaiyāṭṭa

uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi yuṭaiya

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1464 (1541-42.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of tax-free (iṟaiyili) land as
Tirunāmattukkāṇi to the same temple, by Vaṅgāramuḍaiyāṉ Pallavarāyar
for the merit of a certain Periya-rāmappa-Nāyaka.

l.ta: mel cellā niṉṟa pilava varuṣam puraṭṭāti mātam

pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu velaṅkuṭi puravil kuṭikāṭṭu vayal cuṉeṇṭai vayal
vāracci

kuṭikkāṭṭu puravukku uḷpaṭṭa nañcai puñcai nattamum cettalaittiṭarum
māvaṭai maravaṭai kuḷa

•••••••••• maṟṟum innāyaṉār tirukkoḷakkuṭi āṇṭanayiṉār āticaṇṭecura
tevarkku tevatā •••••

••••• periyya irāmappa nāyakkaṟkku taṉmamāka innayiṉārkku iṟaiyili
tirunāmattuk kāṇi āka ikkuṭik

•••••••••• kāṟiyattukku kaṟtaṉ vaṅkāramuṭaiyār pallavarāyar irāmappanāyakkar

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE).
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDRA-
PĀṆḌYADĒVA-1(9)th YEAR, 13TH CENTURY A.D. (1287 A.D.?).
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the lease of land by the temple authorities
to Uyyavandāṉ Pāṇḍyaṉ alias Sundarapāṇḍya-Narasingadēva a Kaikkōla of
Si[ru]-Veṇkuṉṟam near Māḍakkuḷakkīḻ in Madurai, and fixes the rate of
Mēlvāram to be paid by the donee to the temple. One of the signatories is
Ellāndaliyāṉa-Pāṇḍya-Piramādirāyaṉ. Mēlvāram tax was imposed and
collected viz 1/3 from irrigated land (Pāsāṉam), 1/5 from the items like
gingly, bananas, sugarcane, lemon coconuts etc and kamugu on new
excavated land for the first time 1/9 then 1/8 then 1/7 then latter on 1/3
permanently.

l.ta:
vaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ
śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

pakṣattu pañcamiyum putaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa attattu nāḷ miḻalai
nāṭṭu tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyaṉ

rar tevarkaṉmikaḷ
koyil kaṇakkar ivvaṉaivomum māṭakkuḷakkiḻ maturai cūḻnta ci[ṟu]

veṇkuṉṟattuk kaikkoḷaril uyyavantāṉ pāṇṭiyanāṉa
cuntarapāṇṭiya naraciṅka tevaṟku kāṇippiṭipāṭu paṇṇik kuṭutta pari

cāvatu yiṉṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇip paṟṟil uḷḷūṟa
kuṭikkāṭṭuppuravil ōma

vayal pantaṉ vayal taṭṭāṉ vayal āka ivvayal
muṉṟum

muṉṟiloṉṟum tiṉai varaku eḷḷup payaṟu kuṟuvai kaṟumpu koḻuntu
karuṇai

mañcaḷ iñci ceṅkaḻunīr vāḻai vaḻutalai pūcaṇi uṭpaṭap payir
ceyalukkum māppilā nāttai elumiccai kuḷaviruḷi nelli iluppai
uṭpaṭa

marattukku añciloṉṟum teṅku kamukukku ēḻiloṉṟum puñcaippayaṟu
talaipāttu ēḻiloṉṟum kāṭuveṭṭit tirutti innilattukku mutalā

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL (INSIDE)
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA-10th YEAR, 13TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the lease of templelands given to
Uyyavandāṉ alias Parākramapāṇḍya-Naraśiṅgadēvaṉ by the temple trustees
Mahēśvarar for the maintenance of temple. Ellāndalaiyāṉa-Pāṇḍya
Piramādirāyaṉ, Vīrapāṇḍya-Piramādirāya-Chokka-bhaṭṭaṉ, Kulaśēkhara
bhaṭṭaṉ, Ālāla Sundara bhaṭṭaṉ, Uyyaneri-kāṭṭinaṉ etc., are mentioned as
signatories.

l.ta:
kku mel śrī koccaṭaipaṉmarāṉa tirupu[va*]ṉaccakkaravattikaḷ
śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

kkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār tirukkoyil
śrī ruttira

mapāṇṭiya naraciṅkatevaṟkup
piṭipāṭupaṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu ivvūr

kāṇiyāṭci āṇṭārkaḷ aṭaivu nāliloṉṟum paṟṟi aṉupavittu
kāṇiyāṭci āṇṭārkaḷ aṭaivu nāliloṉṟum paṟṟi aṉupavittu
kāṇiyāṇṭu pōvariṉṟi muṉṟu a

ṭaivākam paṟṟi kāṇi yāṇṭu potukaiyil yiṉṉāliloṉṟum
iṉṉāṉ mutal kāṇiyākap paṟṟi aṉu

pavippārākavum ippaṭi kāṇiyāṇṭu potumaḷavil iṉṉālat
toṉṟukkum kāṇiyāṉa oma

pāttaṉ vayalum iṉṉāliloṉṉāl varum maṉaikaḷum iṉṉāḷ mutal
paṟṟi aṉupavittu koyil cuvanti

rattil nāliloṉṟukku aṭaippāṉa nimantaṅkaḷukkum āḷum iṭṭu
iṉṉimain taṅkaḷukku aṭaippā

ṉa nel nimantam nilaaṭaippu coṟṟaṭaippum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa
cuvantiraṅkaḷum paṟṟi aṉu

pavittu tiruvākka viḷariyum nilakkāriya ••••• kkum kāriyaṅkaḷukkum
nāliloṉṟukku eḻuttu

mmiṭṭu ittiruvācal kāriyamāka varum nalantiṅkaḷum eṅkaḷ marucāti
okka niṉṟu aṉupavittu iṉṉā

ḷ mutal kāṇiyāṇṭu potuvārākavum ippaṭi cammatittu piṭipāṭu
paṇṇik kuṭuttom parā
ṇṭiya naraciṅkatevaṟku śrī ruttira śrī mahe

ntalaiyāṉa pāṇṭiya piramātarāyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku virapāṇṭiya
piramātarāyaṉ eḻuttu

ippaṭikku cokkapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu teva piḷḷai eḻuttu ippaṭikku
kulaicekarapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku

periyapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu mā

pattiyam uyya neṟikāṭṭināṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku tiruvempaḻakiyāṉ
eḻuttu aṉukka

ntoṇṭaṉ eḻuttu śrī civapattaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
tiruvuṇṇāḻippiḷḷai eḻuttu ippaṭikku teyya
ippaṭikku śrī māherapavarak kaṇakku tirukkai veḷi

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CHITRASABHĀPATI SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE LEFT OF
ENTRANCE TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of tax-free village Kaṇḍiyadēvaṉ
Sundarapāṇḍya chatur[vēdimaṅgalam] made to 20 Brāhmaṇas, well versed in
the vēdā sāśtrās for building Śiva and Vishṇu temples, (latter named)
Kulaśēkhara-viṇṇagarĀḻvār. The grant was made at the instance of
Kālingarāyaṉ. Taxes like Veṭṭipāṭṭam, Pañchupili, Taṭṭolipāṭṭam, Iḍaiyarvari
were remitted.

l.ta: ••••• tup paṅku maṉaiyum eḻutik kuṭiyirukkalām na •••••

••••• yamum viṉiyokamum taruvatāṉavum veṭṭipāṭṭamum pañcupili
cattivi •••
••••• ṉap paṅku nālum āka paṅku irupattu
tevatāṉam paḷḷic cantam nikki nikki uṉ

edition.p: Built in at the beginning and at bottom.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL, RIGHT OF
ENTRANCE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1450 (1528 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the construction of maṇḍapa besides setting
up of an image in it. Mentions Sundarachōḻapuram, alias
Dēsiyugaṇḍapaṭṭanam, which was a village in Teṉ-kōnāḍu, a sub-division of
Olliyūrkūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 70 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON A STONE BUILT INTO THE SAME
WALL.
inscription.p: This inscription in verse for praises a person Aḻagiyachēmaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 71.
(A.R. No. 71 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME
SHRINE.
•••••••••• 17TH YEAR AND 185TH DAY.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of village Mañjaṉēri in Pūṅguṉṟa-
nāḍu after purchase from somebody and given as offerings to the deity of
Kūttāḍuvār, which was set up in the temple of Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiya
nāyaṉār at Tirukkōlakkuḍi by Sēmāṇḍār, the younger brother of
Akaḷaṅganāḍāḻvār and further it states that at the instance of Kālingarāyaṉ,
the above cited granted village was made taxfree by the king, when he
was encamping at Gaṅgaikoṇḍapaṭṭiṉam.

l.ta: lai koṇṭa pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu mañcaṉeri vilai
patiṉoṉṟāvatu mutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka i[ṭa]ppeṟa ve[ṇu]
akaḷaṅka nāṭāḻvār tampi cemāṇṭār eḻuntaruḷivitta kū
kku cemāṇṭār nakularāṉ pakkal ikkoyil caṇṭe
tevar tirunāmattāl vilaikoṇṭa pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu mañcaṟai
y puṉceyum nattamum toṭṭamum kuḷamum kuḷapparippum uṭpaṭ(ṭa)
kkaṭamaiyum antarāyamum viṉiyokamum ta •••••••••• accum kā
ṭu mutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyākavum ippaṭi cantirātitta(r) vaṟ

inscription.head:
No. 72.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVANṈCHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ.
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription seems to register the order of the king.
The tax iḍaiyarvari is mentioned. Signatories like Paḻandīparāyaṉ, Araiyar
Tirunelvēliyuḍaiyaṉ alias Toṇḍaimāṉ, Arasu-Pallavaraiyaṉ etc., are mentioned
in the inscription.

l.ta: nirnilamum karuñcey puṉceyum nattamum toṭṭamum kuḷamum kuḷa-
pparippum uṭpaṭa nilamu muṉṉuṭaiyārum uḻavuntavuttuk kārā-
ṇmai miyāṭciyum kaṭamaiyum antarāyamum viṉiyokamum taru-
riyum ciṟṟāyamum poṉvariyum maṟṟum epperpaṭṭaṉavum uṭpaṭa pa-
r tampi cemāṇṭar eḻuntaruḷivitta kūttāṭuvāṟkut tiruppaṭi
māṟṟuḷḷuṭṭu veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukkup pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu nāṭṭā
r pakkal nakularācaṉ vilai koṇṭuṭaiya vilaiyolaippaṭi nakularā
ṭa nirnilamum karuñcey puṉceyum nattamum toṭṭamum kuḷamum
tuk kārāṇmai miyāṭciyum kaṭamaiyum antarāyamum viṉiyo

inscription.head:
No. 73.
(A.R. No. 73 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of lands situated at Maṇalūr alias
Sundara-Pāṇḍya-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam a hamlet of Poṉṉamarāvati in
Puramalai nāḍu as dēvadāṉa to the deity uḍaiyār-Tirukalkoṭukuṉṟamuḍaiyar
for the merit of Kaṇiyadēvaṉ.

l.ta: kak karaiyiṭṭuk koḷḷavum ivaiyiṟṟāl uḷḷa kaṭamai-
peṟum yilāñciṉaip peṟum taṟiyiṟai cekkiṟai taṭṭa
p patiṉālāvatu mutal taviṟtu taṉmatāṉa iṟaiyiliyā
ka •••••••••• m ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭākak ko
ṟṟattu aṉantaṉūr ci ••••• mpāṉūr uṭaiyāṉ vaṇṭuvā

inscription.head:
No. 74.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME
SHRINE TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-
3rd YEAR 14TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription states the village Māṅguḍi, was sold publicly by the
ūrār of Śevūr, to the temple of Tirukkōlakkuḍi-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār. It was set
apart to meet the expenses of the shrine of Aḻagapperumāḷ built by one
of the tapasvins of the maṭha, in the same temple.

l.ta:
ṅkaḷ kāṇipaṟṟu māṅkuṭiyai viṟpāṉicaintu
koḷviruḷaro enṟu orukālāvatu mirukālāvatu mukkālā

vatu muṟkūṟiya moḻikeṭṭup piṟkūṟikkoḷvenṟa tirukkoḷakkuṭi
maṭattilirukkumn tapa

tevarkaḷ tevan ikkoyilil aṇiyāntillaiyampalattu
eḻuntaruḷapaṇṇiṉa aḻaka(r naṭṭa)p perumāḷukki

milicainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikkuṟaviṟṟup poruḷaṟak
kaikkoṇṭu viṟṟu vilai

tevaṟku cevūrāyicainta ūrum mutalikaḷu minnilattil
(me)ṉokkiṉa maramuṅ kiṇ(o)kkiṉa kiṇaṟum nattamun to

ṭṭamuṅ kuḷamuṅ kuḷapparappu naṉcey puṉcey nāṉkellaikkuḷppaṭṭa
nirnilamu maṟṟu yepperpaṭṭaṉavum emmi

licainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikkuṟaviṟṟu emmilicainta
vilaipporuḷellām vilaiyāvaṇakkaḷatte kāṭṭi

mutalikaḷum immāṅkuṭi vayalukku vanta yiṟaikuṭimai āḷvari
mutalippeṟu cilvari peruvari veṭṭimuṭṭāvāḷ velikkā

laccup poṉvari eccoṟuceva maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavun nāṅkaḷe
iṟuppomākavum ippaṭi cammatittu ikkuṭikkā

ṭṭukku ituve vilaiyāvatākavu mituve poruḷmāvaṟutip poruṭc
celavolai yāvutākavu mituvallatu veṟu poruḷ māvaṟu

tipporuṭ celavolai kāṇa ••••• ṭṭavum kamavutallātākavum
emmilicainta vilaipporuḷ vilaiyāvaṇakak kaḷattekā

ṭṭeṟṟikkai celakkaik koṇṭu immāṅkuṭip perunāṉkellaiyum
naṭuvun tiruccūlatevarai

liluñ cempilum veṭṭikkuṭuttom cevūr ūrum mutalikaḷum
pāṭikāval māttālp paṉṉiraṇṭarayum māttāl tū

ṇi nellum kuḷattil miṉum pāṭikāval peṟuvom keṭṭa
tiṟuppomākavum ippaṭi cammatittu ikkuṭikkāṭu viṟṟu vilai

māṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom aṇiyāratil vilaiyaḻakar
naṭṭapperumāḷ āticaṇṭe

inscription.head:
No. 75.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME
SHRINE.
inscription.p:
The beginning of the inscription is lost. This inscription speaks about
the sale of land to the temple of Tirukkōlakkuḍi by the ūrār of Vēlaṅguḍi.
The land here sold is the same as that referred to in No. 50 of 1916,
above.

l.ta: kku viṟpataṟku icaiñcu ūrañcāti iṟaiyili tirunā

ku velaṅ(k)kuṭi ūravarom ippaṭi viṟṟa ikkuṭikkāṭṭukku
peruṉāṉ

kkum melpāṟkellai •••••••••• pparuppukku ki(ḻak)kum vaṭapāṟkellai
muṉ
maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭanavum uḷppaṭa iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu

vaṟku velaṅkuṭi ūrāka icainta ūr(o)
m ippaṭi
•••••••••• oru colvācakap peraiyaṉ kuṭikkāṭṭil vayalukku nirreṟṟi
ṭum itile pāyavum ippaṭi cammatittu cattirātitta(vaṟ c)elva •••••

vārkaḷākavum ••••• ñcāti iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇi
vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇi

kkuṭuttom uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār
āticaṇṭe

inscription.head:
No. 76.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVANACHAKRA[VARTIN] VĪRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA.
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. It mentions the place Tirukkōḷakkuḍi in Puṟamalaināḍu.
The names of the signatories like Poṉpaṟṟi uḍaiyāṉ-Ponnappirānvāda
muḍich-sānāṉ Viraṭṭaṉṉaṉ, Toṇḍaiyaṇ, Rājarāja Viḻuppadararayaṉ etc., are
mentioned.

l.ta:
ṟṟattaṉ meṟ[kā]ṟṟu ••••• lūṟkuṭi poṉpaṟṟi uṭaiyāṉ poṇṇappirāṉ
vāta muṭiccāṉā

ṉ viraṭṭanṉana eḻuttu ivai toṇṭaiyaṉ eḻuttu eppeṟpaṭaṉavum
uṭpaṭa tirukkāma

kkoṭṭappuṟam yiṟaiyili yiṟuppatāka kāṉaṉāṭṭukku pātatakkuṭi nāṉkel
laikkuṭpaṭṭa nilat
tiruttāṉatonṟi ivai puravari tiṉanakkaḷ nāyaka miḻalaik kūṟṟattu •••••
nampiyaruḷāṉanāṉa irācarāca viḻuppātarāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai ke

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE ROCK ADJOINING THE SAME
TEMPLE MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA, ‘WHO PLEASED TO TAKE THE CHŌḺA
COUNTRY’-5THYEAR AND 528TH DAY (1221 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of tax-free land for conducting the
service called Muḍivaḻaṅguperumāḷsandi in the temple
Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tirukkōḷakkuḍi, by the king at the
request of his brother-in-law (machchunanār) Aḻagapperumāḷ. The king,
while issuing the order, was occupying the seat called Mālavarāyaṉ in the
Palliyaraikkūḍam of his palace at Poṉṉamarāpadi in Puṟamalaināḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī pūmaruviya tirumaṭantaiyum ••••••••••
(2 to 12 prasasthi)

l.ta:
komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭu koṇṭaruḷiya śrī
cuntarapāṇṭiya tevar yāṇṭu

nāṭṭup poṉṉamarāpatik koyilp paḷḷiyaṟaik kūṭattup paḷḷippiṭa
maḻavārayaṉil eḻuntaruḷiyiruntu iṉṉāṭṭut tirukkoḷakku

yāmattukkum tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu
veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukkun tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyākattu • mānāṭṭu

muḷḷikkuṭiyil nāṭṭi attūṉ kuḷamum naṭuvum ivai
ṉāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nilam aincāvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭu mutal kārāṇmai
miyāṭciyum an

tarāyamum uṭpaṭat tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka iṭappeṟa veṇumeṉṟu
maccuṉaṉār aḻakapperumāḷ namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil ittevarkku
muṭivaḻa •••••

m perumāḷ cantikkum tiruviḷaiyāṭṭattukkum tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu
vēṇṭu nivantaṅkaḷukku ivvūrkaḷ muḷḷikkuṭi nāṭṭiyattāṉ ku

ḷamum naṭuvum ivai nāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa nilam ivvāṇṭu mutal
kārāṇmai miyāṭciyum antarāyamum uṭpaṭat tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka
iṭ

ṭu variyilār eḻuttiṭṭa uḷvariyum nam ōlaiyum taraccoṉṉom
kaikkoṇṭu tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷ cellappaṇ

ṇuka veṉṟum ippaṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka kalliluñ cempilum
veṭṭivittuk koḷkaveṉṟu tiruvāymoḻintaruḷiṉār

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL (OUTSIDE) OF
THE ĀTMANAYAKI-AMMAṈ TEMPLE ON THE SAME HILL.
M[ĀṞA*]VARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA, ‘WHO HAVING TAKEN THE CHŌḺA
COUNTRY, WAS PLEASED TO PERFORM THE ANOINTMENT OF
THE RULES AND VICTORS AT MUḌIGOṆḌA CHŌḺAPURAM’-14TH
YEAR (1230 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records that at the instance of the king's nephew
Kulaśēkharadēva, certain lands situated in Kumāttakkuḍi, a village of Kāna
nāḍu, were made tax-free and given to the temple of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi for
making offerings to the goddess Āḷuḍaiya-Nāchchiyār Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam set
up in the temple, by a resident of Rājavallipuram in Kīḻvēmbanāḍu. It
mentions the royal seat called Paḷḷipīḍam Maḻavarāyaṉ situated in the
Rājarājaṉmaṇḍapa of the king's palace at Muḍigoṇḍachōḻapuram in
Kulōttuṅgachōḻavaḷanāḍu, a district of Chōḻamaṇḍalam. It also mentions the
temples of Tāndōṉṟiśvaramuḍaiyār and Kāṉanāṭṭu-viṇṇagarāḻvār.

l.ta: kirimel varik kayal kaḷviṉaiyāṭa iruṅkaṭal valaiyattiṉi
karuṅkalikaṭintu caṅkoli naṭappa oru kuṭai niḻaliṭu
kait tamiḻumuṉamaiyil viḷaṅka nālvakai veta aṟuvakait
kaṭaṉ tikaḻa eḻuvakaip pāṭalumiyal uṭaṉ parivaram ṉa
rañ cellak koṅkaṇar kariṅkar kocavar māḷavar
ṅkiciṉar kuccar villavar marakatar vikkāka cempiyar
rai kaṭava tantiṟai kuṇarntiraiñca vilaṅkoḷim maṉu
ṇai poykkai kiṉaṉi cūḻa naṭaviṟ kayalāṉai kaiyavaḷar
ṭāmum āṭaraṅkamā mumāḷikaiyum maṇṭapamum pala viṭittu
ṉ nāmattalai piṭuṅki miḷuntaṟukaṉ matacāṉai mel

•••••••••• ril maṇakuṇar yāṅkavavaṉ kaṭṭaṇattiṟakaṟapun tamakkāṇā
poṟcūḻarimaikku murāmāruṅke kaikko

ṇṭu ṭirapaṇippaṭṭaṅkaṭṭi muṭicūṭṭi māravil ṉaitturuvaṉa
mutaṟeviyeṉap per kuṭuttavaḷu cimutarai peyavaḷai

yār • • poṅkupuṟai kumpa mulāppulavar pukaḻa maṅkalaṅkaloṭu maṇakkat
talanteṉata koṭak koṇṭa neṟṟitiṟaṟ te

rapurañ cūḻa muṭikoṇṭa coḻapura maṇṭapattiṟapukkut ticaitoṉuñ
coma poṟceyal kampa nāṭṭi vijaiyā

ṇiyaruḷic ceytu vākaikka tiruvelavaṭa ventātam pātamekatta
puṇiyavarak kaḻalaṇintu vikaiyamaṇipaṇi vira

vaḷakeḻukavar temaṅkacaippak kaṭaleṉ muḻaṅkukaḷi nalulipāṉai vaṭapula
ventar maṇi ••••• ilakkuḻai yarivaiyar toḻuka

niṉṟetta ulakamuḻutuṭaiyār roṭum vīṟṟiruntaruḷiyu śrī komm[āra*]
paṉmarāṉa tira

purattu virarapiṣē[ka*]mu vijayāpiṣekamum paṇṇi aruḷiya śrī
cuntarapāṇṭiya tevar patinālāvatu nāḷmuṉṉūṟṟirupatteṭṭiṉāl

coḻa maṇṭalattuk kulōttuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu muṭikoṇṭa
coḻapurattuk koyil irācarācaṉ maṇṭapattu paḷḷippīṭa maḻavarā
muṭaiyār koyiṟ tāṉapatikaḷ ceyyat tiruvāy(t) tiruvāy

moḻintaruḷiṉapaṭi kīḻvempa nāṭṭu irācavallipurattu uṭaiyāṉ
uvāruṉaintaṉṉāṉa tamiḻa pallavataraiyar yikkoyiṟ tirukkāmaṟ ko

ṭṭattukku eḻuntaruḷivita āḷuṭaiya nācciyāṟkku
tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu nivantaṅkaḷukku kāṉaṉāṭṭuk kumāttakkuṭi
nāṉkel

ramuṭaiyār tevatāṉa
nilaṅkālum kāṉa nāṭṭu viṇṇakarāḻvār nilam

irumāvaraiyum āka nilam eḻumāvaraiyum nīkki nīkki uḷḷa nilattil
ivvāruṭaiya nācciyārkku iṭṭa nilam

iraṇṭu vēliyuṭpaṭa patiṉaiñcāvatu mutal antarāyamum viniyokamum
veṭṭip pāṭṭam pañcupili cantuvikkirakappeṟum maṟṟu
ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār tevatāṉa nilam kālum kāṉa nāṭṭu viṇ
ṇakarāḻvār tevatāṉanilam irumāvaraiyum āka nilam eḻumāva
rai nikki nikki uḷḷa nilattil muṉpiṭṭa nilam iraṇṭu veli uṭ
paṭa ivvāṇṭu mutal antarāyamum viniyōkamum veṭṭi pāṭṭamum
pañcupili cantuvikkirakap peṟum maṟṟum epperppaṭṭiṉavum u
ppaṭa tirukkāmakkoṭṭapuṟa iṟaiyili āka iruppatāka iṭṭamaik
ku variyilār eḻuttiṭṭa uḷvari, nam • • rac coṉṉom
llilum cempilum veṭṭivittu koḷkaveṉṟum tiruvāy mo
ḻintaruḷiṉār ivai miḻalaik kūṟṟattu kīḻkūṟṟu veṭṭaiṉūṟu
ṭpaṭa tirukkāmak koṭṭappuṟam iṟaiyili iṭṭapaṭikku uḷvari kiḻvem
tamiḻap pallavaraiyar puṟanāṭṭu tirukkoḷakkuṭi ivai puravari ti

taruḷivitta āḷuṭaiya nācciyārkku tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu ivai puravari
tiṇaikkaḷa nāyakam pi

kūṟṟattuk kīḻkūṟṟu ponpaṟṟi poṉpaṟṟi uṭaiyār centapirāṉāṉa
cempiyat

āvatu antarāyamum viniyokamum uṭpaṭa
tirukkāmakkoṭṭapuṟam iṟaiyili iṭṭapaṭi

pañcupili cantuvikkirakappeṟu maṟṟum epperppaṭṭaṉavum ivai puravari
tiṇaikkaḷa nāyakam miḻalai

ṉa vīraṭṭāṉṉa eḻuttu ivai toṇṭaimāṉ eḻuttu
epperppaṭṭaṉavum uṭpaṭa tirukkāma

koṭṭappuṟam iṟaiyili iṟuppatāka kāṉanāṭṭu kumāttakkuṭi
nāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nilam

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌḶANĀTH TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-15TH YEAR
(1231 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 2 vēli taxfree lands to the
temple officials (Dāṉattār) by one Tamil Tallavaraiyaṉ, the resident of
Rājavallapuram of Kīḻvēmbanāḍu for the maintenance of newly installed
deity Āluḍaiyanāchchiyār in the temple of Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiyār at
Tirukkōlakkuḍi in Puramalaināḍu. This transcation was done as instructed
by the son-in-law of Kulaśēkhadēvar. (See No. 78 above).

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA-2ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to an order of Gāngaiyaṉ, who issued to the
temple authorities of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi recording the sale of the Kārāṇmai
right of a land in Koṟṟamaṅgalam, a village near Iḷayātkakkuḍi in
Kalvāyilnāḍu to a certain Nakularāya, besides the grant, consisting of
several taxes like Antarāyam, Achchu, Veniyōgam, Karpuravilar,
Kāriyavārāchi, Poṉvari, Siṟṟāyam Silvari, Veṭṭu7muṭṭiyāl, Echchōṟu, Kūṟṟarasi,
etc., on the land to the temple are mentioned.

l.ta: ka viṟṟa kuṭikāṭu kalvāyil nāṭṭu iḷaiyāttakkuṭip
paṭṭa nirnilamum naṉcey puṉceyyum nattamum toṭ
ṭamum kuḷamum kuḷapparippum meṉokkiya maramum kiṇo
mai antarāyam accuvari viṉiokam karppūravilai kāriya
āḷ eccoṟu kūṟṟarici maṟṟum eppeṟppaṭṭaṉavum uṭai
m itu [ip]piṭipāṭāka[k*] koṇṭu kulacekara tevaṟku yiraṇ
tāka kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik koṇṭu nāṉ ke[l*]
vai kāṅkeya ṉeḻuttu ippaṭikku utaiya tivā

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVANA CHA[KRAVARTIN]
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA, WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE [ALL
COUNTRIES]-22ND YEAR [13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a land which was sold publicly to the
temple of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi in Puṟamalai nāḍu a subdivision of
Poṉṉamarāvati-paṟṟu by the ūrār of Viraiyāchchilai in Kānānāḍu, a sub-
division of Kāṉanāḍu alias Virudarājabhayaṅkaravaḷanāḍu in order to pay
the taxes due by them. Mentions the temple as Ulagaviḍaṅgīśvaramuḍaiya-
nāyaṉār. It also states that the original documents connected with the land
were deposited in the same temple. Also mentions the oppression by some
tax collectors of the king.

l.ta:
vattikaḷ[emmaṇṭalamuṅ] koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulacekara tevaṟku
yāṇṭu

tiyaiyum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum
peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ kāṉa ṉāṭāṉa viruta

rācapayaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭuk kāṉanāṭṭup paṭaippaṟṟu viraiyāccilai
•••••••••• ūrom eṅkaḷūrkkuk kaṭamaikkut tevar kaṇṭiyatevar vācalil
karumañcevārkaḷil eṅkaḷ nāṭu piṭitta tirunelveliyuṭaiyār
eṅkaḷukku ivvāṇṭai kaṭamai niṟceyiṟpukkum muṉpoṉa vāṇṭai
cikaikkum nālāyiram paṇam vari kuṭṭu itukku eṅkaḷūr āṟru
ūrumāyc cāpopo perumāṉpaṭiyāle eṅkaḷ aṭṭikuṭi kaṉṟukāli
akappaṭa viṟṟum ēṟṟivaittum pokkiṭalāvatu pokkiṭa ic

celavaṟṟu calavarāta paṇattiṟku veṟoru pōkku yillātapaṭiyālum
akāṟamāyp paṇattukku mukkā nellumāy veṟuoru nāḷ orukācu
taṉicu taruvārumiṉṟi mirukkaiyālum uṭaiyār tirunelveli uṭaiyār
paṉṉāta muṭṭuppāṭu paṇṇukaiyil i

ṉṉāṭṭuk kumāttaṅkuṭi ūravar pakkal iv •••••••••• vataṟku muṉpu eṅkaḷūr
aracu makkaḷil rāmpo ••••• uḷḷiṭṭār vilai koṇṭu eṅkaḷūr puravil
ēraṭṭu āṟupokattu kaṭamai iṟuttu potukiṟa kuṭikāṭu
nāṭṭupperaiyaṉ viṟpataṟku icaiñcu koḷvār uḷaro eṉṟu
orukālāvatum irukālāvatum mukkālāvatum nāṅkaḷ muṟkūṟak
koḷveṉeṉṟu ponṉamarāpatippaṟṟup puṟamalaināṭṭu tirukkoḷakku

ṭi uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār āticaṇṭecuratevaṟku
ūrañcāta ūr kīḻ iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇi vilaippiramāṇam
paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu ippaṭi viṟpataṟku icaiñca ikkuṭikkāṭu
nāṭṭup peraiyaṉukku

perunāṉ kellaiyāvatu kīḻpāṟkellai muṉ kuḷattut teṉvaṭalāka
pokiṟa peruvaḻik kuṭakumaṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ikkumāttakkuṭi
ellaikkuṭa vaṭakkum meṟpāṟkellai eṅkaḷūr nāyaṉār
ulakaliṭaṅkīcuramuṭaiya nāya

ṉār tevatāṉam mutaliyārk koyil vācal pāccamutaliyār paṟṟu
aiññūṟṟu maṅkalattu vayalkkuḷaṅ kovaikkuk kiḻakkum iṉṉāṭṭup
peraiyaṉ kuḷattukku nīreṟṟam ikkum kovai melvarampukku meṟku
ēriṉamaṭṭam ēṟuvatākavum vaṭapā

k kellai muṉṉāḷ uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉārkku
nāṅkaḷ iṟaiyili tirukāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟa iṉnāṭṭā ••••• teṟkum
nāṭṭup peraiyaṉ kuḷattukku nīrvāykkāl mutaliyār kuṭakkāṭu
aiññūṟṟu maṅkalattuk kuḷattil vaṭakaṭaiyai aṭuttum vaṭavaya

lai aṭuttum vaṭakku kīḻ melāka varukiṟa ikkuḷattukkut
teṉkaṭaiyil muṉpilāṇṭu varum mariyātippaṭi uḷḷa kālnīr

iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka emmil icaiñca vilaipporuḷukku
ūrkīḻ iṟaiyili āka vilaikkuṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟak koṇṭu

nāṭṭup peraiyaṉ iṉnāḷ mutal eṅkaḷūrppuṟa kaḻittu
ikkuṭikkāṭṭukku varum kaṭamai accuvari poṉvari viniokam pañcupili

ūrom ippaṭi iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟa inta nāṭṭu
peraiyaṉukku emmilicaiñca vilaipporuḷ niṟcayitta paṇam

k kaḷattekāṭṭi ēṟṟi kaiccala koṇṭu ippaṭi viṟṟa iṉnāṭṭup

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL. (INSIDE)
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-11TH YEAR (1249 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription is highly damaged that refers to the gift of land as
offerings to the temple of Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiyār. Mentions the place
name Sembināṭṭu Kulakkudi-ulagalanda-chōlanallūr etc.

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL. (INSIDE)
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA-7TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Badly damaged. It seems to refers to a gift of land as kārāṇmai
Tirunamaṭṭukkāni by some private individuals viz., Semmaṅguḍaiya
Veliyāṟṟḍaiyāṉ, Kuvalayattu-Araiyan-Chokkaṇ, Āṇḍapillaiyar to the temple
of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi-uḍaiyanāyaṉār at Tirukkōḷakkuḍi in Puramalaināḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī komāṟupaṉmarāṉ [ tiribhuvaṉac cakkaravarttikaḷ] śrī kulacekara
tevaṟku yāṇṭu [7] vatu tai mātam puṟamalai nāṭṭut tirukkoḷakkum
uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār āticaṇṭeśvara te

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL AND SOUTH
WALL OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
(MĀṞAVARMAN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA I,).
inscription.p:
It records the gift of land for the offerings to the shrine of
Āḷuḍaiya-nāchchiyār of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam, built in the temple of
Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiyār by a native of Rājavallapuram in Kīḻvēmbanāḍu.
Mentions the seat called Māḷḷavarāyaṉ [in the king's palace at]
Māḍakkuḷakkīḻ Madurai in Madurōdayavaḷanāḍu and the king's brother-in-
law Aḻgapperumāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 85 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME
TEMPLE ŚA[ṬĀVARMAN] ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-‘WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE THE
CHŌḺA COUNTRY-22ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sale of land called Karikālachōḻa-
Pallavaraiyaṉ of Kuḍikkāḍu to the temple by a certain Tribhuvaṇaśiṅgaṉ, a
native of Kuṉṟattūr in Kalvāyilnāḍu, a sub-division of Kēralaśiṅgavaḷanāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī makkittikkumel śrīkocca[ṭaipaṉma]rāṉa tiripuvaṉac
cakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭu koṇṭaruḷiya śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya dēvaṟku
yāṇṭu 22- vatu puṟamalaiṉāṭṭut tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār
civataṉṭī

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALL ON
THE SAME WALL MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KULASĒKHARADĒVA, ‘WHO WAS
PLEASED TO TAKE ALL COUNTRIES’-15TH+1st = 16TH YEAR (1284
A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This Inscription registers a public sale of the village
Nāṭṭanēri to the temple, by the ūrār of Vīraiyāchchilai under conditions
similar to those mentioned in No. 81 above. The old documents connected
with the land are said to have been deposited in the temple. Further, the
document (ōlai) lucidly describes the shares of paddy among the
functionaries like Ūr-kaṇakkar, kaṇmālar, Paṟaiyar of Vīrayāchchilai besides
the supply of paddy as offerings to be made to the god and goddess. This
document was written by Vayalagamuḍaiyār Uyyavandāṉ-uḻaguḍaiyāṉ.

edition.l:
śrīmaṟkittikkumel śrīkomāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ
e[m*]maṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulacekara[t]evaṟku yāṇṭu 15-
[vatu] eti •••••

rāmāṉṭaik kaṟkaṭaka nāyaṟṟu apa •••••••••• ti[tiy]aiyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum

viṟpiyaḷākil viṟṟu vilai

ṉār śrī paṇṭārattile oṭukkuṅkoḷeṉṟu collukaiyāle aracu
makkaḷum maṟamutalikaḷum viṟpataṟkicaintu ūrañcāti iṟaiyili
tirunāmattukkāṇiyākak koḷviruḷḷiro[veṉ]ṟu palakālum

tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār
āticaṇṭe

ūravar pakkal koṇṭa nāṭṭāṉerikkup perunāṉ kellaiyāvatu
kiḻpāṟkellai teṟku nokkippo •••••••••• peruvaḻikku meṟkum teṉpāṟ
kellai nāṭṭupperayaṉ kuṭikkāṭṭil irācaṉ coṇṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār
kuṭitāṅki

vaṭavarampukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai ciṟṟalai vayalil ilaṅkātāṉ
uḷḷiṭṭār oḷiyāraraṇāṉ vayakkalil kiḻvarampukkuk kiḻakkum itaṉ
vaṭakkuc ciṟṟalai vayalil vayalparippukku teṟkum iṉnāṭṭāṉerik
kuḷattukku nirellai eṟina maṭṭam meṟuvatākavum āka icaiñca
perunāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭa nirnilamum nañcey puñceyyum nattamun
toṭṭamum kuḷamum peciṉa ellaipparippum kiṇokkiya kiṇaṟum
meṉokkiya maramu

m palamaramum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum uḷnilamoḻiviṉṟi
inṉāṭṭāṉerikku emmil icainta vilaipporuḷukku iṟaiyili

tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom
uṭaiya tirukko[ḷa*]kkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār āti caṇṭe

ratevaṟku
aracu makkaḷum maṟamutalikaḷum ippaṭi viṟṟa nāṭṭāṉerikkukkaṭamai
aṉtārayam poṉvari viṉiyokam veṇṭukoḷ kaṟpūravilai
kāriyavārāṭci cilvari peru

vari pañcupili caṉtuvikkiṟakam veṭṭip pāṭṭam muṭṭāvāḷ cevakam
eṟcoṟu kūṟṟarici maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa iṟaiyum tevaiyum ūṟkiḻ
iṟaiyiliyāka viṟṟu ūrileeṟaṭṭu iṟuttup pōtuvomākavum ippaṭi
cammatittu iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka iṉnāṭṭāneri viṟṟu
muṉpu vilaikoṇṭa mulacātanamum paṇṭārattile oṭukki ūrañcāti
iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu vilaippiṟamā-

ratevaṟku aracu makkaḷum
maṟamutalikaḷum ippaṭi viṟṟa nāṭṭāṉerikku emmil icainta
vilaipporuḷ vilaiyāvaṇakkaḷatte kāṭṭiyeṟṟik kaiccelavaṟak
koṇṭu iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka innāṭṭāṉeri viṟṟu
vilaippiṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta ituve vilaiyolaiyāvatākavum
ituvallatu veṟuporuṇmāvaṟutip poruṭcelavo-

lai kāṇavum kāṭṭavum kaṭavatallavākavum ivvolaikku ōlaikkuṟṟam
colkuṟṟam eḻuttukkuṟṟam collakaṭava tallavākavum
ikkuṭikkāṭṭukku viraiyāccilaiyil nāyaṉāṟkum piṭāriyāṟkum tevar
arimukkaiy micukuṟuṇi ūrkkaṇakkar ōlaipeṟum kaṇmāḷarpēṟu
paṟaiyarpeṟu viraiyāccilaip paṟṟiṟpeṟum peṟu eṅkaḷurk kuṭimakkaḷ
peṟuvārkaḷākavum ippaṭi viṟṟa inṉāṭṭā-

paṉammum paṇṇi iccātaṉappaṭiyile
kallilum cempilum veṭṭikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai iṟaiyili
tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka aṉupavip

pārkaḷākavum ippaṭi cammatittu iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu
muṉpu ikkuṭikkāṭu vilaikoṇṭa mulacātaṉamum paṇṭārattile
oṭukki ūrañcāti iṟaiyili tirunāmattukkā

ṇiyāka viṟṟu vilaippiṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom uṭaiyār
tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyaṉāyanār āticaṇṭe

ratevaṟku
viraiyāccile aracu makkaḷum maṟamutalikaḷum ippaṭikku ivarka-

ḷ colla ippiṟamāṇam eḻutineṉ ivvūrkkaṇakku vayalakamuṭaiyāṉ
uyyavantāṉ ulakuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 87.
(A.R. No. 87 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME
TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions an order of Gāṅgēyarāja regarding the
offerings and renovation to be made in the shrine of Āḷuḍaiyanāchchiyār
built in the same temple by Tamiḻappallava-daraiyaṉ.

l.ta: kkum nāṉāḻi aricic coṟu peṟuvatāka ava

inscription.head:
No. 88.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-13TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is a kings order to restore the right of Śivabrāhmaṇa
to receive his share of food in the temple as per the document of the
kings brother-in-law Aḻagapperumāḷ.

l.ta: tāṉ ikkoyilil peṟṟu vanta nāṉāḻi aricic co
celvatāka ikko(yi)ṟ tāṉa(t)tāṟkku taṭiolai poti kā

inscription.head:
No. 89.
(A.R. No. 89 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
SUNDARPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-2ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
Built in at the right end. This inscription records that an order of
the nāṭṭār of Kāṇānāḍu alias Virudharāj[abhayaṅ]karavaḷanāḍu, regarding
the gift of a land to the temple.

l.ta: ttu āḷuṭaiya nācciyāṟku ulakuṭaiya nāyaṉār eṅka •••••
viṉiyokam kaṟpūravilai kāriya ārāṭci maṟṟum ep •••••

māta •••••

inscription.head:
No. 90.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA, ‘WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE THE
CHŌḺA COUNTRY.’-12TH YEAR (1228 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of a water tank called
Amarādichchaṉkuḍik-kāṭṭukkuḷam by the ūṟār of Viraiyāchchilai, which was
a Paḍaippaṟṟu to the ūrār of Kumāttakkuḍi.

l.ta:
vatu āvaṇimātat torunāḷ
kāṉanāṭāṉa virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu paṭai

ppaṟṟu viraiyāccilai ūrāka vicainta araiyankaḷom yiṉnāṭṭuk
kumāttakakuṭi yūvaṟku nīr vilaippiṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu
eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāna amarāticcaṉ kuṭikkāṭṭu

kkuḷam uṭaikuḷappaṭṭu vayalum vatiyaḻiñcu kāṭum mentu
pāḻāykiṭakkaiyil ikkuḷattu nin vilaiyāka ivarkaḷ marutaṅkuḷattilē
pāyvitāka iṉninku emmilicainta vilaip poruḷ niccayitta paḻaṅkācu

ppaḻaṅkācu patiṉeṭṭukkum vilaikkuṟa viṟṟup poruḷaṟak koṇṭu viṟṟu
vilai piramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom kumāttakkuṭiyū

vaṟku
viraiyāccilai ūrāka icainta araiyaṅkaṉom ippaṭi viṟṟa
ikkuḷattil nir

pāyumiṭattuk kuḷam naṭuvākak kālāka veṭṭi pirāṉ kuḷattil
vaṭakaṭaiyi ••••• kālāka veṭṭi marutaṅkuḷattil pāccik koḷvārkaḷa

ṭṭik koṇṭu aṉupavippārkaḷākavum ippaṭi cammatittu nir
vilaippiṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom kumāttakuṭi ūravaṟku
viraiyāccilai ••••• ppaṭikku maṅkala nāṭāḻvāṉ eḻuttu uṭkoṇṭa
tevaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku [na]raciṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku
aññūṟṟuvatevaṉ eḻuttu ••••••••••
