vilaippiramāṇam eḻutiṉeṉ ivvūṟkaṇak ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 91.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULASĒKHARADĒVA-10TH YEAR (13TH CENTURY A.D.)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription mentions the shrine of Akkasālaipiḷḷaiyār
built on the bank of the tank called Poṉṉiūruṇi alias Pichchikuḻi in
Tirukkōḷakkuḍi. Signatories like Karaiyūr Arasaṉ and Teṟkāṭṭūr Saḍaiyaṉ
etc.

edition.l:
svastiśrī komāṟu paṉmarāṉa tiripu[va*]ṉaccakaravatikaḷ śrī
kulacekaratevaṟku yāṇṭu 10- vatu pruścika nāyaṟ

l.ta:
tiyaiyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa rocaṉināḷ
puṟamalaiṉāṭṭut tirukkoḷa

kkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār tirukkoyilt tāṉattom ivvūr poṉṉi
ūruṇiyāṉa picci kuḻikkarai

yil eḻuntaruḷi irukkum akkacālaippiḷḷaiyāṟku kalvāyūr nāṭṭuk
kāḷaiyar kālapperunteruvi ••••••••••
••••• ramuṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu || i ūr aracaṉ aracan eḻuttu ivvū-

••••• ṉūr veḷāṉ puṭṭaṉ celvañāṉacampan eḻuttu ivvūrp perumāḷ
•••••

inscription.head:
No. 92.
(A.R. No. 92 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL (INSIDE) OF THE
SAME TEMPLE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-11TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that at the instance of a certain
Maḷavarāyaṉ, the village of Muṉṉaikkuḍi in Puramalaināḍu which was
purchased once by the temple from the Maravās of Vēlaṅguḍi in
Pūṅguṉṟanāḍu was made tax-free by the king. Signed by Muḍivaḻaṅguvēḷāṉ
Vikramapāṇḍya Aḻagiyapāṇḍya-viluppaṟaiyaṉ.

l.ta:
kkum civappirāmaṇaṟkuṭi iṉṉāyaṉāṟkut tiruppaṭi māṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭu
veṇṭu ṉiva
ntaṅkaḷuk pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu velaṅkuṭi maṟṟum [cayakkal ittevaṟku •••••]

edition.l: yokamum veli[ kūrala catram] poṉvariyum ••••••••••

l.ta: ṉavum uṭpaṭṭa ••••• etirāmāṇṭu mutal iṟaiyiliyāka iṭappeṟa ve

ṇumeṉṟu aiyyaṉ maḻavarāyaṉ namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil
icciva

ṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkut tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭ veṇṭu nivantaṅkaḷukku
pūṅkuṉṟa nā

aṉupavittu varukiṟa puṟamalaināṭṭu muṉṉaikkuṭi vilaiyolaippaṭi
nāṉkel

laikkuḷppaṭṭa nirnilamuṅ karuñcey puṉceyu nattamun toṭṭamuṅ
kuḷamuṅ kuḷa

pparippum uṭpaṭa nilaṅkaṭamaiyantarāyam viṉiyokamu veli[k*]kāla ••••• m
po-
ṉvari ciṟṟāyamum maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum uṭpaṭa ivvāṇṭu mutal
cantirātittavay celvatāka kalliluñ cempilu veṭṭivittu kuṭuta •••••
ṉāṉa aḻakiya pāṇṭiya viḻupparaiyaṉ eḻuttu yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 93.
(A.R. No. 93 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF
THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a pillar by one of the temple
servants named Saiva Sikhāmaṇi-Tiruvottasāmam Aḻagiyāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 94.
(A.R. No. 94 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON ANOTHER PILLAR OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that a pillar was presented by a potter of
Tirukkōḷakkuḍi viz., Avaiyam Pukkāṉaṉ alias Ālāla-Sundaravēḷāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 95.
(A.R. No. 95 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON ANOTHER PILLAR OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that another pillar of the steps, infront of the
shrine of the goddess was presented by Nallaiya poṉṉaṉ, one of the
tandirimārs of the same village.

inscription.head:
No. 96.
(A.R. No. 96 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SUBRAHMAṆYA SHRINE, NEAR THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the construction of the shrine, with steps
besides the images of Subrahmaṇya Piḷḷaiyār, Deivānaiyār and
Vaḷḷināchchiyār which were installed by Tiruvāṉayār Aḻagar, a native of
Kalavaiyūr in Sundarachōḻapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 97.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE ROCK TO THE SOUTH OF
THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
Built in at the beginning. In modern characters. This inscription
records the gift of land by a certain Ārumugam of Pīrāṉmalai to
Chokkalingam, for performing pūja in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 98.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE ROCK ADJOINING THE
SPRING, BY SIDE OF THE TEMPLE. SĀYANA UḌAIYĀR, (1382 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that stone enclosure was built around spring
besides cleaned and maintained by Pārambaiyūruḍaiyāṉ Poṉṉāṇḍār
Tirukkaivēlaḻagiyār, a native of Sundarachōḻapuram alias
Dēsiyugandapaṭṭaṇam in Ollaiyūr kūṟṟam.

inscription.head:
No. 99.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF
ŚIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
In modern characters. This inscription registers the sale of land to
Vikkarabrahmaṉ a tandirimār of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi by some persons viz.,
Perumāḷ, Vallāḻadēvaṉ, Kavasi Kūttaṉ etc., of the same village.

l.ta: • aṟupatum nāṅkaḷ paṟṟikkoṇṭa paṭiya • • ṉālle ituve
maṟṟum eṟpi • • paṭṭa cuvantiramumā[ka] ••••• cātam u

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
(PĀṆḌYA).,-13TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land situated in the village of
Śirukuḍi in Vaḍapirappunāḍu to the shrine of Āḷuḍaiyapiḷḷaiyār built by
him, for offerings during the service called Buvaṉēkaviraṉśandi, named
after the king. Poṉpaṟṟi-uḍaiyāṉ Uyyavandāṉ Sūryadēvaṇ alias Gāngeyaṉ of
Siṟumarudūr in Vaḍaveḷḷāru alias Sundarapāṇḍya vaḷanāḍu figures as
signatory. Taxes like Antarāyam, Poṉvari, Vinīyōgam Veṭṭibāṭṭam, Pañjupili,
Sandivikirappēru, Vāsalperu, Elāñjinai-peru, Tarirai, Taṭṭolibāṭṭam, Āṭṭuvari,
Kāṭṭuvari, iḍaiyarvari inavari etc.

l.ta: kku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷu[ṭ*]ṭu veṇṭum nitta [nima]
ṉ veṇṭotta perunāṉ kellaikkuḷppaṭṭa nirnilattil veṇ-
ṭakaṉ nāyaṉār paḻantevatāṉam nikki nikki uḷḷa nilamum ikkuḷattil

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF
THE SAME TEMPLE,
SUNDARPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-20+1ST = 21ST YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of taxes due imposed on the lands
called Vēlārkuḍikkāḍu and Karikālachōḻappallavaraiyaṉ kuḍikāḍu, to the
temple (Vide No. 85 above) of Śivadharamīśvara-muḍaiya-nāyaṉār at
Tirukkōḷakkuḍi for the maintenance by the Signatories like Vānādhirāyaṉ,
Aṟivāḍaṉ mūvēndavēlāṉ, Udaiya-Divākara mūvēndavēlaṉ, Prākrama
mūvēndavēḷāṉ etc.

l.ta: ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār śrī koyil śrī ru
rarum śrī kāriyañ ceyvārkaḷum kaṇṭu iḷai
ṟṟa namaṉ kuṭippāl veḷār kuṭikāṭu muṟṟum kunṟattūruṭaiya
n tiripuvaṉa kaṅkaṉuḷḷiṭṭār viṟṟa karikāla coḻa pallavatarai
yaṉ kuṭikkāṭu muṟṟum ivarkaḷ viṟṟa vilai ōlaiyil eppaṭiyile [vi]

ttu ippaṭikku vāṇata tārayaṉ eḻuttu ivai aṟivāṭāṇa muventa
veḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai
ma pāṇṭiya muventa veḷān eḻuttu i

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST AND SOUTH
WALL ON THE SAME TEMPLE.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
VĪRASUNDARA PĀ[Ṇ*]ḌYADĒVĀ, ‘WHO HAVING TAKEN THE
CHŌḺA COUNTRY, 20+1ST = 21ST YEAR (1236 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land in the village of
Muṉṉaikkuḍi in Puṟamalaināḍu to the same temple by the Maṟavās of
Vēlaṅguḍi in Pūṅguṉṟanāḍu. The order (ōlai) of Vikramasiṅgaṉ was written
in continuation of the record and signed by Teṉṉavadaraiyaṉ which states
that the gift was to be utilized for the service of Vīramiṇḍaṉ-sandi.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 103 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL
(INSIDE) OF THE TEMPLE KITCHEN ON THE SAME HILL
[MĀṞA]VARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVANACHA[KRAVARTIN SUNDARA
PĀṆḌYADĒVĀ]-[1]5TH YEAR, [13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription records the lease of temple land as Kārāṇ-kiḻamai-
uḻavukāṉi, which was lying waste to a certain Karikālachōḻa-pēraiyaṉ with
permission to cultivate and resettle with kuḍi under the name of
Tiyagasiriya Perumānllūr. Mentions the coin Pudukkuḷigai. Ellāndalaiyāna-
pāṇḍya-brahmādarāyaṉ figured as a signatory.

l.ta:
śrī maṟkīttikku mel śrī komāṟa paṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-8TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of the Kārāṇmai right of certain
temple lands to a native of Tiruppugalūr in Paṉaiyūr nāḍu, a sub-division
of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, which was a district of Chōḻamaṇḍalam. It
also stipulated that the purchased waste land be brought under cultivation
and pay the stipulated taxes. Refers to a tank called Pāvanaṅgakāranēri
that was excavated in the name of Makkaṉāyaṉār alias Parākrama-pāṇḍya-
mahābalivānādhirāyaṉ.

ttālp pāvaṉaṅkak kāṟaṉeriyeṉṟu ivar veṭṭuvitta ivveri

inscription.head:
No. 105.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
(OUTSIDE) [JA]ṬĀVA[RMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVANACHAKRAVAR]TIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription refers to an assurance given to the temple
authorities by the nāṭṭār of Poṉṉamarāvati nāḍu, for meeting the cost of
offerings made to the service called Poṉṉamarāvati-nāḍaṉ-sandi instituted in
the temple from the tax called Viniyōgam, Tappivēlāṉ, Nilamaiyaḻagiyavēlaṉ,
Takkavēlāṇ, etc., Pirakaḍakaṇavēḷaṉ Adhiyamāṉ are figured as signatories of
the record.

l.ta:

tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyaṉ ṉāyanā

yāy [po]tukiṟa ippaṟṟile eṅkaḷ nāṭṭu viniyokamāna nilam • • k

poṉṉamarāpati nāṭaṉ cantikku nāḷ

oṉṟukku amutupaṭi arici patakkum paṭi viñcaṉamuḷḷuṭṭa cilavukku
ippaṟṟil viniyokamāvuḷḷa mutal taṇ

ṭikkoṇṭu poṉṉamarāpati nāṭaṉ canti amuta ceytaruḷap
paṇṇuvatāka viṉiyokam kaḻittu ippa

ṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭik koḷḷa
piṭipāṭu paṇṇik kuṭuttom uṭaiyār

••••• koḷakkuṭi uṭaiya ṉāyanā[r*] tirukkoyil tāṉa[t*]tāṟku
poṉṉamarāpati nāṭṭu nāṭṭavarom ippaṭi nā

ṭṭār colpaṭi tappi veḷ[ā*]ṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku nilamaiyaḻakiya
veḷāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku mana ••••• takka

cūriyāṇ •••••

inscription.head:
No. 106.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA, ‘WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL THE
COUNTRIES’-44TH YEAR (1312 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of the village Perunallūr to the
temple of Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tirukkōḷakkuḍi by the
nāṭṭavar of Kāṉa nāḍu alias Virudarājabhayaṅkaravaḷanāḍu for conducting
the service called Seṇbaga-kulaśēkaraṉ-sandi, instituted by the king
Kulaśēkharadēva on the occasion of the festival Tiruvāṇi-tirunāḷ Kōṭṭaiyūr-
ulagalanda-chōḻa-kānāṭṭuvēḷaṉ, Mēlūrvallanāṭṭuvēlān, Durmanāṭṭuvēḷāṉ etc.,
figured as signatories.

l.ta:
kku mel komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ
emmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷi

edition.l:
ya śrī kulacekara tevaṟku yāṇṭu 44 vatu taim mātam puṟamalaināṭṭut
tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiy[ ār śi]

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉār tirukkoyilt tānattāṟku kānaṉāṭāṉa
virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu

iṉnāyaṉāṟku nāyaṉār [ku]

cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 107.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH AND WEST
WALL OF THE STORE ROOM ATTACHED TO THE SAME
KITCHEN
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARA DĒVA-4TH YEAR, SAKA 1231 (1309 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of land to a devotee of the temple
of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi-uḍaiyanāyaṉār named Aḻagiyadēvar alias
Paramēśvararabhaṭṭar by Kaṉṉimalaināḍāḻvāṉ and others of the same
place, probably for performing pūja. Taxes on Kammāla, Paṟaiyar were
exempted.

l.ta:
śrīmaikkittikku mel komārupaṉmarāṉa
tiripuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrī kula cekara

itiṉ mel cellā niṉṟa
kaṉṉi nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu

yum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇaṟ[pūca]ttu nāḷ puṟamalaināṭṭu
tirukkoḷakkuṭi maṟamutalika

ḷil kaṉṉimalai nāṭāḻvāṉ uḷḷiṭṭārom eṅkaḷūrkku potukāṇi
irācentira coḻaṉ kuṭikkāṭum ceḻiya

ṉ ēmpalum vayalum āka ikkuṭikkāṭu iraṇṭum perunāṉ
kellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nīrnilamum naṉcey puṉceyum

nattamum tōṭṭamum menokkiya maramum kiṇekkiya kiṉaṟum kuḷamum
kuḷapparippu maṟṟum ikkuṭikkāṭukaḷukku peṟunāṉ

kaḷum emmil icainta
vilaipporuḷukku kāṇiyāka koṇṭu nāṅkaḷ tirukko

ḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉākku eṅkaḷ kāṇikaḷukku nāṅkaḷ tevatāṉak
kaṭamai irukkum •••••

iccil taṉ eḻumpotu uḷḷiṭṭār kāṇiyāṉa koṭṭivayal paṟṟukku
irukkum accu kūṟu oṉpatil oṉ ••••••••••

niṉṟa kūṟu eṭṭil oru kūṟu inta irācentira coḻaṉ kuṭikaṭṭukkum
ceḻiya • • yaṉ kuṭi kāṭṭukkum tevatāṉa kaṭamai vik •••••

yāka •••raṇṭu anupokiyār uḷaroveṉṟu orukālāvatu
mirukālāvatu muṟkūṟa immoḻikeṭṭu piṟkūṟa koḷveṉeṉṟa

tevaṉ cokkaṉāṉa na ••••• paṭṭarkku ivvūr maṟamutalikaḷil kannimalai

potukkāṇiyāṉa irācentiracoḻa kuṭikkāṭum ceḻiyataraiyaṉ
ēmpalum •••••

tamum toṭṭamum kuḷamum kuḷapparappum oṭaiyu muṭaippum maṟṟum
ivvayalkaḷ perunāṉ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa

kaḷuma
emmilicainta vilaipporuḷukku vilaikkuṟa viṟṟu poruḷa
vilaiyāvaṉṉak kaḷattil kāṭṭa

yeṟṟi •••••••••• vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttom kaṉnimalai

īḻam puñceyvariyum potuvil kaḻittu nikki

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH TIER OF
THE SUBRAMAṆYA TEMPLE ON THE SAME HILL
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA-4TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that certain privileges like sacred ash, pulling
the rope of the temple car and breaking coconut besides performing pūja
before the deity Vināyaga were given to four nāṭṭārs.

l.ta:
āvatu ••••• tiruttEr uṟcamam koṇṭaruḷukiṟātaṟku nālu
nāṭṭārum

••••• tirunīrukkum vaṭampiṭikkiṟatukkum teṅkāy vināyaka pūjai
paṇṇukiṟataṟkum kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭatu mutalāvatu ••••• nāṭṭāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH AND WEST
TIERS OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription records the list of persons viz., Vaḍatalaisembināṭṭu
Arasapillai, Kāṭṭināyaṇārā's, mother Eḻugapperumāḷ, Āriyaṉ Taṅganārāyaṇa
dēvaṉ who donated stones and pillars to the temple. Shri Āttabhaṭṭar,
Sembināṭṭu Āḷuḍaiyāṉ and malaimaṇḍalattu Sundara Pāṇḍya-gauriyār paid
cash as gift to the temple.

l.ta: ••••• [pi] nallūr perumāṉ atiravaca āvoṉ kāṅkeyaṉ taṉmam
vaṭatalaic cempi nāṭṭu aracapiḷḷai ceyta kallumūṉṟu
rattu āriyaṉ taṅka nārāyaṇa tevaṉ taṉmakallu añcu §
kaṭṭi nāyaṉār tāyār eḻukap perumāḷ taṉmam kallu iraṇṭu §
virapāṇṭiya tevar malaiyāṉ mutalikaḷil periyāṉ pāntā

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH TIER OF
THE SAME TEMPLE.
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA, ‘WHO WAS PLEASED TO TAKE ALL THE
COUNTRIES’-2[2]ND YEAR (1288 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the sale of the village Tiruvāṉḍiyēri as
Tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Iḷaiyapiḷḷaiyār by the temple servants,
Kappalūr-uḍaiyāṉ figures as one of the signatories.

l.ta:
śrī makkittikku mel śrī komāṟa paṉmarāṉa tirahuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ
emmaṇṭalamuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrī kulacekara tevaṟku yāṇṭu

pakṣattu ēkateciyum veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattu nāḷ
puramalaināṭṭut tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya
nāyaṉār āticaṇṭe

rak kaṅkāṇi cikāriyam ceyvār koyiṟ
kaṇakkarom iṉṉāyaṉār tirumalaimel iḷaiyapiḷḷaiyārkku
tirunāmattukkāṇi vilai

paricāvatu iṉṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇi tiruvāṇṭiyeri perunāṉ
kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nirnilamum naṉcey puṉceyyum nattamuṉ toṭṭamu
kuḷapparippum meṉokkiya maramum kīḻṇo

kkiyakkiṇaṟum maṟṟum epperppaṭṭaṉavum uḷpaṭa emmilicainta
vilaipporuḷukku ippiḷḷaiyārkku tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka viṟṟu
vilai

rakkaṅkāṇi cikāriyam
ceyvār koyiṟ kaṇakkarom ippaṭi viṟṟa ittiruvāṇṭiyeri
cantirātittavar iḷaiyapiḷḷaiyārkkut tirunāmattukkāṇiyāy amu

tupaṭikkum tiruppaṇikkum immutal celavaḻivutāka ippaṭi cammatittu
vilaippiramāṇam paṇṇi kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik kuṭuttom
āticaṇṭe

ṅkāṇi śrī kāriyañ ceyvār koyiṟ kaṇakkarom ippaṭikku

ippaṭikku kulacekarappaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ālāla cuntarapaṭṭaṉ•

cantiracekarapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu

kappalūruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku koyiṟkkaṇakku •••••••••• veḷār
eḻuttu •

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF
THE CELLAR CALLED KOḌAGARAI ON THE SAME HILL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-18TH YEAR
AND 203rd DAY.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the kings gift of taxes like ponvaṟi,
Veṭṭipāṭṭam, Pañjupili, Ilañjinarpēru, Tariyiṟai, Chekkiṟai, iṉavari and
siṟṟāyam etc., due from the inhabitants Maravar, Iḍaiyar and Valaiyar
community of the same place to the temple of Śivadharmīśvaramuḍaiya-
nāyaṉār for undertaking some repair works in the name of the king's
elder brother (aṉṉālvi).

l.ta:
koyilt tāṉattāṟku ivvūril maṟavarum iṭaiyarum valaiyarum
uḷpaṭṭa kuṭimakkaḷum iv

vūṟku aṭaippāṉa miḻalai vayal koṭṭi vayalukkum iṟuttuppotum
kaṭamai antarāya

m poṉvari taruvatāṉa accu veṭṭi pāṭṭam pañcipili
cantivikkirakappeṟu vācalppe

yum ciṟṟāyam uḷpaṭṭa āyavaṟkkam maṟṟum eṟppeṟpaṭṭa iṟaikaḷum
aṇṇāḻvikku

irupattāṟāvatu mutal iṉṉāyaṉār koyil namperālc ceykiṟa
cuntarapāṇṭiyaṉ aru

variyilār kaṇakkilumk kaḻippatākavum coṉṉom ippaṭikku
ivvolai piṭipāṭāka

koṇṭu nāṉkellaiyilum tiruccūlattā[pa*]ṉamum paṇṇi kallilum
cempilum veṭṭikko •

ḷkai ivai taccaṉeṉ mala ••••• ṉeḻuttu 2 yāṇṭu 18- vatu nāḷ
203 •

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE CELLAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription mentions the name of a person
Srivengappayyaṉ Kummachchayyan and a measurement nāḻī etc.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF
THE SAME CELLAR
MĀṞAVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KULAŚĒKHARADĒVA-20TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of money for burning six lamps
before the deity in the temple of Tirukkōḷakkuḍi uḍaiyār., The above gift
was made by a Nambiyārūraṉ alias Tarpurushadēvar a resident of
Veliyāṟṟur in Keralasiṅga vaḷanāḍu by Muppattiraṇḍuvaṭṭattu Sivabrāhmanar,
the priests of the same temple. Signatories like Mānicka-vāsaga-baṭṭaṉ,
Brāhmadhirāya baṭṭaṉ, Tillaimūvāyira bhaṭṭan etc., are figured in the
inscription.

l.ta:
komāṟa paṉmarāṉa tiripu[va*]c ca[k*]karavattikaḷ śrī
kulacekara

edition.l:
yāṇṭu 20- vatu puṟamalaināṭṭut tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiyār
śivadharmmiśvara

l.ta:
muṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil muppattira[ra*]ṇṭu vaṭṭattuc
civappirāmaṇarom

keraḷa ciṅka vaḷanāṭṭu veḷiyāṟṟūr nampiyāruraṉ ṉiṟaṇintāṉāṉa
taṟpuruṣa

tevaṟku kalveṭṭik kuṭutta paricāvatu ivaṟkāka ṉāṅkaḷ uṭaiyār
tirumuṉ

pu ciṟukālaic cantiyilirun tiruviḷakka cum tampirāṭṭiyār koyilil
viṉāyakappi

ḷḷaiyāṟku irākkāppirun tiruviḷakkoṉṟum āka nāḷoṉṟukku
iruntiru

ivvac ciraṇṭukkum ivviḷakkāṟum cantiyātipamāka muṭṭāmal
cantirātit

tavaṟ cellumaḷavu miṭuvomākac cammatittuk kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom
mup
ippaṭikku ivai māṇikkavācakap paṭṭaṉeḻuttu ivai cuntarapāṇ
ivai tillai mūvāyirapaṭṭaṉ ṉeḻuttu ikkalveṭṭukkup pa[ṭi olai e]
ḻutiṉeṉivai koyil kaṇakku coḻaṉ āṇṭa piḷḷaiyāṉa tiru

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN [KŌ]ṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ
(KULAŚĒKHARA)-41ST YEAR (1309 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription registers the order of the king
Kulaśēkharadēva that the tax dues must be set apart for conducting the
service called Senbagapperumāḷ sandi. Mentions the taxes like Andarāyam,
Poṉvari Veṭṭibāṭṭam, Tariyiṟai, Chekkiṟai, Taṭṭārbāṭṭam, Iṉavari etc., in this
inscription.

l.ta: iṉṟu ••••• ṭārṭap peṟa veṇumeṉṉu nāyaṉār kulacekara •••••
••••• namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil ivvūṟ kaṭamai antarāyam poṉ •••••
ivai vallavarāyaṉ ṉeḻuttu ••••• yāṇṭu cel ••••• nāṉ •••••

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-28TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription refers to the gift of land as
Tirunāmattukkāṇi located in Peruṅkūṟṟakkuḍi alias Sundarapāṇḍyanallūr in
Ollaiyūrkūṟṟam, a sub-division of Teṉkōnāḍu for supplying sacred food and
awakening ceremony of the deity. The above gift was made for the
expenses of the service called Ayyaṅgāṟaṉ sandi instituted by
Kulaśekharadēvaṉ.

l.ta: ramuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār koyil tāṉattārkku iṉṉāyaṉār tirup
paḷḷi eḻucci amuta ceytaruḷa ṉāyaṉār kulacekaratevaṉ kaṭṭi
ṉa aiyyaṉaṅkakkāṟaṉ cantikku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷ
ḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nittal nivantakaḷukkut teṉ koṉāṭṭu
paḷḷiccantam paḻantevatāṉam nikki nikki uḷḷa nilattil
kkuk kārāṇmai tirunāmattuk kāṇiyākat tanta nilam irupa
nilattukku māttāl iṉṉāṭṭuk kālāl mukkalamāka vanta [kaṭam]ai-
••••• ḷḷum uḻavum tavittu nam variyilār kaṇakkilum kaḻippatākac coṉ

ṉom ippaṭikku ivvolai piṭipāṭākak koṇṭu
centirāti[t*]ta[varai]
mum paṇṇik kalliluñ cempilum veṭṭik koḷ[ka*] ivai •••••

edition.l: tu yāṇṭu 28- vatu ṉāḷ 1[4]2 §

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE ROCK NEAR THE
ENTRANCE INTO THE SAME CELLAR.
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription refers to its tanks and the adjecent
fields located to the north of Tirukkōḷakkuḍināyaṉār dēvadāṉam at
Śittiramēḻinallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1916.)
TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK,
RAMNAD DISTRICT.
SIVADHARMAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST TIER OF THE
POYYĀMOḺI-IŚVARA TEMPLE, AT THE FOOT OF THE SAME HILL
TRIBHUVANACHA[KRAVARTIN VĪRAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA,-6TH YEAR,
[13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription refers to a grant of some additional land
made by one Gāngeyanto the bhattars of the Koṟṟamaṅgalam in lieu of
some old land which was not suitable for cultivation, for doing priestly
functions in the temple of Poyyāmoḻi-Iśvaramuḍaiyanāyaṇār.

l.ta: tiripuvaṉa cakkaravarttikaḷ śrī virapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

[ñca]miyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ puramalaināṭṭu
poṉṉamarāpati mutalikaḷil veṭṭuvaṭṭuvāṉ ••••• vaṉeṉ innāṭṭut
tirukkoḷakku

ṭiyil uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉār tirumalai
aṭivārattil muṉpu ••••••••••

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku tiruppaṭi māṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭu
nivantaṅkaḷukkun tiruppaṇikkuk kuṭutta nilamāvatu niyamattut tevar
kāṅkeya tevar pakkal nāṉ muṉpu vilaikoṇṭa kuṭikkel

vāyūr nāṭṭuppaṟṟuk koṟṟamaṅkalattu vayalukkuk kaṭamai antarāyam
aracuvari po •••••

yokañ ciṟṟāyam epperpaṭṭiṉavum uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya
nāyaṉārkku iruppatākak koṇṭa kāṅkeya tevar tirumukaṅ koṇṭu
eṉakkuk kārāṇmaiyāka ivar kaiyālum

ḷ kaiyālum vilai koṇṭu aṉupavittuvarukiṟa koṟṟaimaṅkalattu
•••••••••• var paṭṭiṉavum nāṉ vilai koṇṭaṉupavittupaṭiye nāyaṉār
poyyāmoḻi ī

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku tirunāmattukkāṇiyum
uṭaiyār tirukkoḷakkuṭi uṭaiya nāyaṉārkku atteṉam paṇṇi
akara

vaippatāka niṟcayiṟttu ponta paṭṭarkaḷukkuṅ koṭukka ippaṭṭarkaḷ
atteṉam paṇṇap potāmaiyālum innilam payir ceytal ceyvittal
ceyāmaiyālum ivarkaḷukkuk koṭutta nilam payireri kaṭamai
iruttāmaiyālum ippaṭṭakarukkuk koṭutta nilattukku

pūṅkuḷa nāṭṭār pakkal nāṉ vilaikoṇṭaṉupavittu varukiṟa mañcaneri
vayalile nilamum kuṭuttu ivvūrt tattile maṉaiyuṅ kuṭuttu
ippiramaṇarai koṟṟamaṅkalatti niṉṟum ēṟaviṭṭu poyyāmoḻi
ī

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku tirunāmattukkāṇi āka koṟṟa maṅkalam
perunāṉ kellaikku

ḷpaṭṭa nīrnilamum naṉcey puṉceyyum nattamun toṭṭamuṅ kuḷamuṅ
kuḷapparappuk kiṇaṟu maramu maṟṟum epperppaṭṭaṉavum nāṉ vilaikoṇṭu
aṉupavittapaṭiye tirunāmattuk kāṇiyāka kuṭuttu tevar aḻakukaṇṭa
perumāḷ cuntarapāṇṭiya kāṅkeyar tirumukamuṅ koṭuppittu ikko • • •

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH AND WEST WALLS
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that a certain Poṉṉambalanāda-Toṇḍaimāṉār
restored the taṉikkāṇiyātchi right of the village of Thaḷināṭṭumaṅgalam-
kāraiyūr near Iṭṭiyūrtaḷi to Teṉṉavadaraiyar the temple accountant. The land
had been wrongly given to another person.

l.ta: kku varakkāṭṭiṉa kāriyam iṭṭi[yū]r taḷikoyil kaṇakku u
ṉṉiṭa kāṇiyāṭci eṉkiṟa viparam aṟiyātapaṭiyāle pū
pālarāyaṉ makaṉukku kuṭuttom uṉakku pūṟuvam iṭ
ṭiyūr taḷināṭṭu maṅkalam karaiyūr uṉṉiṭa taṉikkā
ṇiyāṭci eṅkira viparam keṭṭom ākaiyāle nām
avaṉukku kuṭutta kāṇiyāṭciyum ōlaiyum koṇṭu a
vaṉukku paramillai pūṟuvam pole iṭṭiyūr taḷināṭṭu ma

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1365 (1443 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of Kāniyāṭchi right of certain
fields in the village of Māṅguḍi in Vaḍaveḷḷāru alias Sundarapāṇḍya
vaḷanāḍu to Nāyaṉār Ilakkaṇadaṇḍaṇāyaka-Toṇḍaimāṉār son of Aḻagiya
Maṇavālapperumāḷ-Toṇḍaimānār, who was the chief of Arantāṅgi for
performing certain services in the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiyār.

l.ta:
aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār makaṉār nayiṉār
ilakkaṇa

ḷāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu māṅkuṭi vayalil eṭṭiṉakkoṭṭai
tiruvakatti āvuṭaiyāṉ

ttu kuṭuttu veṭṭimuṭṭi iṉiyokam mākkalamum kaḻittu nayiṉār
tiruvakatticura muṭaiyāṟku māttāl añcumarakkāl poṉ kuṭu

koṇṭa
yiñ cūṟaṇi[ṭṭa] cantirātittavaraikku kāṇiāṭci āka

ippaṭikku uṭaiyavar aruḷicceyaṟpaṭikku nirampavaḻakiyāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1366 (1444 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers an order of Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ
Toṇḍaimāṉār issued to a temple servant Āṉanda dēvaṉ to measure out a
certain fixed quantity of paddy as taxes due to be paid by him on certain
lands to the temple of Agattīśuramuḍaiya-nayiṉār. Signatory Niramba-
aḻagiyāṉ-Kāliṅgarāyaṉ is mentioned in the inscription.

l.ta:
aṟantāṅki aracu uṭaiyār vaḻakiya maṇavāḷapperumāḷ
toṇṭaimāṉār nāṭṭiṉ maṅkalam koyil [ka]ṉmiyāṉa aṉanta

tevaṉukku kuruntaraiya koṭṭai kiḻaip perumāṇṭi vayalil muṉṉāḷ
ivaṉukku nām viṟṟa nila vaiyittiyaṉ kuṭikkāṭu nāṉ kellai

yu perumāṇṭi kuḷattilum ūruṇikaḷilu yiṉṉilattukku varum
viḻukkāṭu meṭṭu vayalil ivaṉ koṇṭa maṇavāḷa vayakkalu meṟpaṭi
nāṟ

kkāluk karuppukkālu āka inta nilattāl varum kaṭamai viṉiyokam
veṭṭimuṭṭi vecāri maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa[camu]ttap piṟāttikaḷukku nayiṉār

tiruvakatticuramuṭaiya nayiṉāṟku māttāl irutūṇi poṉ kuṭukkumpaṭi nām
kaṟpittu kuṭutta aḷavukku inta nerile cantirātittavarai
kku aṉupavittup potakkaṭavāṉākavu itukku akutam paṇṇiṉavaṉ

ttaip peṟakkaṭavāṉākavu ippaṭikku uṭaiyavar aruḷic ceyaṟpaṭikku
nirampa vaḻakiyāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1364 (1442 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of the Kāṇiyātchi right over certain
lands located in the village of Maṅguḍi in Ilaṅgōnāḍu, a sub-division of
Vaḍaveḷḷāru alias Sundarapāṇḍya vaḷanāḍu to Nayiṉār Ilakkappadaṇṇāyaka-
Tonḍaimāṉār, the son of Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷapperumāḷ-Toṇḍaimāṉār. The taxes
were to be paid to the temple.

l.ta:
aracu uṭaiyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār nayiṉār
ilakkappa

kuṭiṉāṭṭāril ēḻumper taṉpiḷḷaiyār uḷḷiṭṭār pakkal
vaṭaveḷḷāṟāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷaṉāṭṭu yiḷaṅko nāṭṭu

māṅkuṭi vayalil avaṉ pakkal koṇṭa nilam tevaṉ vayakkal rūpāy • •
nirpokiṟa tevaṉ vayakkal rūpāy • • āka nilam

yiliyāka kaḻittu yitukku veṭṭimuṭṭi viṉiyokam mākkalam yivai kaḻittu
nayiṉār tiruvakatticuramuṭaiyāṟku māttāl
añcu marakkāl poṉ kuṭu[k*]ka kaṟpitta aḷavukku yiṉṉilam

peṟa kaṭavārākavum yippaṭikku uṭaiyavar aruḷicceyapaṭikku
nirampavaḻakiyāṉ kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu va ••••• koṭṭai
tiruvakatti uṭaiyāṉ pakka māṅkuṭi vayalil koṇṭu colai uruva

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1363 (1441 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land by Uyyāṇḍār Pāṇḍipperumal
of Māṅguḍi vēlar in Vaḍaveḷḷār alias Sundarapāṇḍya vaḷanāḍu to the deity
Aḻagiya-Maṇavāḻapperumāḷ of Arantāṅgi, probably for performing pūja in
the temple. Among the three signatories in the record only the name of
Kāliṅgarāyar is clearly mentioned.

l.ta:
aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār yilakkaṟaikku nam
vaṭaveḷḷāṟāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷaṉāṭṭu māṅku

ṭi veḷḷār uyyāṇṭār pāṇṭipperumāḷ pakkal koṇṭu kalveṭṭik
kuṭutta poṉṉaṉ vayakkal rūpāy

cuḷḷi nālumā marutañcey rūpāy orumā nampaṉ vayakkal rūpāy orumā
āka eṟṟac curukkam uṭpaṭa rūpāy

māvukkum yiṟaiyiliyāka kaḻittu kuṭuttu yiṉṉilattukku veṭṭimuṭṭi
viṉiyokam mākkalam eṟpeṟpaṭṭa camutta

ttikaḷukkum ṉāyaṉār tiruvakatticuramuṭaiyāṟku māttāl añcu
marakkāl nellukkuṭukka kaṟpittu kuṭutta a

ḷavukku yiṉṉilam añcumāvum īñcūraṇiyum ilakkappa cantirātittavarai
kāṇi āṭciyākap paṟṟi āḷa

peṟak kaṭavārākavum ippaṭi uṭaiyavar aruḷicceypaṭikku tiruccuṟṟaṉ
kāliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1364 (1442 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of the kāṇiyāṭchi of some land to a
Taṇṇāyaka-Toṇḍaimānār by Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷaperumāḷ Toṇḍaimāṉār of
Aṟantāngi. Nirambavaḻiyāṉ Kāliṅgarāyar is figured as signatory in the
inscription.

l.ta:
aṟintāṅki
aracu uṭaiyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār

toṇṭaimāṉāṟku vaṭaveḷḷāṟāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷaṉāṭṭu māṅkuṭi
veḷḷ[ā*]ḻaril piḷḷaiyār piṟavikku nallār pakkal koṇṭa marutañce

ivvūr tirucciṟṟampala muṭaiyār āvuṭaiyār pakkal koṇṭa
uṭaiyāṉ vaya[ka*]kal rūpāy orumā āka rūpāy ma orumā iṉṉila iraṇṭu māvaraikkum
yiṟaiyi

liyāka kaḻittu yiṉṉilattukku veṭṭimuṭṭi viṉiyoka mākkalam
eppeṟpaṭṭa camutta piṟāttikaḷukku ṉāyiṉār
tiruvaka[t*]ticura[mu*]ṭaiyāṟku māttāl
añcu marakkāl poṉ kuṭu[k*]ka kaṟpittu kuṭutta

yiṉṉilam
teṇṇāyakka toṇṭamāṉār cantirātittavarai kāṇiyāṭci āka paṟṟi
āḷakaṭavarākavum itukku

ṇaraiyu[m*] kapilaiyaiyum
koṉṟa pāvam peṟakkaṭavārākavum ippaṭi uṭaiyavar aruḷac
ceyaṟppaṭikku

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA [13]63 (1441 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of kāṇiyāṭchi of some land by of
Arantāṅgi Aḻagiya maṇavāḻapperumāḷ to a Meṉṉiṉṟaperumāḷ for performing
certain services in the same temple.

l.ta:
uṭaiyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār meṉṉiṉṟa
perumāḷukku nām vaṭaveḷḷāṟāṉa

cuntarapāṇṭiya[vaḷa*]ṉāṭṭu māṅkuṭi veḷḷāḷar pakkal koṇṭu
kalveṭṭi kuṭutta ariya piḷavaṉ periyaperumā[ḷ]

pakkal koṇṭa paṭṭaṉ vayakkal rūpāy orumā nālumā kaṇṇi vayakkal rūpāy orumā nālumā
tamiḻnāṭaṉ vayakkal rūpāy

edition.l:
rūpāy orumā a[nta]ri vayakkal rūpāy- 20 āka rūpāy- (1/4) 20 piḷḷaiyār piṟavikku
nallār pakkal koṇṭa vayakkal rūpāy orumānālumā

l.ta:
ūṟpotuvāṉa piḷḷaiyār
periyaperumāḷum ariya piḷḷai periyaperumā

ḷum uyyāṇṭār pāṇṭiperumāḷum tirucciṟṟampalam uṭaiyār
āḷḷuṭaiyārum ivarkaḷ uḷḷiṭṭār
pakkal koṇṭa tiruvakatti vayakkal rūpāy pacu piccivayakkal rūpāy orumā āka rūpāy

iṉṉilam patioru māmukkāṇiyum iṟaiyiliyāka kaḻittu kuṭuttu
iṉṉilattukku veṭṭimuṭ-

ṭu kaṭamai viṉipokammāka kalam eppeṟpaṭṭa camuttapiṟāttiyum kaḻittu
ṉāyanār tiruvakatticuramuṭaiya[r]

kku māttāl añcu marakkāl nellu kuṭukka kaṟpittu kuṭutta aḷavukku
imariyātiyile kuṭuttu yiṉṉilam

patuorumā mukk[ā*]ṇiyum meṉṉiṉṟa perumāḷ cantirātittavarai
kāṇiyācciyāka paṟṟi yaṉupavittup potakkaṭavarāka

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land as tirunāmattukāṇi to the
temple Tiruvagattiśuramuḍaiyār at Iṭṭiyūrttali by Tirunelvēliperumāḷ-
Toṇḍaimāṉār, the chief of Aṟantāṅgi. One of the signatories is mentioned
as Niramba-vaḻagiyār kāliṅgarāyaṉ.

l.ta:
aṟantāṅki araicu uṭaiyār
tirunelvelip perumāḷ toṇṭaimāṉār iṭṭiyūrttaḷi nāyaṉār
tiruvakatticuramuṭaiyārkku vayirāṇṭi veyal nāṉkellaiyum
tirunāmattukkāṇi āka kuṭuttamaikku itu

ṇaṉaiyum koṉṟa pāvattaip
peṟak kaṭavarākavum ippaṭi uṭaiyavar aruḷicceyalpaṭikku nirampa
vaḻakiyār kāliṅkaraiyar eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1364 (1442 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records that Aḻagiyamaṇavāḻapperumāḷ-Toṇḍai[māṉār]
the chief of Arantāṅgi in Iḷaṅgōnāḍu, a sub-division of
Sundarapāṇḍyavaḷanāḍu ordered that certain lands in Nāṭṭumāṅgalam
belonged to Mēṉṉiṉṟaperumāḷ Ātiśu[ra]rāma Toṇḍaimāṉār, to be made as
tax-free to the temple. The signatory is mentioned as Nirambavaḻagiyāṉ
Kāliṅgarāyaṉ.

l.ta:
ṟāṉa cuntirapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu iḷaṅkonāṭṭu aṟantāṅkiyaracu
uṭaiyār aḻakiya maṇavāḷa perumāḷ toṇṭai •••••

nayiṉār meṉṉiṉṟa perumāḷ aticū[ra*]rāmat toṇṭaimāṉāṟku
nāṭṭumaṅkalam ācākuṭi vayalil a • • •

mañcakarai vayalil oruvarum ovvātāṉkāṇi ūrār pakkal koṇṭa rūpāy
kurunta vayakka cāttiyapaṭi vaya

kkalum peṟṟā vayakkalum vayirāṇṭi vayalil tevatāṉa nikki niṉṟa nilam
nāṉkellai kkuṭpaṭṭa

nilattu rūpāy āka yiṉ nilattukku kaṭamai maṇveṭṭi muṭṭi uḷḷatu kaḻittu
kuṭutta aḷavukku yimmariyā
ti canṟātittavarai āḷakkaṭavārākavum itukku akitam paṇṇukiṟavarkaḷ

yum kapilaiyuṅ koṉṟa pāvattai peṟak kaṭavārākavum yippaṭikku
uṭaiyavar aruḷic ceyalpaṭṭikku

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1365 (1443 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the chief mentioned in No. 126 above who
remitted the taxes on certain lands, in favour of the temple of Nayiṉār
Tiruvagattīśuramuḍaiyār.

edition.l: satyam cakāttam 1365- ṉ meṟ cellā niṉṟa utiro

l.ta:
aṟantāṅki aracu uṭaiyār
aḻakiya maṇavāḷap perumāḷ toṇṭaimā • • •

• • nayaṉār meṉṉiṉṟa perumāḷ aticūrarāmat toṇṭaimāṉāṟku nām
vaṭaveḷḷāṟāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu

māṅkuṭiyil veḷḷāḻaril āripiḷḷai pakkal koṇṭa pāci uṭal rūpāy
cu(1/4) makkaṇayiṉā
koṇṭa avayuḷ uṭal rūpāy (1/4) periya perumāḷum piṟavikku nallārum
koṇṭa celvaṉ uṭal rūpāy (1/4) piṟavikku nallār

ṇṭa poṟkāṭaṉ uṭal rūpāy (1/4) [a]ntari uṭal rūpāy metaṉ
uṭal rūpāy cu

lattukku varum kaṭamai iṟaiyiliyāka kaḻittuk kuṭuttu veṭṭimuṭṭi
iṉiyokamākkala maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa

camuttapiṟāttikaḷukku nayiṉār tiruvakatticuramuṭaiyāṟku māttāl
añcumarakkāl poṉ kuṭu[k]ka kaṟpittu kuṭutta aḷavukku

iṉṉilamum periyaperumāḷ piṟavikkunallār ariyapiḷḷai
pāṇṭipperum āvuṭaiyār

in • • koṇṭa muṉṟum cantirātittavaraikku kāṇiāṭci
[ākapa]ṟṟi aṉupavittu potakkaṭavārākavum itu[kku]

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a field [mākalam] situated in
Miḻalaik-kūṟṟam, to the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at
Iṭṭiyūttaḷi by Tirunelvēlipperumāḷ Toṇḍaimāṉār.

l.ta: tiyati aṟantāṅki aracu uṭaiyavar tirunel • • lip perumāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME
MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 12 mā of land in Miḻalai nāḍu,
a sub-division of Vaḍaveḷḷāṟṟūr nāḍu by the chief of Arantāṅgi for
worshipping in the temple of Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār. It is signed
by Maṅgalarāyar of Māṅguḍi.

l.ta: ciṟukāla cantikku vaṭaveḷḷāṟṟu nāṭṭu miḻalaināṭṭu vayalil viṭṭa nilam

kku uḷpaṭṭa nilam tirun[ā]mattukkāṇiyāka cantirātittavaṟ cella
aṉupavikka

koṉṟa pāvattile p[o]v[ā*]ṉākavum ippaṭi uṭaiyavar
aruḷicceyaṟpaṭikku māṅkuṭi

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the image of Tiruvādavūr-tambirāṉār was
set up by the merchant group nālu nagarattār.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA INFRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land by Araṅguḷavaṉ alias
Paḻiyaṅgapperaiyāṉ of Nāṭṭimaṅgalam for conducting Śivarātri festival
besides burning a lamp in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1916.)
ETTIYĀTTALI, ARANTANGI TALUK,
TANJORE DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE ON THE WEST WALL OF THE OLD
AMMAṈ SHRINE IN THE SECOND PRĀKARA OF THE SAME
TEMPLE.
PĀṆḌYA KULAŚĒKHARA DĒ[VA]. 42ND YEAR (1310 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged and built in at the end. It mentions the taxes paid by the
śāliyas and Kaikkōḷa. The place name Ittiyūrtaḷi is also mentioned in it.

l.ta:
ṭayāṉeṉum iṭṭiyūrtaḷi ••••• kkum cāliyan kāttārkkum kaikke
••••• kalveṭṭi nāṭṭi kuṭutta paricāvatu •••••

araicupeṟu pala viṉipakaṅ •••āka •••oṉṟukku •••māka ••••• paṇam arai kuṭukka
kaṭavār[ka*]ḷākavum •••
vākavum ituvoḻiyac co •••••••••• ṭaṉ eṉṟu eḻuttu veṭṭi •••••

edition.p: Built in at the right end and damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1916.)
MĪÑJUR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VARADARĀJAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH, WEST AND
SOUTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PARAKĒŚARIVARMAN ALIAS RĀJĒNDRA CHŌḺA DĒVA-4TH
YEAR (1016 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 1080 kuḻi of land for
conducting festivals on the occasion of Aviṭṭam and Śripali-kalamuduvar in
the temple of Chōḻakulasundara-Viṉṉagarāḻvār at Mīñjūr in Nāyaru nāḍu, a
sub-division of Puḻarkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam by the Ūravar of
the same place.

l.ta:
koṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattup puḻaṟkoṭṭattu nāyaṟu nāṭṭu mi[ñ]cūr
ūrom emmūr coḻakula

cuntaraviṇṇakar āḷvārkkut tiṅkaṭoṟum aviṭṭat tiruviḻāvukku
veṇṭum cattukku nānūṟu

kuḻiyum ittevarkkey nāṭoṟum śrīpalikku kāḷamūtuvariruvarkkuk kuḻi
ainnūṟṟaimpatu

māka nipantam vaitta nilattukku kiḻpārkkellai
kāṭavaṭṭarkaḷiṟaippūmikku meṟkun teṉpārkkellai

ramuṭaiyār pūmikkum ivvuṣṇu śrīpalipaṭṭikku vaṭakkum
melpārkkellai
ḻaḷi lālavāya ••••• kuḻinūṟṟu muppatum āka kuḻi •••••

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1916.)
MĪÑJUR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VARADARĀJAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH, WALL OF
THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
MADHURĀNTAKA (?)-3rd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription partly in verse form refers to the gift of 1900 kuḻi
of land as Sālabhōga to the temple of Chōḻakulasundara Viṇṇagar. It also
mention a maṭha known as Mēlai-Chōḻakulattāyaṉ.

l.ta:
kālattu urom • kuḻi •••tiruviḻāp
puttukku vaitta ••••• coḻa kula cuntaravaṇṇā

ṭu • melakamuḷi iravi kulamāṇikkap peruceṟi uḷḷiru taṭi mā
••••• kumuṟuyaraṅkamaya itaṟku ā • ḷāttoṉ miḷāttomu maturāra •

ṟṟukku teṟku ••••• nokki vākkāṟu cetulaiya • tūravācema • • vatākacaḷūr
tirutevaṉ cella kuṭutte kuḻi

tom ūrom inṟum ittevar śrī paṇṭārattuvokkoṇṭu śrī
koyilil melai coḻakulattāyān maṭattu māccey viyattāṉ
vaṇṉalamikku

uttamākkiram kalam iraṇṭukku vaicca nilam āṟṟuvayappūlattu
kiḻpurankellai miñcūr kiḻavākaḷuyūrkku meṟkum tenpura • • vaṉa
tirukaikkte

ṟkellai āṟṟukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai varukap ••••• kellaikkum
paṟaippāka ka • • ṟṟu ellaikkum kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ittevar
tiruviḻāppuṟamāna iravikula māṇikka peruñ

ṟkum inānkellaikuḷḷum viṭṭa taṭipalaviṉāl kuḻi āyirattu
toḷḷāyiraṅ kuḻikum valai ••••• ṇṭu ca

ṟṟum ittevarkku ••••• viṇṇakaracceri kuḻi nūṟṟumuppatum akavātta
to • • tiru • teva • • nam kuḻi aiñnūṟum itanukkum kālukum teṟkil
tiruvaḻuti ••••• kuḻi

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1916.)
MĪÑJUR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VARADARĀJAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON BOTH SIDE OF THE
ENTRANCE INTO THE SAME SHRINE.
[PARAKĒŚA]RIVARMAN ALIAS RĀJĒNDRACHŌḺADĒVA,
(1012-44 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription is obliterated. Only the king's name Ko-Parakēsari Sri
Rājēndra Chōḷadēvar is mentioned in it.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1916.)
MĪÑJUR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VARADARĀJAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE ENTRANCE INTO
THE MAṆḌAPA INFRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE, LEFT SIDE
[PARAKĒŚA]RIVARMAN ALIAS CHAKRAVARTIN
VIKRAMACHŌḺADĒVA-12TH YEAR (1130 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription mentions the name of the village Nāyaṟu
in Nāyaṟu nāḍu a sub-division of Vikramachōḻavaḷanāḍu, which was a
district of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURTĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH, WALL OF
THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGŌPALA DĒVA 32ND
YEAR. 13TH CENTURY A.D. (1282 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 100 paṇam to the Śivabrāhmaṇa
Sēnāpati and Nārpatteṉṉāyira-baṭṭar for worshipping and offerings to the
image of Tiruppālaivaṉamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār installed by Sirukumūr-uḍaiyāṇ-
Tulaiyāda-selvaṉ in the temple Tirumaṇaṅgāvuḍaiya nāyaṇārkōyil at
Vallūr in Nāyaṟunāḍu, a sub-division of Puḻalkōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḻa
vaḷanāḍu in Jayagoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta: var pakkal kaikkoṇṭa aṉṉāṭu naṭakkum naṟ

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH AND WEST
WALLS OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of some voluntary contribution of
merchandise like paddy by the merchant assembly called ‘the palar of 56
desam’ for food offerings in the temple of Tirumaṇaṅgāvuḍaiya-Tambīrāṉār
at Vallūr for the merit of Rāchchaviṭṭi-nāyaka on the instructions given by
Tirumalai-nāyaka. They also agreed to bear certain expenditure connected
with the performance of festivities. They belonged to many places like
Chandiragiri, Tiruppati, Kālakasti, Maṇḍiyam, Vēlumpādi, Toṇḍaimāṉāṟṟūr,
Nāraṇapuram, Nagari, Āraṇi, Kottappāḷaiyam, Muttaram mēḍu, Chērambaḍi
Pūṇḍi, Koḷḷūr, Chōḻappattar, Chōḻapuram, Kōḍambākkam, Puliyūr, and
Mayilāppūr.

l.ta: intat taṉmam mātam mātam tappāmal inta mutalk
kavum inta potivakaiyil vanta tara • varuṣa •••••
veḷārkkaḷum māṉiyam me ••••• ṭṭārum oru upaiy
m oru upaiyamum viyāpārikaḷ oru upai
ñcikaḷum oru upaiyamum mutalikaḷil iraṇṭu upaiyamum
naṭatta kaṭavārākavum tirumalai nāyakkar vacca teṉṉa

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
SAME SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGŌPALADĒVA-5TH
YEAR (1255 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 100 paṇam to the Śivabrāhmaṇa
for preparing food offerings to the shrine of Tirukkāmakkōṭṭamuḍaiya-
Tiruvuḍai-nāchchiyār in the temple of Tirumaṇaṅga-araiya-nāyaṉār at Vallūr
of Nāyarunāḍu in Puḻarkōttam by two persons Arulālaṉ-vānavaṉ and
Arulālapperumāḷ-Tiruppālaivaṇamuḍaiyāṉ-Cheḻiyakōṉ.

l.ta:
puḻaṟkoṭṭattu nā[ya*]ṟunāṭṭu vallūr āḷuṭaiyār tirumaṇaṅkā araiya
nāyanā[r*] tirukkāmakkoṭṭamuṭaiya tiruvuṭainācciyāṟkku ivvūṟ
pākaṉ aruḷanen vāṉakāvāṉ aruḷḷāḷap

perumāḷ tirupp[ā*]lavaṉa muṭaiyāṉ ceḻiyakoṉ cantirātittavarai
celuttuvatāka vaitta amutupaṭikku nā[ḷ*] oṉṟukku kuṟuṇi nellu
cantirātittavarai amutupaṭi

ippaṇam nūṟum kaikkoṇṭu
cantirātittavarai celuttuvatāka ikkoyiṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya

ceṉāpatipaṭṭaṉ periya ••••• niṭṭārum ikkuṭi taḻuvakkuḻaintāṉ paṭṭa
avaiyam pukkāṉuḷḷiṭṭārum kau

•••••••••• ippaṇam nūṟum kaikkoṇṭom nāṟpatteṇṇāyira
caraṇappiḷḷaiyum ceṉavati ••••• periya nā • uḷḷiṭṭārum
taḻuvakkuḻaiñcāṉ avaiyam pukkāṉuḷḷiṭṭārum civañāṉa tevarum
ivvaṉaivom kaikkoṇṭom ivai paṉ

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST AND SOUTH
WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGŌPALA DĒVA-
3rdYEAR (1253 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. This inscription mentions the agreement of some
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple of Tirumaṇaṅgāvuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Vallūr of
Nāyarunāḍu of Puḻarkōṭṭam of Jayaṅgoṇḍa-chōḷa-maṇḍalam for maintaining
a perpetual lamp.

l.ta:
coḻamaṇṭalattup puḻaṟkoṭṭattu nāyaṟunāṭṭu vallūruṭaiyār
tirumaṇaṅkāvuṭaiya nāyanāṟku ivvūr ••••••••••

ttuc ceṉāpatipaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum ikkuṭi
taḻuvakkuḻaintāṉ paṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum ••••• nāṟpatteṉṉāyirapaṭṭaṉ
caraṇappiḷḷaiyum

vañāṉa
•••ṉum ivvaṉaivomittiri ṉuntāviḷakku oṉṟukku cantirātittavarai
••••• kaṭavomākavum celuttu •••••

yum ceṉāpatipaṭṭaṉum uḷḷiṭṭārum taḻuvakkuḻaintāṉpaṭṭaṉ
uḷḷiṭṭārum āḷumpirāṉpaṭṭaṉ civa • • ñaṉa tevaru ippaṭikku koyiṟ
karaṇattārom

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-15TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged Built in at the beginning. This inscription refers to a gift
of money which was entrusted with the Śivabrāhmaṇa Nārppatteṉṉāyira-
bhaṭṭan for the maintenance of the temple.

l.ta:
••••• ṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭi tevaṟkku yāṇṭu
patiṉañcāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa co

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. This inscription mentions Puḻarkōṭṭam alias
Vikramachōḻavaḷanāḍu in Nāyirunāḍu.

l.ta:
ḻa maṇṭalattupuḻaṟ koṭṭamāṉa vikkirama coḻavaḷanāṭṭu nāṭavarom
nāyaṟu nāṭṭu ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1916.)
MĒLŪR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SUGANDAPURIŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A SLAB LYING IN THE
COURTYARD OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
•••••••••• KALIYUGAM-48[1]7 (1716 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the construction of kitchen (maḍappaḷḷi) and
yāgaśālai before the shrine Tiruvuḍaitāyi-Ammaṉ in the temple complex
of Tirumaṇgīśvaram at Mēlaiyūr located to the west of Vallūr, built by
merchant Tummiṇikumāraṉ, the son of Tummuni, who belonged to the
Kaṇṇandai-mahārishigōtram of Periyapāḷaiyam.

l.ta:
melaiyūr tiruvaṇaṅ kāvalil tirumaṉaṅkīcuvarar tiruvuṭaittāyi ammaṉ
kovili

l viyāpārikaḷil periyapāḷaiyakkarttāril kaṉṉantai makāriṣi
kottiram pā-
ḷaiyam tummuṇikumāraṉ tummuṇi kaṭṭivicca yākacālai maṭappaḷi §

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1916.)
NĀYAR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
PUSHPARATHĒVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE.
VĪRAPRATĀPADĒVARĀYA-MAHARĀYA, ‘WHO LEVIED TRIBUTE
FROM ĪḺAM (CEYLON)’-ŚAKA-1360 (1438 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription mentions the gift of a lamp to the temple
of Pūtēri-Āṇḍār at Nāyar in Nāyaṟu nāḍu a sub-division of Puḻar kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
maṇṭalecura pakṣaikku muvarāyara kaṇṭa • • kaṇṭa
nāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu koṭātāṉ inturāyacurattāṉ iḻantiṟai
koṇṭa ••••• ṉ pūṟuva tekṣi[ṇa]
paccima uttara • • ṉ virapiratāpatevarāya mahārāya[ṟ*]ku cellāniṉṟa

āyirattu muṉṉūṟṟu aṟupatiṉmel cellā niṉṟa kuṟotaṉa

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1916.)
NĀYAR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
PUSHPARATHĒVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA
VĪRAPRATĀPA ACHYUTAIYADĒVA-MAHARĀYA,
ŚAKA-1448 (1533 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of the village Pūttēriviḷāgam to the
temple of Pūtteri Appaṉ by Nagamma-nāyaka, son of Poṉṉappa-nāyaka of
Kandaṉavoḷu (Kurnool), for the merit of the king Achchuda-Mahādēvarāyar
who bestowed the nāyakkattanam territory on the donor.

edition.l:
1448- ta[ṉ]mel cellāniṉṟa vicaya varuṣa mācimātam civarātri
puṇṇiya kālattile nā[ya*]ṟṟu pūte[ri*] appa[nuk]ku tantaṉma •••••

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1916.)
NĀYAR, PONNERI TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
PUSHPARATHĒVARA TEMPLE-ON A STONE IN THE RUINED
ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE SAME VILLAGE
JAṬAVARMAN TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARA PĀṆḌYA
DĒVA-14TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. Refers to the date as 14th year of the
reign king Tribhuvana Chakravattigal Sri Sundara Pāṇḍya devar and the
place Puḻar-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1916.)
KĪḺVĪDI, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
DHARMALIṄGEŚVARA TEMPLE-ON STONE BUILT INTO THE
MAṆḌAPA INFRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PARAKĒŚARIVA[RMAN], ‘WHO TOOK MADURAI AND ĪḺAM
(CEYLON).
inscription.p:
This is a fragmentary inscription which mentions the prasasti portion
of Chōḻa king.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1916.)
KĪḺVĪDI, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
DHARMALIṄGEŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A STONE SET UP NEAR
DHVAJASTAMBHA OF THE SAME TEMPLE
RĀSHTRAKŪTA KA[ṈṈRA] DĒVA-17TH YEAR (956).
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions the territorial division as Vallanāḍu of
Damarkōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1916.)
KĪḺVĪDI, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
DHARMALIṄGEŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE
BUND OF THE TANK IN THE SAME VILLAGE.
PARAKĒŚARIVARMAN, ‘WHO TOOK MADURAI AND ĪḺAM
(CEYLON)’-41ST YEAR (948 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the sale of land and the ērikāḍi tax by the
sabhā of Kīḻaivaḻi in Vallanāḍu a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1916.)
POYYAṈŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
AGASTYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE NORTH
WALL VAYIRAMĒGAVARMAṈ-20th YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription refers to some of the provision made for
preparing sacred food offerings to the temple of Tiruvagattiśvaramuḍaiya-
mahādēva by the sabha at Poy[gai]nallūr in Dāmaṟkōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1916.)
POYYAṈŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
PERUMĀL TEMPLE-ON A STONE SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL
OF PERUMĀL TEMPLE IN THE SAME VILLAGE
••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the gift of 1000 kuḻi of land for
preparing food offerings to the temples of Mahāvishṇu and Mahādēva.
Mentions Iṟalūr in Ayiṟkāḍu and Tiruvindaḷūr in Tiruvindaḷūr nāḍu, a sub-
division of Chōḻanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1916.)
KĪLPULAM, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
KAILĀSANĀTHA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH AND SOUTH WALL
VAYIRAMĒGAVARMAN-2ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of land by the ūrār of Palakalam to
five persons meant for beating Śegaṇḍi and for blowing trumpets on the
occasion of sribali in the temple of Tirukkūḷichcharattu Āḻvār at Palakalam
in Dāmar kōṭṭam, by Muḷḷikkuḍaiyāṉ Aditanāḷi. Also mentions the
signatory as Ilaṅgōvaṉ, son of Peruntachchaṉ.

l.ta: ttu āḷvārkku ivvūril muḷḷik kuṭaiyāṉ ātittaṉāḷi itte

ttuc ceytār ceyta pāvattup
paṭuvār ivai

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1916.)
KĪLPULAM, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
KAILĀSANĀTHA TEMPLE-ON TWO FRAGMENTS IN THE
COURTYARD OF THE SAME TEMPLE ••••• KĒSARIVARMAN . .
•••••
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription registers a gift of 144 sheep which were
neither old nor aged for burning a lamp in the temple by two person viz.,
Adīthaṉ and Maṉṉāḍi Poṉṉi and some others names are lost.

edition.l: ātittaṉ [ dara] ṉi vait

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE [RĀJĒNDRA CHŌḺA I] (1012-44).
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This inscription contains only a portion of the historical
introduction of the king Rājēndradēva I.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH AND WEST WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJĒNDRA CHŌḺA DĒVA (I)-20TH YEAR (1032 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers gift of 160 pon to the temple of
Kumbīśvaramuḍaiya mahādēva at Vikramaśiṅga chaturvēdimaṅgalam in
Dāmaṟnāḍu a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam.
Mentions a person Karambiseṭṭu Chōḻapirān, one of the āḷuṅgaṇattārs, as
signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST AND SOUTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
•••••••••• 26TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription mentions a number of persons Viz.
Kārambi-Cheṭṭau-chaṉṉaya krimavittaṉ, Chōḻapiran Kramavittaṉ
Vaṅgipuṟattu-Veṇṇaiyakkiramavittan, etc., were members of the sabhā of
Vikramaśinga-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN ALIAS UḌAIYĀR SRI-RĀJĒNDRADĒVA-
11TH YEAR (1063 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription, which consists of several bits, refers to the gift of
100 kuḻi tax free land measured in terms of Padināṟu Jāṉkōl, by
Govindakramavittar, one of the Samvatsaravāriyam members of the
assembly of Vikramaśiṅga-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, for conducting the festival
Āditya Perumbali ie., offering made on Sundays to the image of
Pāsupatamūrtigaḷ in the temple of Tirukkumbīśvaramuḍaiya-mahādēva.
Mentions the coin named Madurāntakadēvaṉ māḍai.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL OF THE
SAME SHRINE PARAKĒSARIVARMAN ALIAS
RĀJĒNDRACHŌḺADĒVA-5TH YEAR (1017 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 182 mākāṇi of land for the
supply of offerings and burning lamps in the temple of
Kumbīśvaramauḍaiya mahādēva at Pañjirai kaḷattūr alias Vikramaśiṅga
chatūrvēdimaṅgalam by the sabhā of the village. Also mentions a person
Kūttaṉ-tiruveṅgadadēvaṉ an officer (adhikāri) of Uḍaiyapiraṭṭīyār, who
supervised the gift. In addition to this a donation of 591 kuḻi of land was
given to a person for the supply of turmeric sacred water regularly to the
temple.

l.ta:
rāṭṭiyār tirumaṭaip paḷḷipuṟam jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa maṇṭalattu
tāmaṟkoṭṭattu tāmar nāṭṭup pāñcirai kaḷattūrāṉa
vikkiramaciṅkaccaturveti maṅkalattu ku

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
KAṈṈARADĒVA-2(7)TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of 25 kaḷañju of gold for burning
lamp in the temple of Tattēri mahādēva at Pañjiraikaḷattūr in
[Dāma]ṟkōṭṭam, by one of the [āḷuṅganat]tār of the village.

num eṟacivanum ivvaṉaivom koṇṭom entaṅ kūṟṟe pātinicati oru

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAJPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
VĪRABUKKAṆA UḌAIYĀR, DUNMATI (1381-82 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription mentions the gift of a lamp and food
offerings to the temple of Kumbīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Kaḷattūr alias
Kulaśēkharanallūr in Kaḷattūr nāḍu, a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta: ku cantiviḷakkum nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaikkum iru nāḻi

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
FIRST PRĀKĀRA OF THE SAME TEMPLE
••••• SAKA 1467 (1545-46).
inscription.p:
Damaged. Built in at the end. It refers to the gift of 120 kuḻi of
land by Kṛishṇarāya, resident of Saṅkaraṉbāḍi, a hamlet of Sōḻiṅgagpuram
in Karivitti nāḍu for performing pūja and neyvēdhya in the temple of
Agattīśuramuḍaiya nāyiṉār.

puṟupakṣattu pañca ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1916.)
KAḶATTŪR, ARKONAM TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
SUKANĀTHĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A PILLAR IN THE INNER
ENCLOSURE OF THE SAME TEMPLE SOUTHSIDE
•••••••••• 5TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 20 kalañju gold for burning a
lamp in the temple of Tattēri mahādēva of Kaḷattūr by Kāḍupaṭṭi
Kāraḷaiyuḍaiya Kaḍupaṭṭi-Vayiriyappar of Chō[ḻa]nāḍu in Paiyūr Iḷaṅgōṭṭam.
The money was entrusted with the assembly of Pañjiṟaikaḷattūr in Kaḍigai
nāḍu a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam along with the annual supervision
committee (Samvatsaravāriyam).

edition.l: tom sabhaiyom ita[ dharmmam]

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌNĒRIṈMAI[KOṆḌĀṈ]
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA-DĒVA-17TH YEAR. 13TH CENTURY A.D.
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription registers the king's gift of the village
Poygainallūr for the supply of food offerings to the temple of
Vijayarāghava perumāḷ at Tirtuppuṭkuḻi in Dāmaṟ nāḍu, a sub-division of
Dāmaṟkōṭṭam. Taxes like poṉvari, mukkai-sakkuk-kaḍamai, tariyirai, taṭṭār
bhāṭṭam, iṉavari and ērimīṉ pāṭṭam are mentioned.

[cuvāti] nakṣatarattu nāḷ koneriṉmai koṇṭāṉ ceya

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL
OF THE SAME SHRINE. MADHURĀNTAKA-POTTAPPICHCHŌḺAṈ
VIJAYAGAṆḌAGŌPĀLA DĒVA-15TH YEAR (1265 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of a land in the village
Vikramachōḻanallūr, (a hamlet) situated between Vaḍagarai vallanāḍu and
Teṉkarai Dāmal kōṭṭam for conducting festivals in the temple. It was
gifted after removing taxes like poṉvari, kudiraivari, sekku kaḍamai,
tariyiṟai, taṭṭārbhāṭṭam, iṉavari and ēri-mīṉbhāṭṭam.

puvanaccakkaravattikaḷil maturāntakap pottappic cOḻaṉ

tiruppati tiruppukkuḻi vijayarākavap perumāḷukku tirunāṭpaṭikkum palapaṇi
nimantattukkum tiruviṭai

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE•••••••••••
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAGAṆḌA [GŌPĀLA] DĒVA-4TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
The inscription refers to the gift of 33 cattle to the temple of
Tirupputkuḻi nāyaṉār by Kāḍavaṉ maṉaikaḻiyāmēśvara Nāyakkaṉ, a
native of Malaimaṇḍalam. The above gift was entrusted with the shepherd
of Kāvēripākkam to supply probably necessary ghee for burning lamp.

nāyakkaṉ vaitta tirunaṉtāviḷakku oṉṟukku pālpacu
pattum ciṉaipacu pattum polimaṟai nāku paṉṉiraṇṭum

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH BASE
OF THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of money for the supply of sacred
things to the temple of Pōrēṟṟu nayiṉār for worshiping on the occasion of
the Ēkādaśi days for the merit of a certain Veṅgappāpiḷḷai by Rāmayyaṉ,
the son of Tāmalmañjar, belonging to Kāsyappagōtra of Muḍippaṇi Agaram.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL
OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the installation of the images Perumāḷ and
Nāchchiyār in the temple, by the Gōmasaṭhanaruḷalap perumāḷ alias
Tyagapperumāḷ of Māmaṉūr in Pōśaḷa rājya.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription records that a certain Nandambākkiḻāṉ Dēvapperumāḷ
Śaraṇadēvaṉ of Nandambākkam in Pērūr nāḍu a sub-division of Puliyūr
kōṭṭam set up the image of Tirumaṅgai Āḻvār in this temple.

l.ta: ttu nantam pākkiḻāṉ tevapperumāḷ caraṇa tevaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
KṚISHṆADĒVAMAHĀRĀYA SON OF NARAŚIṄGARĀYA,
ŚAKA 1437 (1515 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It mentions the place name
Tiruppuṭkuḻi in Dāmar nāḍu, a sub-division of Dāmarkōṭṭam in
Jayaṅgoṇḍamaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
ṇṭalecura metiṉ micurakaṇṭakaṭāri
cāḷuvacāḷuva naraciṅkaya kumāra

inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the gift of land viz., Nisanavantāṅgal for
making offerings at the temple of Vijayarāghava, on the occasion of the
Māśi festival, by a certain Mēnkaḷa Tirumala-somayājin.

·aṇnayyaṁgāru

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift made to the same temple and to
brāhmaṇās by the same donor mentioned in No. 170.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VĪRAPRATĀPADĒVARĀYAMAHĀRĀYA, SADHĀRAṆA (1430-31 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of 1310 paṇam by the king while he
was at the temple of the Śri Virupākshadēva located on the banks of the
Pampā river. His order was sent to Sirigirināyan at Chandragiri. The gift
was meant for the supply of food offerings, conducting festivals and
repair works to the temple of Porēṟṟuperumāḷ at Tiruppuṭkuḻi in Dāmaṟ
kōṭṭam, in Chandragiri rājya. Śrī Virupāksha is engraved in Telugu at the
end of the record.

l.ta: śrī man irājātirājaṉ irāyaparamecuraṉ śrī vira
rumāḷukku cantirakiri paṇṭārattukku cellum coṭivarākaṉ poṉ
nūṟṟu muppattu toṉṟum paṇam āṟekālum ittile poreṟṟup-
perumāḷukku nāḷḷoṉṟukku nālu taḷikai tiruppanākamum tiruna •••••
kku viṇṇappañ ceykaiyil nām tiruvuḷḷam paṟṟi
yirattu munnūṟṟu oru pattu āṟekāl paṇamum koṇṭu nammuṭaiya śrī-
nāmattāle poreṟṟup perumāḷ nāḷ oṉṟukku nālu taḷikai tirup
panākamum tirunāḷum tiruppaṇiyum naṭakkum[pa*]ṭi tārai iṟaittu
unakku uttāramum tiruvuḷḷam paṟṟiṉom inta coṭip paṇam ā

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
DĒVARĀYA-MAHĀRĀYA.
inscription.p:
This inscription is may the continuation of the above inscription and
mentions the confirmation of the gift by the officer Sirigirinātan.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH AND
EAST WALL OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1563 (1641-42 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the partitioning of the total lands at
Tirupukkuḻi into thirty-six shares for performing puja in the temple (karai)
at the instance of Puṉyakōḍi-ayyaṉ who was the patron of Kumāra
Tirumalai Tatāchārya of Eṭṭūr. Aḻagiya Poreṟṟeṉa Kumaraṉ-Paramānandan is
mentioned as the accountant.

inta kalveṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
SADĀŚIVADĒVA-MAHĀRĀYA, SON OF ŚRIRAṄGAPPAYADĒVA-
MAHĀRĀYA-ŚAKA 1495 (1573 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the images of Āḻvārs, which had been
destroyed by the Muhammadans were re-installed by Appalammanavargaḷ a
member of the palace of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Veṅgalaiyya in the temple
at Tiruppuṭkuḻi.

edition.l:
śubhamastu śrīman makāmaṇṭalecuran rācātirāca paramecuran
śrīṟaṅkappayateva makārāyar kumārar catācivateva makārāyar
piṟativirācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷāniṉṟa cakāttam 1495- ṉ mel
cellāniṉṟa śrīmuka ca[ṅ]vassarattut tulā

l.ta:
nāyiṟṟup pūṟuvapaṣṣattu ēkāteciyum putavāramum peṟṟa ṉāḷ śrī man
makāmaṇṭalecuran kācipa kottirattuc cillēṭu veṅkaḷaiyyateva
makārācāviniṭaiya araimaṉaiyar appalammanavarkaḷ muṉṉāḷ tiruppati
tiruppuṭkuḻiyile

eḻuntaruḷiyirunta āḻvārkaḷ tulukkar āvaṉiyile pokayil putitāka
appalammanavarkaḷ taṅkaḷ upayamāka eḻuntaruḷap paṇṇina āḻvārkaḷ
poykaiyār pūtattār peyāḻvār tirumiḻicaiyār maturakaviyār • •

periya āḻvār toṇṭaraṭippoṭiyār tiruppāṇāḻvār kulacekarar āka
āḻvārkaḷ oṉpatimarum muṉṉāḷ nammāḻvār tirumaṅkaiyāḻvār
cūṭikkuṭutta nācciyar yinta muṉṟu peṟkum tiruppaṇikku • • kkuṭutta

kaḷ piṟatiṭṭaikku ceṉṟa ceṉṟapoṉṉum yinta yiraṇṭu vakaiyum
appalammaṉukkum puṇṇiyamāy mukintitu koyiṟkaṇakku vaṭamalai
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL
OF THE FIRST PRAKĀRA OF THE SAME TEMPLE
ŚAḌAGŌPAVARMAṈ ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-13TH YEAR [13TH CENTURY A.D.]
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of land with all taxes at
Tayirpākkam, a hamlet of Dāmar, for the supply of food offerings to the
Āḻvārs setup in the temple by Varandarum-perumāḷ-Eḍuttakai-Aḻagiyāṉ alias
Pallavarāyaṉ of Tuñjalūr in Tirumaḻalaikkūṟṟanāḍu.

l.gr:
svastiśrī madāmagrāmapikratrama saripurvvapatigrāma bhūsam
paścimaṇḍapākam pisuyāsimnasti • •

subhagaḥ kṣirāpagāda kṣi • svāmibhyōdadhipāka samjñamadiśatsvāmi
pallavādhiśva

l.ta: tiyaiyum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ tāmaṟ

koṭṭattu tāmaṟnāṭṭu tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻiyil nāṉ eḻuntaruḷivitta
āḻvārkaḷḷukkut tirumaḻalaik kūṟanāṭṭut tuñcalūr uṭaiyaṉ varan

tarum perumāḷ eḻuttakai aḻakiyāṉāṉa pallavarāyaṉ innāṭṭut tāmaṟ
piṭākai tayir pākkam pālāṟṟukkut teṟku vati • ellaikku me

ṟku muṭṭaippākkat tellaikku vaṭakkut tayirppākkattu nīrvarukiṟa
kāl muṭakkukkum ikkoyil • ḻivanta perumāḷ tiruviṭai āṭṭa

māṉa muppatu vaṭṭikkum kiḻakku • naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilattu naṉcey puṉcey
nilattu iṟaiyili tevatāṉa tiruviṭai āṭṭaṅkaḷ nīkki uḷḷa ni

laṅkaḷil kaṭamai poṉvari āyam puṟakkalaṉai uḷpaṭṭa aṉaittu
varikaḷum uṭpaṭa

yile paṉṉireṇṭu taḷikai tiruppoṉakamum irāpaṉṉiraṇṭu
taḷikai tiruppoṉakamum āka irupattu nālu taḷikaiku nāḷ va

ṭṭam kala ariciyum kariveñcaṉaṅkaḷḷukkum cāttuppaṭikkum
viḷakkukkum tiruppari caṭṭaṅkaḷukkum tirumāvaṭi

var
tirumukappaṭiye kallilum cempilum veṭṭi kal

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARĀGHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGŌPĀLA DĒVA-
20TH YEAR (1270 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sarvamānya grant of a land in
Dāmaṟkōṭṭam by Madhurāntaka pottappichchōḻaṉ Vijayagaṇḍagōpālaṉ for
the supply of food offerings to the temple of Vijayarāghava Perumāḷ.
Taxes like taṟiyirai, taṭṭārppāṭṭam, iṉavari and ērimīṉpāṭṭam are mentioned.

l.ta:
tiyaiyum caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
mulattu nāḷ maturāntaka

jeyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu tāmaṟkoṭṭattu tāmaṟ nāṭṭu tiruppati
tiruppuṭkuḻi vicaiyarākavap perumāḷukku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭikkum
palapaṇi nām nicayittu

makiḻpāṟkellai kūrattār elaikkum ciṟuniṉṟaiyāl vaṭakku meṟkum
teṉpāṟkellai pokala muṭṭaiyākkattāṉ ellaikku vaṭakkum
melpāṟkellai

vatayūrāṉa ellaikkut teṟkum iṉṉālpāṟkellaikku naṭuuṭpaṭṭa
nirnilamum kollainilamum ivvāṇṭai āṭimātam mutal kaṭamaiyum
āyamum kācu kaṭa

mai taṟiyiṟai taṭṭārppāṭṭam iṉavari erimiṉpāṭṭam maṟṟu epperppaṭṭa
aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum uṭpaṭa

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKJUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAGAṆḌAGOPĀLA DĒVA-2ND
YEAR.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription records the gift, by the king, of the
village Kaṭṭaṉūr as Tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam by the king to the temple of
Vijayarāghava Perumāḷ at Tiruppuṭkuḻi. Also some lands were granted to
temple of Kailāśamuḍaiya nāyaṉār in the same village.

l.ta: tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī virakaṇṭakopāla tevaṟku yāṇṭu

vap perumāḷukkut
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka iṭṭa kaṭṭanūrkku tirumukappaṭi uḷḷūril uṭaiyār
śrī kailācamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku vaṭakaḻaniyil ampatiṉ kala varicaippaṟṟil
tevatāṉa • • •

m
ivvūril viṉāyakap piḷḷaiyār tevatāṉam naṉceyi kuḻi

edition.l:
m kuḻi 179 m ivaruṭaiya koyil nilam kuḻi 350 m piṭāripaṭṭi kuḻi
295- m ittevi kollai kuḻi 50- m āka palataṉi
tevatāṉaṅkaḷāl naṉcey puṉcey uṭpaṭa niṅkum kuḻi 9249
ikkuḻi oṉpatiṉāyiratti ••••••••••

l.ta:
ṉṟu nikki paḻavakāttu ellaikkum puttakarattu ellaikkuk kiḻ
perellaikku vaṭakkum a[ppi] akaratārellaikku meṟkum
pūttoṇṭār paṭṭār ellaikkum oṭṭa pākkattārellaikku vaṭakkum
innāṟpa •••••

•••cay puṉceyuṭpaṭṭa eppeṟpaṭṭa kaṭamai poṉvari aṉaiccāyaṅkaḷum
uṭpaṭa inta perumāḷ vijayarākavap perumāṉukke tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka
viṭṭeṉ virakaṇṭa kopāla •••••

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VĪRAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-2ND
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift by king, of 3 vēli of land by
Nuḷappiyāṟukiḻāṉ Vāḷavaṇḍāṉ Tiruvēṅgaḍa Saraṇālayaṉ to a dancing girl
Aḍichchēripeṇḍi Bukkavaḷḷi, the daughter of Padiṉeṉbhūmi Māṉikkam for
their maintenance of her generation together.

l.ta:
vatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut tāmaṟkoṭṭattut
tāmarnāṭṭut tiruppati

tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār vicaiyarākavap perumāḷ ceṉainātaṉ olai nam
aṭiccerip peṇpayyakaḷil aḻakapperumāḷāṉa patiṉeṉpūmi māṇi

kkattiṉ makaḷ āḻvārāṉa pukkatuṟai valli māṇikkattukku nām
cantāṉaccāmam kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu nam vijayarā

cāttuppaṭikku nuḷappiyāṟu kiḻāṉ vāḻavantāṉ tiruveṅkaṭa caraṇālaiya
maṉaiyiṭuvittu nam virakaṇṭa kopālaṉai veṇṭik koṇṭu tam

māka tirunāḷ eḻuntaruḷukiṟa
ivaṟku intak kaṭṭaṉūrait tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka muṉpe uṅkaḷ
tiruttavappa

kam paṇṇiṉār ummuṭaiya
tirumukaṅkuṭuttaruḷa veṇumeṉṟu vāṅkuvittut tantu ikkaṭṭaṉūrait
tiru

viṭaiyāṭṭamāka [vi]ṭuvittapaṭiyāle ivaṉ cantāṉa cantāṉa
mu[ḷḷataṉaikkun] nāḷoṉṟukkuk kalamāka nellu munnūṟṟu aṟupatiṉ
kalatti

le aṟupatiṉ kala nellukku aiñcil iraṇṭuppaṭi nāḷoṉṟukku
aṟunāḻi
mum nūṟṟukkala nellu māta viḻukkāṭṭile peṟavu

m nikki nellu irunūṟṟuk kalattukkum ivvūrile mūṉṟu velinilam
iṭuvittuk koṇṭu innilam neṟpayiṟ ceytu kaṭamai koṇṭu nik-

ki ūrvarikaḷum poṉvari [pu]ṟakkalaṉaiyum maṟṟum epperpaṭṭaṉavum
āmaiñciyumakappaṭa ūropāti ceytu ikkoyilait tāṉ cantāṉa
cantā

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-18TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a previous gift of lands located on the way
between Kanchipuram to Uttiramērūr for setting up agrahāram as well as
lands donated as bhaṭṭavirutti to Mādhavabhaṭṭaṉ by Perumānāyakkar
during the reign period of Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla. It is said that the
brāhmaṇas of the village sold those lands for paying tax arrears.

l.ta: tiripuvaṉa cakkaravattikaḷ śrī cuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu

mārkaḻi mācam irupatteḻāntiyati nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu
tāmark koṭṭattu tāmal nāṭṭut tiruppati tiruccittirameḻi
tiruppukkuḻināyaṉār vijayarā

m
paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu nāṅkaḷ vijayakaṇṭakopāla tevaṟku

akaramākat tanta nilam kāñcīpurattukkup pokiṟa peruvaḻikku uttaramerūr
mātavapaṭṭaṉukku nāyakkar paṭṭavirutti viṭṭaniḷamuṭpaṭa mucaṟupākkat
tellaikku vaṭakkum kaṭavaḷūr ellaikkuk kiḻakkum veṅkaṭapāṭi
ellaikkuk kiḻakkum tāmartūṉi kāṭṭuppaṭṭi ellaikkut teṟkum

kaṭṭaṉūr paḻavakaramāṉa ammaiyappa caruppetimaṅkalattu ellaikkut
teṟkum ivvūr ēriuṭainta maṭuviṟkālukku vaṭakkum

puṉṉaiccerivatikku meṟkum inta ellaikkuṭpaṭṭa naṉcey puṉcey
nilattu āṟṟaṅkaraiy tevatāṉam kuḻi

kaḷum poreṟṟukku
eṅkaḷuṭaṉ eḻutivuṭaiya paṅku oṉṟum nikki eṅkaḷ paṅku

itil
kāṇiyāḷar paṅku mūṉṟum tiruppukkuḻip paṭṭarkku perumāṇā

ṇaruṭaṉ kuṭutta paṅku oṉṟum • ṅkaḷ paṅku
pattoṉpataraiyum āka paṅku

ṉāyum iṟṟai nāḷile eṅkaḷukku
rājākkaḷ rājamāy vakuppirukka māṭṭāta paṭiyāle viṟpomāka
niṉaittom koḷvīruḷirō eṉṉa

reṟṟiṉa śrī paṇṭārattu tiruvāparaṇam viṟṟup paṅkoṉṉukku vācipaṭar
varākaṉ paṇanūṟākak kaikkoṇṭa paṇam

ippaṇam
iraṇṭāyirattu muṉṉūṟṟu ai[mpatum] kaikkoṇṭu akarattu naṉcey
puṉcey ērikkāl āṟṟukkāl meṉokkiṉa maram
kīḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟu maṟṟuṅ kaṉṟunilai pāḻ uṭpaṭa epperppaṭṭaṉavum

kaḷum uṭpaṭa tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka
poreṟṟiṉāṉ tirukkaimalarile utakam paṇṇi piramāṉamum paṇṇi
eṅkaḷ irupattu mūṉṟaṟaip pereḻuttum akaraṅkaḷil

ṇarum
ivvaṉaivom § iṟṟai nāḷilē ippaṭikkaṇṭu eḻuttu veṭṭiṉeṉ
taṇṭaka nāṭṭu poruḷācāriyaṉeṉ eḻuttu camantakaḷattūruṭaiyāṉ
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the nāḍavar of Dāmal-nāḍu, who had
assembled in the temple of Dāmal declared the grant of lands in the
village as tax-free Tirunāmattukkāṇi for the maintanance and repair works
in the temple of Pōrēṟṟu. It also mentions Nāṭṭukkaṇakkaṉ Tiruvāṇḍai as
signatory.

l.ta:
jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut tāmaṟkoṭṭattut tāmal
nāṭavar poraṟṟukku mutaliṭaṅka iṟaiyili āka viṭṭa puttakarattukku
nāṭāka tāmar ampalattile niṟaivaṟa niṟaiñcu kuṟaivaṟakkūṭi iruntu
āṭi amāva

ṉattukku kalluveṭṭac
coṉṉapaṭi tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattārkku ciṟumuṟi
pattoṉpatāvatu āṭi mā

mutal poṟeṟṟiṉ tirunāmattukkāṇi
kaṭṭaṉūril puttakaramum tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka koḷkaiyil ivvūr
innāḷ mutal nāṟpāṟ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa naṉcey

kaḷum tiruppaṇikkum
tiruvārātaṉattukkum mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyili āka viṭṭatu ippaṭikku inta
olaiye piṭipāṭākak koṇṭu kallilum cempilum veṭṭi

ttavarai
naṭattik koḷvate § ivai nāṭṭuk kaṇakkaṉ tiruvāṇṭai eḻuttu §
ippaṭi kaṇṭu eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL,
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-4TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to an agreement made by the great assembly
of Kaṭṭaṉūr alias Uttamachōḻachaturvēdimaṅgalam which was a
tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam of the temple Sittiramēḻi-Tiruppuṭkuḻi-nāyaṉār in Dāmaṟ
nāḍu, a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam that they should not mortgage any
lands of that village.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vicaiyakaṇṭakopāla tevaṟku
yāṇṭu

nāṭṭu tiruppati cittirameḻit tiruppuṭkuḻi n[āya]ṉār koyil śrī
paṇṭārattārkku ināyaṉār tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kaṭṭaṉūrāṉa uttamacoḻa
catu

aiyāro[m*] icaivutiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu ivvāṇṭait
tai mātavarai ivvūr eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa nāṟpāṟ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa

ṉaṅkaḷum paṇṇak
kaṭavomalla śrī paṇṭārattārai aṟivittu śrī paṇṭārattile
kuṭukkumatoḻiya viṟkakkaṭavom eṉṟu

piṟantatuṇṭākil nāyaṉār āṇai maṟuttār paṭṭatum pāṭu camaiya
teṇṭamum paṭakkaṭavom ivvūr

aikkuc camainta ūrkaṇakku tattaṉūruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu || ippaṭi
aṟiveṉ cittirameḻic camaiyakaṇakkup pecum perumānāṉa taṇṭaka
nāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL,
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIKRAMPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-8TH YEAR
(13TH CENTURY A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the gift of the village Paḍuvūr alias
Vīrachampanallūr in Paḍuvūr kōṭṭam to the temple of Emberumāṉ
Vijayarāghava perumāḷ, at Tiruppuṭkuḻi for celebrating the festival of Āvaṇi
Tirunāḷ and conducting the service of Vīrachampaṉśandi, (which was
instituted) by one Sambukulōttuṅga-sambuvarāyaṇ Vīrachampaṉ in the
temple on the constellation of Ayiliyam which was his natal star.

l.gr:
svastiśrī grāmādāmu taṇṭalāt prathamadaśyāham gukanāmataḥ pratyaka
daśyadhisa • • tāthanaddaśyā khēgavatyāstatiyāmyā yāndaśi bhura • •
caḷava

l.ta:
tiyaiyum
veḷḷikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa āyiliyattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattup paṭuvūṟ

koṭṭattup paṭuvūrāṉa viracampanallūr kiḻpāṟkellai • ṉikaḷ
taṇṭalattār ellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai vaḷavanallūr
ellaikku vaṭakkum

melpāṟkellai peruṅkāyanallūr ellaikku kiḻakkum
vaṭapāṟkellai veḵ kāvāṟṟukku teṟkum āka inta nāṟpāṟ
kellaikkum naṭuviṭpaṭṭa nilattil iṟai

yili tevatāṉam tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam paḷḷiccantam muḷḷiṭṭa nilaṅkaḷ
nikki nikkiyuḷḷa naṉcey puṉcey nilattu kaṭamaiāyam poṉvari
kutiraivari pala kāṇikkai puṟakka

laṉai taṟiiṟai taṭṭārpāṭṭam cekkukaṭamai iṉavari erimiṉpāṭṭam
iruntu parimāṟuvār periṟkaṭamai maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭa aṉaittu varikaḷum
inta toṇ

ṭai maṇṭalattut tāmaṟkoṭṭattut tāmarnāṭṭut tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi
emperumāṉ vijayarā

māka kaṇṭa āvaṇittirunāḷukku tirunāḷ paṭikkum nāṅkaṇṭa
viracāmpaṉ

kku amutupaṭi kala aricikkum itukku veṇṭum
ciṟiviñcaṉaṅkaḷukkum tiruppali

tta varaikkum
celvatākac cilā lekaiyum tā

l.gr:
campaścampū kulōttuṅga ssārvvabhauma
sabhāpatiḥ bhrātu bhissa

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-8TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of the village Oṭṭaittāṅgal alias
Pōreṟṟi to the temple of Vijayarāghava perumāḷ at Tiruppuṭkuḻi in
Dāmarkōṭṭam, a subdivision of Toṇḍaimaṇḍalam in accordance with a
previous grant made by Rājarāja Sambuvarāyaṉ.

l.ta:
koneriṉmai koṇṭāṉ toṇṭaimaṇṭalattu tāmar
koṭṭattu tiruppuṭkuḻiyil vijayarākavap peru

māḷ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam oṭṭaittāṅkal āṉa poreṟṟiṉatāṅkal
ūravarkku muṉpi lāṇṭukaḷḷum irācarāca campuvarā

yar ōlaippaṭi innāyaṉārkku tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāy pontapaṭiye eṭṭāvatu
mutalakaramum ivvūr nārpāṟ kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa

naṉcey puṉceyyum itukku irukkuṅ kaṭamai antarāyam uḷḷiṭṭa
aṉaittāyavarkkamum uṭpaṭa mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyili ākak ku

ṭuttom ippaṭikku ivōlai piṭipāṭāka koṇṭu muṉpi lāṇṭukaḷum
iruntu pontapaṭiye tiruppati śrī vayi

ṣṇavarkaḷḷuk kokaṭamai kuṭimaipokka ivai yātavarāyaṉ eḻuttu
ivai vaḻutiyat taraiyaṉ eḻuttu ivai mātavarāyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
JAṬĀVARMAN TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA
DĒVA-17TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land purchased at Puttagaram
alias Svāmisantosh chatūrvēdimaṅgaḷam a hamlet of Kaṭṭaṉūr, and made as
tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple of Śittiramēḻi Tiruppuṭkuḻi in Dāmaṟ nāḍu,
a sub-division of Dāmaṟkōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-chōḻamaṇḍalam by Aṇantaṉ
Pōrēru and brothers of the same village. Signatory Taṇḍaganāṭṭup-
perarutāchāriyam is mentioned.

l.ta:
caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa catayattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut
tāmaṟ koṭṭattut tāmarnāṭṭut tiruppati cittirameḻit tiruppuṭkuḻi
nāyanār

koyil śrī paṇṭārattukkuk kaṭavārkku immaṇṭalattu ittoṭṭattu
inta tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kaṭṭaṉūr pirinta puttakaram

maṅkalattu • ṟṟu aṉantaṉ poreṟum tampimārum pakkal
iṟṟai nāḷil paṇam nūṟṟu muppatu vilaiyākak koṇṭa paṅ

kāy ippaṅkil vilaivikkattūkka muḷḷatuṇṭākilum eṅkaḷ
tamappaṉārkku[n]ṉākiṟa teṉṟu piccaṉ allāḷapperumāḷukku
kuṭuttapaṇam nūṟṟai

m irupatāl vantatum menteṉ
oṉṟu poreṟṟiṉa

ttavarai tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka cellakkaṭavatāṉmai || ivai
taṇṭakanāṭṭup poruḷācāriyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLA.
inscription.p: This inscription mention the king name as Śri Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF
THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌ[YA DĒVA]-18TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sale of land as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to the
temple by the mahāsabhā of Kaṭṭanūr alias Uttamachōḻa-
chatūrvēdimaṅgaḷam.

l.ta:
puraṭṭāti mātattu orunāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu
tāmarkkoṭṭattu tāmar nāṭṭu tiruppati cittirameḻi tiruppuṭkuḻi
nayiṉār vijayarākavap perumāḷ koyil śrī paṇṭārattāṟku
iṉnāyaṉār tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kaṭṭaṉūrāṉa uttamacoḻac catu

kāṇivilaip piramāṇam paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa
•••••••••• kaṭṭaṉūrāṉa uttamacoḻa caturvetimaṅkalattu attikkuṟi
aruḷāḷapaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār paṅku oṉṟaraiyum ikkuṭi varatarāja paṭṭaṉ
uḷḷiṭṭār paṅku oṉṟaraiyum āka paṅku mūṉṟil varatarājapaṭṭaṉ
uḷḷiṭṭār pātiyāṉa paṅku oṉṟaraiyil āṟava • •

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-5TH YEAR
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sale of land for 1300 paṇam which was
sent to the temple by some brāhamana individuals.
