inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-18TH
YEAR.
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This is a counterpart of the above inscription and refers
to several shares of land in the village given to the temple, by the
assembly of Kaṭṭanūr alias Uttamachōḻachatūrvēdimaṅgaḷam.

l.ta:
puraṭṭāti mātattoru nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu
tāmaṟkoṭṭattu tāmaṟnāṭṭuc ci

vap perumāḷ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kaṭṭaṉūrāṉa uttamacoḻac
catu

innāyaṉār śrī paṇṭārattārkku eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa ikkaṭṭaṉūril
vara

mukkālil kuṇṭūr nārāyaṇapaṭṭaṉ akamuṭaiyāḷ āyiṉa ••••• yum
tiruppuṭkuḻiyum

ṉamāka kuṭuttapaṅku kāl nīkki nīkki paṅku araiyil
muṉi

iḷaiyārutumāy ivarkaḷil tiruvaṭiccānta varantaruvāropātiyum
iḷaiyār opātiyum uṭpaṭa ippoṟeṟṟiṉa pakkaḷ paṇam nūṟṟai

mpatu kuṭuttuk koṇṭa paṅku kālum tiruppuṭkuḻiyum āḻvārum
tāmarile cila veḷḷāḷarkku taṅkaḷ opāti viṟṟomeṉṟu piramāṇam
paṇṇikkuṭuttu ciṟitu paṇamum paṟṟi avarkaḷum maṟittuppokaiyil
tāmaṟ ūravar namporeṟṟiṉ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭa ūrumāy namuṭaiya
tiruvārātaṉamumāṉa nāṭṭukkoyilil tiruviṭaiyāṭṭa ūrile

ḷḷakkaṭavomalla avarkaḷ koṇṭupoṉa paṇattai śrī paṇṭārāttār
tantu tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka pirāmāṇam paṇṇik koḷḷuṅko
ḷeṉkaiyil ittiruṭpukkuḻi makaṉ varantaruvārai iṭuvittu piramāṇam
eḻuti eḻuttum iṭuvittu meleḻuttum tāṅkaḷe iṭṭut tirukkaiyil
inta tiruppuṭkuḻi aṅ

ttukku vilaippaṭi paṇattil tāmarūravaṟku
kuṭuttapaṇam eḻu

nūṟṟaimpatum kuṭuttukkoṇṭa paṅku kālum āṟāvamutupaṭṭaṉ aṅ

piḷḷai varataṉ opāti paṅku kālum ivaṉ a

vattil ivaṉ
akamuṭaiyāḷ āṇṭavaṉ cāṉimuṉi makaṉ poṟeṟṟai mutukaṇṇāka
koṇṭu piramāṇam paṇṇik ku

akamuṭaiyāḷ varataṉ kāṇiviṟṟuk koṇṭa paṇamāyk kuṭutta paṇam

paṅku araiyil muṉṉāḷ tāmarūṟavar

ku āḻvārum tiruppuṭkuḻiyum piramāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttuk koṇṭa
paṇattil opāti paṇam eḻupattaiñcum tāmarūravarkkuk kuṭukka
veṇṭukaiyil ivaṉ makaṉ tirukkuṟukaippirāṉai iṭuvittu
cīpaṇṭārattile paṇam vāṅkik kuṭuppittup piramāṇam paṇṇik kuṭutta
paṅku araikkāl nīkki paṅku kāle araikkālil āḻvār opāti paṅku
mūṉṟu mā mukkāṇiyum

ivaṉ makaṉ tirukkuṟukaipirāṉ piramāṇam paṇṇittarak kuṭutta paṇam
nūṟṟerupattaiñcum āka utaka

utaka

periyakoyil nampikku vāṭkaipaṭukaiyil ivarukku

kkuk koṇṭa paṅku araiyum attikku[ṭi] āṇṭāṉum ciṅkarum

perumāḷ uḷḷiṭṭār

ṉaṅ kuṭutta paṇam irunūṟum

cipaṇṭārattārkku iṉṉāyaṉār tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kaṭṭanūrāṉa
uttamacoḻacatu

yom ippaṭikku
allāḷapaṭṭaṉ periyaperumāḷ eḻuttu ippaṭikki

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLA DĒVA-6TH
YEAR (1256 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 32 cows for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Vijayarāghava Perumāḷ by Sambuvarāyar-Vīrachōḻaṉ.
Signatory Kōyirkaṇakku Tiruppuṭ-kuḻipiriyaṉ is mentioned in the inscription.

edition.l:
svastiśrī tribhuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vicaiyakaṇṭagōpāla dēvaṟku
yāṇṭu 16- vatu ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu

l.ta:
tāmaṟkoṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭut tiruppati tiruppukkuḻi nāyaṉār vicaiya
rākavap perumāḷukku nāyaṉār campu

edition.l:
varāyar viracoḻaṉ tirununtāviḷakkukku viṭṭa pacu 32 ṛṣapam 1- āka
uru 33- m koṇṭu centirātitta

l.ta:
varai naṭattakkaṭavom tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār koyiṟ tāṉattārom
paṇiyāl koyiṟkaṇakku tiruppuṭkuḻipiriyaṉ eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
VĪRASADAŚIVAMAHĀRĀYA-ŚAKA 1483 (1561 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of the village of Tuṟaiyūr, for the
supply of food offerings in the temple, by Rāyasam-Veṅkaṭādri-Ayyaṉ, son
of Mosalimaḍugu Timmarāja.

edition.l:
śivadēva mahārāyar pradhivirājyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa śakābtam
1483- ṉ mel [cel*]lā niṉṟa tummiti samvatsarattu
tulānāyaṟṟu puṟuvapakṣattu cattamiyum putavāramum peṟṟa

l.ta:
ttu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut
tāmalkoṭṭattu t tāmalnāṭṭu tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi perumāḷ
poreṟṟu nayiṉār koyil keḻvi mātavaciyarum śrī paṇṭārattarum tā-

edition.l:
[ṉa]ttār eḻu niṟuvākakāṟarum [mo]mosalimaṭukut timmarāju kumārar
rāyasam veṅkaṭāttiṟi ayyaṉukku poliyūṭṭu sāsanam
paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭit tammiṭaya poliyūṭṭāṉa ticai[ kra] .

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
NALLASIDDHARASA OF THE FAMILY OF MUKKAṆA KĀḌUVEṬṬI
-15TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that in accordance with an order of the king,
the earlier tirunāmattukkāṇi village Kaṭṭanūr including Paḻaiyagaram and
Puttagaram, were confirmed as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam gift to the temple. The king
belonged to the Bharatvāja gōtra and described as the ornaments of the
Pallava race.

l.gr: nāthadaiva śrī pādapatma rāga kāñcipuravarādiśvara kāmakōṣṭam

l.ta:
rkoṭṭattu tāmarnāṭṭu tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār
vijaya

māḷ koyil tānattār kaṇṭu tirunāmattukkāṇiyāṉa kaṭṭanūrp
paḻaakaramum puttakaramum iṉṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇiyākat tāṅ

kaḷ muṉṉāḷ kalkoṭuṭaiyapaṭiye patiṉaiñcāvatu mutal
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka nāmum mutalaṭaṅ[ka*] iṟaiiliyāka

vippatu ippaṭi cantirātittavarai
celluvatāka utakam paṇṇik kuṭuttom ippaṭikku kallilum cempilum
veṭṭik koḷ

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
DĒVARĀYA MAHARĀYA ‘WHO WITNESSED THE ELEPHANT-
HUNT’-ŚAKA 1360 (1438 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of temple land to various servants
and functionaries of the temple, on condition that they should not involve
themselves in the sale or mortagage of those lands. This was made in the
presence of the agent of Lakkappa-Nāyaka.

niṉṟa kālayukti

tiruppuṭkuḻi yi •••••

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-19TH
YEAR (1269 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of 8 cows for burning (1/4) lamp by
Amarakōṉ alias Periyaperumāḷ, the son of Uḍaiyānkoṉ a native of Āndai
nāḍu.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vicaiyakaṇṭakopāla tevarkku
yāṇṭu

koṭṭattu tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉārkku tiruviḷakkukku āntai nāṭṭil
irukkum uṭaiyāṉ koṉmakaṉ periyaperumāḷāṉa amarakoṉ

ippacu eṭṭum kaikkoṇṭu kālviḷakku
cantirātittavarai naṭattakaṭavom tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār koyil

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF
THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a person named Mukkanadēvaṉ Śaraṇapiḷḷai
of Dāmar alias Nittavinōdanallūr who set up an image of
Nittavinōdachchelvar in the temple of Tiruppukkuḻi Nāyaṉār.

l.ta:
nāyaṉār koyilil nittat tiruvārātaṉaṅ koṇṭaruḷukiṟa
nittavinotac celvarai
ukantaruḷuvittāṉ tāmarāṉa nittaviṉota nallūr tāma • ṅkiḻavaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-7TH
YEAR (1257 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land, by purchase in Kaṭṭaṉūr
alias Svamisantosha Chaturvedimaṅgalam alias Puttagaram and made it as
tirunāmattukkāṇī to the temples, by Vīravaḷḷippillār Dēvaperumāḷ
Maḻavarāyaṉ of the same village. The engraver was named as
Pērarulāchchāryaṉ of Daṇḍaga nāḍu.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ civicaiyakaṇṭakopāla tevaṟku
yāṇṭu eḻāvatu

taṉu nāyaṟṟu orunāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu tāmaṟkoṭṭattu
tāmarnāṭṭu

aḻittāṉ pakkal paṟṟuḻāṉ tevapperumāḷāṉ maḻavarāyaṉeṉ ivar
pakkal nāṉ pon

kuṭuttu koṇṭa paṅkālu eḷviḷai nilamuṅ kollaiyum maṉaiyum
poreṟṟukku iṉakku naṉṟākac ciṅkā ••••• ntikke oru taḷikai

ivvaka[ra*]ttil piccaṉ allāḷap perumāḷ paṅkāl koṇṭa pākam
araiyum melpaṭiyāṉ viṟṟuk koḷvateṉṟu viṭu

kaiyil itu araiyum ākappaṅku oṉṟum ivvakarattu attik
kuṟicciṅkattiṉ kāṇippaṅkāy koṇṭuṭaiya paṅku arai

koṅku kāle araikkālum
vatu tai māta varaikku poreṟṟukku eḻu

ku nālukkum ivai kalveṭṭiṉeṉ taṇṭaka nāṭṭuk
peraruḷācāriyaṉeṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN VIJAYAGAṆḌAGOPĀLADĒVA-30TH
YEAR (1280 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of lands located in Kaṭṭanūr by
Madurāntakap-pottappichchōḻaṉ Vīragaṇḍagopālaṉ for the supply of food
offering and other sacred services to the temple. Taxes like Sekkukaḍamai,
Taṟiyiṟai, Taṭṭārbāṭṭam, Inavari, E7rimiṉbāṭṭam, Poṉvari, Kudiraivari etc., were
exempted from the gifted land.

l.ta:
vaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī vicaiyakaṇṭakopāla tevarkku
yāṇṭu

ḷḷik kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattut tāmaṟ koṭṭattuttāmar nāṭṭut tiruppa

ti tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār vicaiyarākavap perumāḷukku maturāntakap
pottappic coḻaṉ virakaṇṭakopāla tevaṉeṉ nāyaṉār vicaiya

vile
innāyaṉārkkut tirunāṭpaṭikkum amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭikkum palapaṇi
niman

tattukkum utakam pa[ṇ*]ṇi aruḷiṉa kaṭṭaṉūr paḻavakaramum puttakaram
iṟaiyili tevatāṉa nikkik kiḻpāṟkellai ampi akarattāṉ el

laikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai pūttoṇṭaṉpaṭṭu ellaikkum
oṭippākkattāṉ ellaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟ kellai putiya

akarattār ellaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai mīpārk kellaikku
teṟkum āka inta nāṟpāṟ kellaikku naṭuvuṭpaṭṭa nilam muppatāva

tu aṟpaci mātam mutal naṉcey puṉceykkaṭamai āyam puṟakkalaṉai
cekkukkaṭamai taṟiyiṟai taṭṭār pāṭṭam iṉavari erimiṉ pāṭṭam

ṇikkuṭutteṉ maturāntakap pottappic coḻaṉ virakaṇṭakopāla
tevaṉeṉ ||

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the installation of the image Vāḻavanda-
perumāḷ in the temple by Saravanappiḷḷai-mukkaṇa-dēvaṉ, the resident of
Nittavinodanallūr.

l.ta:
tāmarkkoṭṭattut tāmarnāṭṭut tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi nāyaṉār
ko
yilil vāḻavanta perumāḷai ukantaruḷap paṇṇiṉā[ṉ] tāma • ṉ nitta

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of lamps and installation of the
image of Sēṉaimudaliyār by the Vaishṇavars of Māśandi nāḍu.

edition.p: On another slab to the right of above.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
DĒVARĀYA MAHARĀYA-ŚAKA 1354 (1432 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription refers to the gift of 10 poṉ for renovation
and repair works undertaken in the portion of the south wall of the
temple by Sakkaraśar, son of Āyilu-araśar. Tirupputkuḻipiriyaṉ was the
accountant of the temple.

l.ta: āyilu aracar makaṉār cakkaracar poṉ pattiṉāl paṇṇa •••••

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 201 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of 1100 kuḻi of land for the
purpose of extension of area and renovation of the sub-shrine, as well as
food offerings, in the temple of Subrahmaṇyapiḷḷaiyār at Kaṭṭaṉūr by the
Mahēśvarā who had assembled in the Gaṅgaikoṇḍāṉ maṇḍapa.

l.gr:
sya gaṅgāgraha maṇḍapē pālinantuṇḍapatinam
śrī māhēśvara śāsanam |

l.ta:
keṅkai koṇṭān tirumaṇṭapattu niṟaivaṟa niṟaintu kuṟaivaṟakkūṭi
irunta śrī ru

porēṟṟiṉ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāṉa kaṭṭaṉūril eḻuntaruḷi irukkum
cuppiramaṇṇiyap piḷḷaiyārkku tirukkoyil parappu po

tāmaiyāle ikkoyilukkum tirumatiḷukkum nāṟpāṟ kellaikku
uḷḷu nūṟu kuḻi viṭavum amutupaṭikku paḻantevatāṉa

m uṭpaṭa āyiram kuḻi nirnilattile ceraviṭak kaṭavatākavum ippaṭi
koṇṭu koyilukku pūcaiyum amutu paṭiyum celu

ttavum ippaṭiya oḻiya vēṟoru putumai paṇṇak kaṭavatallavākavum ippaṭi
cantirātittavaraiyum celvatāka cilāle

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers that certain Sembiyadaraiyar, the younger
brother of Pallavarāyar, one of the mudalis of Kulaśēkharadēvar-maṇḍapa
made some provision for the supply of garlands and other services to the
temple.

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL
(INSIDE) OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA OF THE SAME TEMPLE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1483 (1561 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription contains the information regarding an agreement
made between the Jīyar and the Sthānattār of the temple for the supply
of food offerings to the god and goddess at the time of procession.

l.ta:
ṇṭalattu tāmalkoṭṭattu tāmalṉāṭṭu tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi perumāḷ
poreṟṟu nayiṉār koyil keḻvimātavajiyarum

tta poṉ muppatukkum
nilavoṟṟi piṭittu atil utayam koṇṭu perumāḷ vicayarākavarum
navarā

ntaruḷiṉa potellām aṉaittaḻukuṅ kaṇṭa perumāḷ kovil
vācalile [kaṟ]pūram [ti]ru[to]caipaṭṭi tammu ••••• yumāka amutu ceyyap
paṇṇakkaṭavomākavum tinam oru tirumālai

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VĪRAPRATĀPA ••••• SON OF NARASAIYA DĒVA, ‘WHO LEVIED
TRIBUTE FROM ALL COUNTRIES’, ŚAKA 1414 (1492 A.D.)
inscription.p: Built in the wall. It contain just the prasasti of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p: Damaged. This inscription seems to register the gift of 35 poṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of 100 paṇam for making offerings
like vaḍai 24, jaggerry Pānagam, Paṉigāram etc., on the occasion of
swinging festival in the temple. Signatories Kōyil-kēḻvisiyar, and
Tirupputkuḻi priyaṉ are mentioned in the inscription. The donor was
Narasammāḷ daughter of Tāttaiyangār of Kūram.

l.ta: ṅkār kumāratti naracammāḷ tiruūñcal tirunāḷ upaiya
ppaṇam patiṉeṭṭukkum tirunāḷ ēḻukkum nāḷ oṉṟukku vaṭai
nālum cukiyaṉ yirupattunālum pāṉa[ka]ttukku vellam ku

ṟuṇiyum payaṟṟam tirupaṇiyārattukku payaṟu kuṟuṇiyum amutu ceyyap
paṇṇak kaṭavomākavum

ittaṉmam cantirāttavaraiyum naṭattak kaṭavatākavum ivai koyil
keḻviciyar eḻuttu ivai tirupuṭkuḻippiriyaṉ eḻuttu•

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the construction of maṇḍapa and the shrine
of Periyapirāṭṭiyār by Jagadabbagaṇḍaṇ Mahābali-Vāṉarāyaṉ. Kōyilkaṇakku
Tiruppuṭ Kuḻippiriyaṉ Pachchaiyappaṉ is mentioned as signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF
THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE
MARATAKAVALLINACHCHIYAR SHRINE IN THE SAME TEMPLE
VĪRAVEṄKAṬAPATI DĒVA MAHĀRĀYA, ‘WHO LEVIED TRIBUTE
FROM ALL COUNTRIES’-ŚAKA 1514 (1592 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to gift of land by purchase in Tuṟaiyūr for
the supply of food offerings to the shrine of Maragatavaḷḷi-Nāchchiyār as
per the agreement between the Jīyar, Eṭṭūr-Tirumalai-Kumāra
Tatāchārya, and others of the temple of Pōreṟṟu-nāyiṉār at Tiruppuṭkuli in
Dāmaṟkōṭṭam, a sub-division of Dāmaṟnāḍu, situated in Sōḻiṅgapura paṟṟu
which belonged to Chandragirirājya in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-Chōḻamaṇḍalam. It also
mentions the donor Nāgama Nāyaka, son of Timma Nāyaka, grandson of
Pāppu Nāyaka, and great grandson of Veṅgaḷappa Nāyaka of Vēlūr.

l.ta:
śrī
viraveṅkaṭapati teva mahārāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷā niṉṟa
cakāṟttam

ttarattu
meṣaṉāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu tutikaiyum ātivāramum peṟṟa asvati
ṉakṣattirattu ṉāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu cantirakiri
rācciyattu coḷiṅkapurapaṟṟu tāmalṉāṭu tāmalkkoṭṭat tiruppatit
tiruppuṭkkuḻi perumāḷ poreṟṟu nayiṉār kovil keḻviciyarum
paṇṭārattārum śrīkāriya

ayyaṉum stalam eḻuniṟuvākakāṟarum velūril irukkum tuṅkaḷa
kottirattu veṅkaḷappaṉāyakkar pāppaṉāyakkar kum[āra tim]maṉāyakkar
ṉākamaṉāyakkarkku cilā cāsaṉam paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi tuṟaiūr kiṟāmam
aṅkuṣam 3- l oru aṅkucam ṉākaṉātaṟkku poy tāṅkaḷ
vicayarākavapperumāḷ kovilil aṅkucam oṉṟu kiṟayamāka koṇṭa pon_
275 yatu aṭṭavaṇai veṅka[ṭa]yaṉ aṅkuṣam oṉṟum kiṟayamāka
koṇṭa pon_ 275 āka kiṟayamāka koṇṭa pon_ 550- kku aṅkuṣam
2- m tāṅkaḷ eṟi aruḷa paṇṇivitta poroṟṟu nayiṉār ṉācciyār
marutakavalli ṉācciyārukku poliyūṭṭāy tāraivāṟkkaiyil yinta
kiṟāmam aṅkuṣam 2- l varum palaṉ varuṣa[ 1- kku] ø360 yitu pon_

ø(1/4) poy taḷi

kai melukku kariyamutu ney paruppu tayir ømukkāl amutu ceyita taḷikai 5-
kku stalam ṉiṟuvākakāṟarukkut ••••• vipoyāramāñki kūṭam uḷpaṭa
parateciyaḷukku taḷikai kaṟiyamutu ø tiṉakkaṭṭaḷaiyāka yintappaṭi

ṉaṭattak kaṭavārākavu yintappaṭikku cilāccāsaṉam paṇṇik kuṭuttom
koyil keḻviciyar tāttāccāriyar ayyaṉ eḻuniṟuvākakāṟarum ōm

tāṇaiyam kuruvarājā

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VĪRAPRATĀPA VĪRA SRIRAṄGAYADĒVA MAHARĀYA,
ŚAKA 1499 (1577 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the sale of the right of sacred services like
supervision, repairs (tiruppaṇi-nirvāhakaṇiyātshi) by Eṭṭūr-Tirumalai-Kumāra
Tatāchārya, along with other trustees of the same temple, to Tiruppullāṇi
Parāṅkuśaṉ of Tirumalai-tiruppati and his disciples, in order to continue the
holy service without interruption. It also mentiones the items of sacred
food like as Prasadam, Appam, Tōsai, iṭali etc., to be supplied to the
temple along with the other items like betel leaves and nuts etc., regularly.

edition.l:
svastiśrī man mahāmaṇḍaleśvaran rājādhirājaparamēśvara pūṟuvatekṣiṇa
paścima uttaracatu samudrādhicura śrīvirapradāpa śrīviraciraṅkayadēva
mahārāyar pradhivirājyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉ[ṟa] śakābtam 1499-
ṉ meṟ cellā niṉṟa bahudhānya varuṣattu mēṣaṉāyaṟṟu kṛṣṇapakṣattu
daśamiyum sōmavāramum peṟṟa śravaṇa nakṣatrattu ṉāḷ jayaṅkoṇḍa
coḻamaṇṭalattuc

l.ta:
ttu coḷiṅkapurappaṟṟu tāmalkoṭattu tiruppati
tiruppuṭkuḻiyil viṟṟiruntaruḷiya perumāḷ poreṟṟu nayiṉār śrī
paṇṭārattārum śrī kāriyam ceyyum yeṭṭūṟt tirumala kumāra tātācāriyar
ayyaṉum

lam eḻuniṟuvākakāṟarum tirumalai tiruppati tiruveṅkaṭa
muṭaiyāṉ śrī kāriya ••••• ntārāna tiruveṅkaṭacciṟukkaṉ tiruppallāṉi śrī
parākucaṉ tiruppaṇi piḷḷaikku•

nam paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi nāḷatu mutal
ikkoyil tiruppaṇi ni
kam oṉṟukku kiṟayamāka niccayittu śrī pa

edition.l:
yiṭattu yitukku tirukkovilil peṟumpaṭi ṉāḷ 1 kku pisātam 34§
appa[m] 3 tocai 3- yiṭṭalikku tiruvatticāmam •••mātamoṉṟukku
yilakkai ø mukkāṇi mai600 mūn_ṛumām cantaṉa pākku veṟṟilaiyum maṟṟumuṇḍāṉa
tiṅkaḷ tivasam tiruṉāḷ viceṣa vakaiyum sakalātāyamum yinta
ēḻunirvākattukku aṟṟapaṭiye eṭṭām nirvāhamāka uḷḷa
suvantrasvāmiya mellām paṟṟik koṇṭu tiruppaṇiyaḷukku atikkam
orāḷai vaittu tiruppaṇiyaḷum

l.ta:
ṇṭāram olukalip perumāḷjiyar
yivai tātācāriyar ayyaṉ yivai allāḷappar yivai [ku]

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VĪRAPRATĀPA ACHYUTARĀYA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1458 (1536 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the poliyūṭṭu gift of the village of Paṭṭālam
by Narasammaṅgār, daughter of Gaḍḍam Tirumala Tāttayaṅgār of Kūram,
for the supply of food offerings, nāluvagai tirupaṇiyāram and Pānagam to
the temple on the occasion of swinging festival.

edition.l:
1458- l mel cellāniṉṟa dummukhi samvatsarattu mituna
nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu sattamiyum ātitta vāramum peṟṟa atta nakṣatrattu
nāḷ jeyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu tāmalnāṭṭu tāmalkoṭṭattu
tiruppati tiruppuṭkuḻi poreṟṟu nayiṉār koyil keḻvi śrī
vayiṣṇavaciyarum śrī paṇṭārattārum kūram ga[ḍḍam] ti

l.ta:
nam kuṭuttapaṭi tammiṭa poliyūṭṭāṉa tiruvūñcal tirunāḷ eḻukkum
tinacciṟappu nāḷ oṉṟukkut taḷikai nālukkum tām koṇṭu viṭṭa śrī

mamoṉṟum śrī paṇṭāratattile āṇṭu koṇṭu
inta
mattil uṟpanam koṇṭu tiruvuñcal tiruṉāḷeḻukkum ṉāḷ

oṉṟukkum ciṟappukku yirupattunālu taḷikaiyum nāluvakai
tiruppaṇiyāramum pānakamum amutuceyyak kaṭavārākavum tinacciṟappu nāḷ
oṉṟukku nālu taḷikai amutu ceyyak kaṭavarākavum yitil viṭṭavaṉ
viḻukkāṭu tamakku nālil oṉṟukku nāḷ oṉṟukku oru taḷikaiyum
tirunāḷ eḻukkum nāḷ oṉṟukku taḷikai āṟum tiruppaṇiyāram pānakkam
nāliloṉṟum

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VĪRAPRATĀPA VĪRASADĀŚIVARĀYA MAHARĀYA.
inscription.p:
Built in at the end. This inscription refers to the gift of food
offerings to the perumal temple by the son of Timmarajayyan.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the measuring rod named Rājavibhāḍaṉkōl
and Gaṇḍaragaṇḍaṉkōl.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SAME MAṆḌAPA.
VĪRAPRATĀPA SADĀŚIVARĀYA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1466 (1544 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of the village Oṭṭipākkam, besides
house to a certain Veṅgappapiḷḷai, the son of Muḍumbai Arasuppiḷḷai
belonging to the Kāshyapagōtra of Īñjambāḍu by the temple officials
Kēḷvikkēsuvachchayarum, Srī Bhaṇdārattār for the supply of sacred food to
the temple Tirupputkuḷi Pērāṟṟunāyīṉār of Tiruppati of Dāmalkōṭṭam in
Dāmal-Nāḍu of Jayaṅgoṇḍa chōlamaṇḍalam. Kōyirkaṇakku Tiruppuṭkuḻi
Priyaṉ Kāttamuttāṉ Vijayar is mentioned as signatory.

l.ta:
ḷuva[na*] ariyarāya vipāṭan pāṣaikkut tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ
muvarāyar kaṇṭan rācātirāca rācaparamecuran śrī virappiṟatāpa
catāciva teva makārāyar piṟitivirācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa cakāṟtam

rattu
ciṅkaṉāyaṟṟu pūṟuva paṣṣattu caṣṭṭiyum comavāramum peṟṟa coti naṣ

ṣattiṟattu ṉāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattut tāmalṉāṭu
tāmalkkoṭṭattut tiruppati tiruppuṭkkuḻi poreṟṟu nayiṉār koyil
keḻvik kecuvacciyarum śrī paṇṭārattārum

mpa cūttiṟattu kācipa kottiṟattu muṭumpai
aracupiḷḷai puttiṟaṉ veṅkappāpiḷḷaikku poliyūṭṭu

ciṟappu ṉāḷ oṉṟukkut taḷikai āṟukkut tāmal lappaṉāyakkar
catācivarāyar vicanakaram koṇṭa ceytikku rāya

m vanta santoḻa
veḷaiyile tārāpūṟuvamāka paṇṇik koṇṭu viṭṭa

oṭṭipākkam śrī paṇṭārattile āṇṭu koṇṭu yintak kiṟāmattil
uṟpaṉṉam koṇṭu ṉāḷ oṉṟukku āṟu taḷikai tiruppa ṉām amutu
ceyappaṇṇivikkak kaṭaiomākavum yitil

viṭṭavaṉ viḻukkāṭu tamakku ṉālil oṉṟukku oṉṟarait taḷikaiyum
caṉṉatit teruvil oṉṟarait taḷikaiyum caṉṉatit teruvil
teṉciṟakil aṇṇaya[ṅ*]kār kuṭiyirukkiṟa maṉaikkuk kiḻakku
kiḻaivitikku meṟkuppaḷḷi koṇṭa perumāḷ

kuṭiyirunta kaṇakkuppiḷḷai eḻuvakkariyar kāṇiyāṭciyāṉa maṉai
oṉṟukku a[ṭa]muppattāṟum vālvīccaṭi ṉūṟum yinta maṉaiyum kuṭuttu
cantāṉa paramparai cantiṟātittavaraiyum naṭatta yintat-

taṟmamum cantiṟātittavaraiyum naṭattak kaṭaivomākavum yinta maṉai
yivaṟku tāṉāti taṉmaṅkaḷ viṉivikkiṟaiyam cela niti nikṣepam
akṣaṇi ākāmi citta

miyaṅkaḷukkum urittākak kaṭavatākavum yippaṭi cammatittu maṉaiyum
poliyūṭṭum taṟma

ṉam paṇṇik kuṭuttom
veṅkappāpiḷḷaikku poreṟṟu nayiṉār śrī paṇṭāra

ttārom § kecavacciyar eḻuttu yivai koyiṟ kaṇakkut
tiruppuṭkuḻip piriyan kāttamuttiṉ

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-AT THE ENTRANCE INTO
A VACANT SHRINE ON THE WESTERN ENCLOSURE OF THE
SAME TEMPLE, LEFT SIDE
(SAMBUVARĀYA) 17+1 YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to an order of Sambuvarāyaṉ regarding the
gift of some land as sarvamaṇya in the village of Tugavaḷūr by the king
Sambuvaraya for the supply of food offerings to the image of
Peṟṟaperumāḷ and Nāchchimār set up by the Jīyar of Tiruppuṭkuḻi in the
temple of Peṟṟēaperumāḷ. Taxes like Poṉvari, Pāṇḍivari, Puñjey
Āsuvappoṉvari, Paṭṭōlaippaṇam, Nalleruduvāsal, Oḍumugampāvai
Mūṉrukānikkai etc., are mentioned.

l.ta: vallattu ūravar kaṇṭuttu [kava]ḷur palataḷi pūcai pātiyu
m paṭṭavirttiyum ••••• paṭṭiyum nikki patiṉ eḻāvatu
kku etirāmāṇṭu ā[ṭi]mātam mutal kaṭamai āyam komuṟṟa
vapeṟu putiyitu uḷḷiṭṭa palanel lāyamṅkaḷum po
ṉvari pāṇṭivari puñcai[yu]m puṉcey ācuvap poṉvari
paṭṭolai paṇam nalleerutu vācal oṭumukam pā
ko • ḷu pātikaḷ uḷpaṭa tiruppukkuḻiyil pe
kuḻi ciyar eṟi aruḷappa[ṇ*]ṇiṉa perumāḷ peṟṟa perumā
tavaraiyum cella tiruviṭai āṭṭam iṭṭa nilam muḷ

edition.l: niṉṟa kūṟu 3- l oṉṟum cantirātittavaraiyum naṭakku

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-AT THE ENTRANCE INTO
THE GŌPURA OF THE SAME TEMPLE, LEFT SIDE
(VIJAYANAGARA).
inscription.p:
This inscription seems to registers the gift of taxes collected from
the village Tiruppuṭkuḻi by a certain Kāñjikkuṟivaradarāja for burning
lamps and food offerings in the temple of Pōreṟṟu nāyiṉār, for the merit
of Ellamarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-IN THE SAME PLACE
KṚISHṆADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA, VISHA (1521-22 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription refers to a certian person named
Vasavappa Nāyaka, the elder brother of Aḍappattu Vaiyappa Nāyaka, who
made a gift of the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam village Vānavantāṅgal for a festival to
be celebrated on the day of Jyēshṭhā in the month of Māśi which was
the natal star of the king Krishnadēva Mahārāyar.

l.ta:
irāyar aṭappattu vayyappa nāyakkar tamaiyanār vacavappa
nāyakkar tāmalkoṭṭam tāmal nāṭṭu tiruppati tiruppu

ka naṭantu vanta tāmal piṭākaiyāṉa vāṉavantāṅkal
tiṟantu paṇṭāravāṭaiyāka naṭantu varukaiyil nā-

koḷḷukaiyil tāmal piṭākaiyāṉa vāṉavaṉtāṅkal nām iṟṟai nāḷ
coma vāramum yekateciyum mi[ṟu]kacīriṣamum peṟṟa tivacattile irāyar
ki

keṭṭaiyi
••••• tirukkoṭi ēṟittirunāḷ naṭatta nammiṭa upayamāka
i[nta]vāṉavantāṅkal nañcai puñcai nāṟpāṟ kellaiyummā

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE GŌPURA; RIGHT
OF ENTRANCE.
VĪRA HARIHARARĀYA, BHAHUDĀNYA (1398 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the Kaikkōḷa and other Iḍaṅgai members
made some contribution for burning a lamp in the same temple.

l.ta: yaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum tiṟitikaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ jayaṅko
puṭkuḻi nāyaṉāṟku tiruviḷakkukku oṉṟu pātiyum ivvūr
yāṟkum maṟṟum iṭaṅkaiyil uḷḷāṟkum oṉṟu pātiyum
āka iṭaṅkaivari iṭaṅkaiyaiy nokkivarum palavari māniyam
āka viṭṭa aḷavukkum caṅketa camaiyattāṟar pukutakkaṭavatalla
eṉṟu collak kallilum cempilum veṭṭik kuṭuttom
inta tarmattukku virotam paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅkai karaiyil
kurāl pacuvaik koṉṟa pāpamuṅ koṇṭa nā • paṭutturokam āka

kku ivai ivai iṭaṅkai kāmaveḷāneṉ eḻuttuteṟku āka ticai
iṉṉāṉku ellaikku uṭpaṭṭa

li innilam aṟuveliyum ivvūr nattamum cettalait
tiṭallum mēl nokkiṉa mara

mum kīḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟum uṭpa[ṭ*]ṭatu ivarkku iṟṟaināḷ
viṟṟukkuṭuttuk koḷvatāṉa emmili

nāṉku ellaikku uṭpaṭṭa naṉcey rūpāy aṟuveliyum nattamum cettalait
tiṭarum kuḷamum vilai āvatākavum

ippaṇam toṇṇūṟum ikkiḻppaṭa paṟṟik koṇṭu intaṅ karuppūr
viṟṟu vilaip piṟamāṇam pa

ṇṇik kuṭutteṉ uṭaiyār tiruvampalamuṭaiyār toṇṭaimāṉāṟkku
kaḷañ[ca*]lūr kiḻavaṉ

cellappiḷḷaiyāṉa koṉāreṉ ippaṭikku ivai cempiyaṉ teṟaṉār
eḻuttu
ivai tiruvīraṭṭāṉa muṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu puṅkaṉūr kiḻavaṉ umaiyuruva •••••
eḻuttu ivai perumaṅkala muṭaiyān tirumeṉi aḻakiyāṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON A SLAB SET UP IN
FRONT OF THE GEṄGUṆḌĀR MAṆḌAPA
•••••••••• 30TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
Incomplete. This inscription refers to an order issued by
Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻaṉ in connection with the grant of the village
Kaṭṭaṉūr excluding the adjacent part of the hamlet Paḻaiyagaram and
Puttagaram as tax free dēvadāna village to the temple Vijayaraghava
Perumāḷ at Tiruppuṭkuḻi.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME SLAB;
BACK SIDE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the Pūjai tiruvārādana that was celebrated
by the people of chittiramēḻi malai maṇḍalam in the temple at
Tiruppuṭkuḻi.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1916.)
TIRUPPUKKUḺI, CONJEEVERAM TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
VIJAYARAGHAVAPERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF
THEMAṆḌAPA IN BĀLŚEṬṬI-CHHATRAM NEAR THE SAME
VILLAGE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1675, VIJAYA (1753 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the construction of the Kalyāṇa maṇḍapa,
tank and Chatram by Būta Veṅkaṭapati seṭṭi, Vaiśya kula, of the seṭṭigōtra
of Tiruvidhi sūtra.

l.gr:
ladhali śēṭṭi gōtrōdbhava
tiruvidhi mutyālu śēṭṭi pautra-

nu nirmāṇam cēśi samarppimci nāṭhaḍu śvōkam || svamśajāḥ madhvam
dhvam
śajāha bhumipāssata tamurjvala dharmacittāḥ | madhdharma mēkhasa

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1916.)
TAIYŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
MURUGĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
MARAGATAVALLI AMMAN SHRINE.
JAṬĀVARMAN TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA
DĒVA-19TH YEAR (13TH CENTURY A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 2100 kuḻi of land as
Tirunāmattukkāṇi by six residents of Tiruviḍavandai in Paḍuvūr nāḍu a
sub-division of Āmūr kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa maṇḍalam, for the supply
of food offerings to the temple of Nāyaṉār Aḻagiya Śokkaṉār. Signatories
like Muḷḷikkuḍaiyāṉ Mudali, Muḷḷikkuḍaiyāṉ Periyaperumāḷ are mentioned
in it. Refers to some punishment by the king's officers.

l.ta:
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu āmūrkkoṭṭattup paṭuvūrnāṭṭut
tiruviṭavantai muḷḷikkuṭaiyāṉ kolavarākaṉ mutaliyum
periyaperumāṉum tiruvekampamuṭaiyāṉum

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1916.)
TAIYŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
MURUGĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A STONE IN THE MUKHA
MAṆḌAPA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
VĪRAPRATĀPA ACHCHUTAYADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1458 (1536 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of land for worship and carry out
repair work in the temple Nayinār Tirumurugī [śvaramu]ḍaiya-Tambiraṉār
at Rājakēśvari [nallūr] alias Taiyūr in [Ā]yiravēlippaṟṟu a village of Kumiḻi
nāḍu, which was a sub-division of Āmūr kōṭṭam. It is stated that the land
was situated in Kuṉranallūr in Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu, which was the hamlet
of Saundariyachōḻapuram in Puḻar kōṭṭam a sub-division of
[Jayaṅgo]ṇḍachōḻa maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1629 (1707 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the sacred services undertaken in the
temple of Seṅgaṇmalīśvaramuḍaiyār at Taiyūr in Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu at
Kumiḻi nāḍu, a sub-division of Āmūr kōṭṭam by a certain Muttuvālu śeṭṭi,
son of Paraśurāma śeṭṭi of Cheṉṉapaṭṭaṇam.

l.ta: m paracurāma ceṭṭiyār kumāraṉ muttu vālu ce

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
ACHUDHADĒVA MAHĀRĀYAR, SON OF NARASIMHA DĒVA
MAHARĀYAR, YEAR, Ś. 1458 (1536 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription is found by the side of an image (of the donor).
The subject of the inscription is that Vijayarā(ya)r of Koṟṟamaṅgalam,
made the gift of lands for performing pūja and other sacred services in
the temple at Tayiūr.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA INFRONT OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
VĪRAPRATĀPA KṚISHṆAYADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA-ŚAKA 1442 (1520
A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of a part of a village separated
from Araiyaṉśēri a hamlet of Seṅgaḷunīrpaṭṭu together with lands for
worshipping and other sacred services in the temple of
Seṅgaṇmalīśvaramuḍaiyār Nāyaṉār at Rājakēśvarinallūr alias Taiyūr in
Āyiravēlippaṟṟu a sub-division of Kumiḻi nāḍu, in Āmūr kōṭṭam, under the
orders of Sellappar Vi[ra]naraśi[ṁha]rāya Nāyakar, who was the son of
Taḻuvakkuḻaindāṉ Bhaṭṭaṉ of Kāñchipuram, who belonged to Agasthiya
gōtra. The gift was made for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA
KAMPAṆṆA UḌAIYĀR, SON OF VĪRA BOKKAṆA UḌAIYĀR,
SOBHAKRIT (1363 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a grant of 10 vēli of land as sarvamāṇya
by the king for worshipping and repairs works in the temple of
Śeṅgaṇmalīchchuramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Taiyūr alias Rājakēśarinallūr in
Kumiḻi nāḍu a sub-division of Āmūr kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa maṇḍalam.
The order regarding the grant was issued by Sōmaya-Daṇṇanāyaka in
accordance with the written order of Rājanārāyaṇaṉ-Sambuvarāyar.

l.ta: coḻamaṇṭalattu āmūr koṭṭattu kumiḻi nāṭṭu taiy
yūrāṉa irācakecari nallūr uṭaiyar ceṅkaṇ māliccuram

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA
MĀRAVARMAṈ TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIṈ VĪRAPĀṆḌYA
DĒVA-11TH YEAR. (1290 A.D. ?)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the assembly of Vaiśya and Vāṇiya
merchants (nagarattōm) of the ‘18 deśās’ constructed the Alaṅgāra maṭha
for the merit of Daṇmadāvaḷa kūttar in the temple of
Śeṅgaṇmalīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṇār at Taiyūr alias Rājakēśvarinallūr in Kumiḻi
nāḍu, which was a sub-division of Āmūr kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa
maṇḍalam and also states that it set apart certain tolls on their merchandise
(pepper, arecanut, etc.)

taṉma tāvaṉ tiruvācalum maṟṟum uḷḷa tiruppaṇikaḷum ceykai

tiruvācalil iruntu uṭaiyār ceṅkaṇamālicuramuṭaiya nāyaṉār taṉma

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1916.)
SEṄGANMĀL, CHINGLEPUT TALUK,
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
SEṄGANMĀLĪŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE
SAME MAṆḌAPA
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN JAṬĀVARMAṈ SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA
DĒVA-13TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the rate of monthly rent to be paid by the
merchants and weavers who had settled in the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam of the
temple of Śeṅgaṇmālīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyaṇār and also mentions the name of
officers Viśaiyapāla and Varaguṇarāya who endorsed the request submitted
by the tenants.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE
MĀṞAVARMAN TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ VIKRAMA PĀṆḌYA DĒVA-7TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of poṉ 450 collected as taxes from
the four villages in Rājēndrachōḻapurappaṟṟu, for conducting the festivals
and offerings on the occasion of Rājakkanāyaṉ sandi, instituted in the
name of the king in the temple of Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at
Tirumuṭṭam by Moṇṇaippirāṉ Sīrāmadēvar alias Sēdirāyaṉ, a native of
KaruvūrPoṉpaṟṟi in Naḍuvilkūṟṟu a sub-division of Miḻalai kūṟṟam in
Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam. Signatory Kappalūruḍaiyāṉ of Ulagaḷandaṇ-Chōḻanallūr in
Miḻalaikūṟṟam of Pāṇḍimaṇḍalam is mentioned in it.

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
VĪRAPRATĀPA DĒ[VARĀYA] MAHARĀYA-ŚAKA 1365 (1443 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a sarvamaṇya grant by the nāṭṭār and
tandarimār of the eighteen districts (Paṟṟu)given to the ryots who
inhabited in the nattam lands within the premises of the temples of
Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya nāyaṉār and Ādivarāha nāyaṉār at Tirumuṭṭam in
Viḷandai kūṟṟam, a sub-division of Iruṅgōḷapāṇḍi nāḍu in Mēlkālnāḍu,
which was a district. of Vaḍagarai Virudarājabhayaṅkara Vaḷanāḍu, for the
supply of food offerings at the service called Periyanāṭṭāṉ śandi. The
names given as follows Pattiṟai, Kāsupāḍu, Pāḍagavari, Paḻavari, Puduvari,
Puḻugubāḍu, Poṟpāḍu, Kāsupāḍu, Lulpāḍu, Mūlaikkūḻipuḍavaipāḍu etc.
imposed over the newly settled residents. This document was written by
Kachchirāya-Venāvuḍayār.

l.ta: ṭu melkkāl nāṭṭu iruṅkoḷap pāṇṭināṭṭu vi
kum yiṉṉāṭu patiṉeṭṭuppaṟṟu nāṭṭār tantarimārrom taṉ

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME
SHRINE
RĀJAKĒSARIVAR[MAṈ] •••••••••• KULŌTTUṄGA [CHŌ]ḺADĒVA I-
30TH YEAR (1100 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This inscription seems to refers to the gift of dēvadāna
lands located in the villages Suṅgandavirttachō[ḻanallūr*] and
Ādhivarāhanallūr made to the temple of Tirumuṭṭa muḍaiya mahādēva and
to the temple of Śrivarāha-Āḻvār respectively at Muḍigoṇḍachōḻanallūr in
Viḷandaiyīr kūṟṟam a sub-division of Iruṅgōḷappāḍi, by the king while he
was comping at Tirumaḻuvāḍi in Poygai nāḍu, a sub-division of Gēyavinōda
vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJADĒVA-6TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of Pāḍikāval right and other income
of the devadāna villages for performing pūja in the same temple, by
Āḻappiṟandāṉ Eḻiśaimōgaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa Kāḍavarāyaṉ for the merit
of himself and his descendants.

l.ta: pūr kāṇi uṭaiya paḷḷi āṟappiṟantāṟ ēḻicai mokanāta ku
kku uṭalāka śrī pātattey nirvārttu kuṭutten āḷap
rum encātiyil uḷārum maṟṟum āṟeṉum ittaṉma[m*] aḻivu ce

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SAME
SHRINE RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN ALIAS CHAKRAVARTIN
KULŌTTUṄGA CHŌḺADĒVA-31+1ST YEAR (1101 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Built in wall. At the request of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Suvarṇṇādhirāja, the
king instructed to regroup the devadāna villages of Suṅgandavittachōḻanallūr
and Ādhivarāhanallūr, respectively to the temple of
Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiyamahādēva and to the temple of Ādivarāhāḻvān to avoid
their overlapping. Also mentions the temples like Piḍāri, Tirunārāyaṇa-
Iśvaramuḍaiya Mahādēva, in the village of Kāvanūr alias
Vīrarājēndramaṅgalam in Mēṟkā nāḍu, a sub-division of
Virudarājabhayaṅkara vaḷanāḍu and the big road leading to Peṇṇāgaḍam.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE SAME SHRINE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1395 (1474 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Unfinished. This inscription records the gift of lands located in
Suṅgan-davirttachōḻanallūr alias Tirumuṭṭam for maintaining the service
called Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ śandi. Mentions Veṭṭuṅgai Aḻagiyār Kachchiyarāyaṇ.

l.ta: mel cellā niṉṟa ceya rūpāy āṭi

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1413 (1491-92 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription registers the gift of (after exempting
certain taxes as) sarvamaṇya land for conducting festivals and
Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ śandi in the same temple. The signatories are Paḷḷigoṇḍa-
Perumāḷ alias Kachchiyarāyan. Taxes like Nāṭṭukkānikkāi, Kudiraikkāṇikkāi,
Chekku-Āyam, Paṉivilai, Nallerudu, Naṟpasu, Nallerumai, Nallādu are
mentioned in the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
KULŌTTUṄGA CHŌḺADĒVA-44TH YEAR (1114 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription furnishes the details of the gift of three Kāsus for
burning three lamps in the temple of Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiyār by Anantīśaṉ
Āḍavallāṉ of Śundaravaḻundūr.

l.ta:
āvatu aippaci viṣu
mutal tirumuṭṭamuṭaiyāṟku cu
ṭaiyāṉ anantīcaṉ āṭavallāṉ pakkal ikkoyil kāṇi uṭaiya
yaṅ koṇṭa kācu muṉṟu itu śrī pa

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1294 (1372 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to gift of lands for conducting the service
called Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ śandi, instituted by Śēvugapperumāḷ Kachchiyarāyaṇ
son of Iḷamaipperumāḷ-Kachchiyarāyar, the grandson of Veṭṭuṅgai Aḻagiyār
Kachchiyarāyar in the temple which is described as
TirumuṭṭamuḍaiyaNāyaṉār at Suṅgadavirthachōḻanallūr alias Tirumuṭṭam in
Viḷandai-kūṟṟam in Iruṅgōḷappāḍi nāḍu a sub-division of Mēṟkānāḍu in
Vaḍagarai Virudarājabhayaṅkara vaḷanāḍu. The donor inherited the gifted
lands from his father, who in turn purchased it from some Veḷḷāḷas.

l.ta: veḷḷāḻar palar pakkalilu muṉṉāḷ eṉ pāṭ

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
MAHĀRĀJA IMMAḌI TIRUMALAIDĒVA (1485-86.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of nañjey and puñjey lands located
in and around Neduñjēri by a person named Nāgaiya Nāyaka, one of the
servants of the king Saluvaimmadināyakar to the Tānattārs of the temple
Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya nāyaṉār for undertaking certain sacred services in it.
Taxes like Kudirai Kāṇikkai, Taṇḍukkānikkai, Karpūrakkāṇikkai,
Taṟikkaḍamai, Iṉavari, Pulvari, etc., are mentioned in the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
KULŌTTUṄGA CHŌḺADĒVA I-44TH YEAR (1114 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 24 sheep to a person Marudūr-
uḍaiyān for burning two lamps in the temple of Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiyār at
Viḻandaiyūṟ-kūṟṟam, a sub-division of VaḍagaraiVirudarājabhayaṅkara
vaḷanāḍu by Kāmadēvaṉ Poṉṉambalakkūttaṉ, a native of Vāgūr in Vāgūr
nāḍu, a sub-division of Puraṅgara(m)bai nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON A PILLAR INFRONT OF THE SAME
TEMPLE.
•••••••••• KALIYUGA 4060 (959 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the construction of a sluice in the big tank
Mahādēvippērēri by Iruṅgōlakkōṉ alias NārāyanaṉPugaḻuyppavargaṇḍaṉ who
was also called Vijayādittaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE FIRST
PRĀKĀRA OF THE SAME TEMPLE.
TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN [VIKRAMA CHŌḺADĒVA]-8TH
YEAR (1127 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Fragmentary. This inscription contains only a portion of the historical
introduction of the king with the words Pūmādupuṇara.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This incomplete inscription refers to the gift of 292 vēli and odd as
the total extent of land located in Kāvanūr, the eastern hamlet of
Muḍigoṇḍa chōḻachaturvēdimaṅgalam in Mēṟkā nāḍu, a sub-division of
Rājadhirāja vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta: ṟa ivvūr kīḻpiṭākai kāvanūr eṉṟu per kū

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
Fragment. Mentions the Viḍaiyil officers like Rājarājan Pāṇḍiyan,
Kaliṅgatharaiyan, Araiaṉ Tiruvegambarān alias Sēnavaraiyar, Ēḻattaraiyar,
the Uḍankūṭṭa-Adhikariagal, Narasiṅga-Chandrabhusham Vīrarājēndra-
brahmadhirājan, etc.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• 12TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the gift of 24 kāśu probally for the
cultivation of land. Mentions Srivaishṇavamāmuṉi.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
VIKRAMA CHŌḺADĒVA-16TH YEAR (1134 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 11 kāśu for burning a lamp by
Ānanda Nārāyaṇabhaṭṭa Sōmaiyājiyār a native of Iḍaiyāṟṟukkuḍi in Sūḍa-
Chūḷamaṇichchēri.

nṟum śrī paṇṭāratte mutalā ikkāciṭṭu pon koṇṭu maṇ-

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL OF THE PRĀKĀRA
KRISHNADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA-ŚAKA 1435 (1513 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to some tax concession given by Ādiyappa-
nāyakkar for the merit of his brother Chiṉṉappa nāyakkar to the people
of Ēḻupaṟṟu nāḍu who had suffered due to heavy tax burden and left
their villages.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1426 (1503-04 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to an order of Triṇētranātha Kachchirāyar, son
of Paḷḷigoṇḍa perumāḷ Kachchirāyar, who held the birudā Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ to
relieve the tenants of the Padinēḻuppaṟṟu district. from the hefty taxation
imposed by the Kaṇṇadiya rulers, which had forced them to leave their
holdings. Hereafter he also ordered that the lands shouldbe measured by 34
feet Kōl every year and fixed conducive rates of taxation. Accordingly
about 5 paṇam on puñjey, 20 on nañjey, Araikkālpaṇam on
Arasaper ṟukkuḍi, 3 paṇam on Saṭṭitari, 2 paṇam on Kanmālar, 3 paṇam on
each Taṟi held by Kaikkōlan, (1/4) paṇam on pasu, (1/2) on Erumai, 8 Āṭṭukku
(1/4) paṇam 1 paṇam for a Kurumpāḍu were imposed.

vāramum peṟṟa

ratecattu śrīmat tuvārakāpuri caṅkai

māṭṭu

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL OF THE SAME
PRĀKĀRA,
JAṬĀVARMAN ALIAS TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN
SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA DĒVA-8TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of some levies on their looms to
the temple of Tirunārāyanīśvaramuḍaiyā[r] Aḻagiya nāyaṉār for its
maintenance by the weavers, kaikōlars and kaikkōla mudali, who constituted
the iḍangai-samayam.

l.ta:
yaiyum kāmukilaiyum kaittalattāl poṟpamanta ina •

tiṟaikucavintavaṟku i • tiyaḷippa kalimeliyappakai paṇiya

mutta • muṭṭattuk kaikkoḷḷarum kaikkoḷa mutalika

tavaraiyum cella kallilum
cempilum veṭṭik koḷka itukku ilaṅkaṇakku

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL,
PAṬṬUKKAṬṬĀRI KONĒRIDĒVA MAHĀRĀJA, KILAKA (1488 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the gift of all taxes on nañjey and puñjey
lands in the village of Kiḻai-neḍuvāyil, by a certain individual named
Kōnērināyakar Nāgaiya Nāyakkar, a servant of the king Kōnēri
dēvamahārāya to Tānattārs of the temple Tirunārāyanīśvaramuḍaiyā-nāyaṉār.
The donor got his nāyakkattanam territory from the king. Taxes like
Kudiraikkānikkai, Koṭṭaikkānikkai, Nāṭṭukkāṇikkai, beside other taxes
imposed on the communities like Iḍaidurai, Cheṭṭiyār, Kaikkōlar, Kaṇmālar,
Paṟaiyar are mentioned in it.

l.ta:
licuran paṭṭukkaṭṭāri
vacavacaṅkaraṉ matiyāta maṉṉar maṇavāḷaṉ kāñcipuravarāticuran
koneriteva makāirācāviṉ ūḻiyam koṉeri nāyakkar

nākaiya nāyakkar tirumuṭṭam uṭaiya ṉāyaṉār koyil tānattāṟku
kuṭutta tanma cātaṉap paṭṭaiyam eṅkaḷukku āṇṭavar nāyakkat
taṉamākap pālittu tiruvuḷḷam paṟṟi aruḷina

patin oru paṟṟu cirmaiyil viḷantaipaṟṟu kiḻaiṉeṭuvāyi tirumuṭṭam
uṭaiyavar aḻakiya tirunārāyaṇi curam uṭaiya nāyaṉāyaṉāṟku
tevatāṉam āka āṇṭavar tirumenikki

koṭṭaikāṇikkai nāṭṭukkāṇikkai ṉāṭṭu viṉivo

neyyum paṟaiyar peril koḷḷum mutal veṭṭivari koṭṭippaṇam

āṭu eṇṇai ney koḻimuṭṭai nilavaḻakku koḷḷup payaṟu nel
mutalukku uṟai nāḻi iṉaṅkāl vāci

koḷḷavum intataṉmattukku akitam paṇṇinavarkaḷ keṅkaik
karaiyile kavilaiyai koṉṟa pāvattile

āka kallilum cempilum veṭṭi

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL,
RĀJARĀJA III, 7 (1223 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a gift of 22 kāśu to the temple for the
supply of ghee measured by Veḷḷaippaṉṟi nāḻi by Dēvakaṇmigaḷ of the
temple.

l.ta:
caṉikkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa
cittirai nāḷ uyyak koṇṭā

r vaḷanāṭṭu ampar nāṭṭu [a]mpāruṉtai a[ra]yaṉ civetavaṉap

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VIKRAMACHŌḺADĒVA-8TH YEAR (1126 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the names of some lamp-bearers,
Ādivarāhappichchar, Vellaippaṉṟipichar, etc., inthe temple of Āḻvār at
Tirumuṭṭam, who received some gift from Sriramaṉ Paramēshvara bhaṭṭaṉ
and also mentions the name of the village Sri Tribhuvana Mahādēvichchēri
eḻilūr in SrīVīranārāyana Chatūrvēdimaṅgalam.

l.ta: macoḻa tevaṟku yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatu tirumuṭṭat tāḻvār ko
yililt tiruviḷakkup piccakaḷil ātivarākap piccarum tiruviḷakkup

edition.l: piccarum veḷḷaippanṟippiccarum uḷḷiṭṭār vacam śrī viranārāyaṇa[ ccatu]

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE GŌPURA OF THE SAME
TEMPLE, RIGHT SIDE,
VĪRAPRATĀPA DĒVARĀYA MAHĀRĀYA MALLIKĀRJUNARĀYAR-
ŚAKA 1385 (1462-63 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a sarvamāṇya gift of the taxes imposed on
each Taṟi of by the Kaikōḷas and others artiśans of
Suṅgadavirthachōḻanallūr alias Tirumuṭṭam for the supply of food offerings
and conducting the service called Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ śandi instituted in the
temple, Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya-nāyanār at Suṅgamtavirtta-chōlanallūr alias
Tirumuṭṭam in Virudarājabhayaṅgaravaḷanaḍu.

l.ta: ṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭa nāṭu ko
ṉtaik kūṟṟattu cuṅkam tavitta coḻanallūrāṉa tirumuṭṭattu
uṭaiyār tirumuṭṭamuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku irākutta miṇṭaṉ canti
kkum nitta nimantaṅkaḷukkum inta ūr tirunāmattukkāṇi tirumaṭai
viḷākam uya • kaikkoḷar peril taṟikkum pala paṭṭaṭaik kuṭikaḷ
peṟkum āyam kāṇikkai cammātam piṟavari viṉiyokam ma
ṟṟum pala upātiyum uya • caṟuvamāniyam āka cettapaṭi
āle inta mutal koṇṭu irākutta miṇ •

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON SAME PLACE
•••••••••• ŚAKA 14[24] (1502 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription seems to register some gift made to
meet out the requirement of the temple of Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya Nāyaṉār.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME GŌPURA; LEFT SIDE.
VĪRAPRATĀPA DĒVARĀYA MAHĀRĀYA MALLIKĀRJUNARĀYAR-
ŚAKA 1383 (1460-61 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the construction of the Mahāmaṇḍapa in
the temple of Tirumuṭṭamuḍaiya nāyaṉār, probably by the chief
Ekā[m]baranāda Kachchiyarāyaṉ of Viḻandai, who had several birudas like
Rāhuttaṇavālaṉ, Arasamārtāṇḍaṉ, Vayira Vīragaṇḍan, Arasanārāyanaṉ, etc.,

l.ta: ṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa nāṭu kuṭātāṉ pū
ttu ekāteciyum comavāramum peṟṟamirapati nāḷ cuṅkam tavi
tta coḻanallūrāṉa tirumuṭṭattu uṭaiyār tirumuṭṭamuṭaiya nā
kaṅkai viṭaṅkap perumāḷ cipatta pattaṉ viḷaṉtai ēkā[m*]para

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
NITYĒŚVARA TEMPLE-ON THE SAME PLACE; ABOVE AN
IMAGE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to a person Viḍaikoṇḍa Tambirāntōḻaṇ-
Mānakkañjāraṉ who probably constructed the temple (Tirupati).

edition.p: Above an image with a beaer (an animal)

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE FIRST PRĀKĀRA
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1504 (1582 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the wall of the temple Ādivarāhamūrti at
Tirumuṭṭam that was constructed by Koṇḍama Nāyaka, son of Vaiyappa
Krishnappa Nāyaka.

l.ta: varuṣam tirumuṭṭam ātiva[rā]ka muṟtikku vaiyyappa kiṟuṣṇappa nā
yakkar kumāra koṇṭama nāyakkar kaṭṭivitta tirumatiḷ tiruppaṇi §

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1504 (1582 A.D.)
inscription.p: This is a duplicate of the above inscription.

l.ta:
ppa nāyakkar kumārar koṇṭama nāyakkar (ce)yavitta tirumatil
tiruppaṇi

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VĪRPRATĀPA VĪRAVENKAṬAPATIDĒVA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1514 (1592 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of the village Chilarabūru in
Kurichipattu, a sub-division of Pallikoṇḍapaṭṭanamsima to the temple of
Ādivarāha-perumāḷ by Avasaram Kōnappa, son of Timmarāsu, the grandson
of Haṇḍeriga-Gaṅgādharāya, to whom it was granted as an agrahāra by
Koṇḍamanāyaniṅgāru.

inscription.p:
This inscription registers a gift of land situated in Arasūr for
constructing a feeding house in the temple of Ādivarāhasvāmin to Rāyaśam
Aṇṇaya, son of Venkaṭaya and grandson of Kaliyappapuṭṭi Bhaṭṭa of the
Vishnuvardhana gōtra by Venkaṭappanāyani Ayyavāru.

inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of land located in the village
Yalamaṅgala to the servants of the temple of Ādimūrti at Śrimūshnam by
Venkaṭarāvu Kōnēṭayya, the agent of Koṇḍāmanāyani
Krishnappannāyanayya and the temple authorities.

ratnasiṁhāsanārūḍhulai prithivi sāmrājyaṁ śēyucuṁḍaṁgānu

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL
OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA
VENKATAPATIRĀYA, SUBHANU (1643 A.D. ?).
inscription.p:
This inscription narates that a certain Virisaḍaiyarāmaṇ performed
severe penance for 18 months and obtained the conch, discuss and bow
from the god Adivarāgar.

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE NORTH
WALL OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA
VIJAYANAGARA: VI7RAPRATAPA SRIRANGADĒVA MAHARAYA-
ŚAKA 1508 (1586 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of the village Arantāṅgi, otherwise
known as Ādivarāhakoṇḍasamudram in Chōḻa-Uttamapurappaṟṟu of
Vaḻudalambaṭṭusāvaḍi in Veṇṇaiyūr nāḍu, a sub-division of Vaḍagarai
Rājādhirāja vaḷanāḍu in Chōḻamaṇḍalam, for special worship and the
maintenance to the temple of Ādivarāhaperumāḷ temple by Kāḷañji
Mallappa Nāyaka son of Mārappa Nāyaka, for the merit of Vaiyappa
Kṛishṇappa koṇḍama Nāyaka.

l.ta:
ṭṭu vaḻutalampaṭṭuc cāvaṭi aṉucāramāṉa coḻauttamapurapaṟṟil
kāṭārampamāṉa naṟṟam aṟantāṅkikku

piṟutināmamāṉa ātivarāka koṇṭa camuttiram vaiyyappakkiṣṭiṇappak
koṇṭama nāyakkarayyaṉu

kku puṇṇiyamāka mārappa nāyakkar kumārar kāḷañci mallappa nāyakkar
tirumuṭṭam cuvāmi ātivarākap perumāḷu

kku viceṣa tiruvārātaṉattukkum tiruppaṇikkumāka utakatārā
pūṟuvamāka kaṭṭaḷai iṭṭu cilācāṉam paṇṇi

kuṭutta[pa*]paṭiṉāle anta kiṟāmattil nāṉkellai uḷpaṭṭa cakala
camutāyap piṟāttiyam cuvāti ātivarākamūrtti śrīpaṇṭāra

ttile aṉupavittuk koḷḷak kaṭavārakavum itukku viceḻakaṭṭaḷai
tirumañca kaṭṭaḷaikku teṅkāy oṉṟu pa[ḻa]m pattu

teṉṟu cakkarai pala oṉṟu veḷḷāṟṟu tirumañcaṉa orukuṭam tirumālai
eṭṭu amutupaṭi tūṇi kaṟiyamutu palām paḷam uḷpaṭṭa

niṟṟakkaṭṭaḷaip paṭittaram ceṉṟa [yi]tiruppaṇikkumāka •••••••••• nāyakkar
inta kiṟāmam caṟuvamāṉiyamāka śrī paṇṭā

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VIJAYANAGARA: SRIRAṄGADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1505 (1583 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of two villages viz., Saṉṉānattam
and Kīḻāvuḍaiya nattam in Ālambāḍipaṟṟu of Buvanēgaviraṉ paṭṭanam to
Rāyasam Veṅkāṭayar, son of Kōnamarasaiyar, a servant of Koṇḍama
Nāyaka, for food offerings, lamps and maintening garden in the temple of
Ādivarāha perumāḷ. The gift was made by Koṇḍama-nāyakkar along with
the nāṭṭavar of Paṭṭaṇa-sīrmai and Eṭṭu-paṟṟu-sīrmai.

l.ta:
kāṟtikaiyum amāvāciyum peṟṟa canivārattu nāḷ vaiyyappa
kiṣṇappanāyakkar kumā

nāyakkar ayyaṉum paṭṭaṇaccīrmai ṉāṭṭava āt • • nayiṉār
eṭṭuppaṟṟu ṉāṭṭava

rum koṇṭama nāyakkar vācalil irukkum konamaracayyar puttirar
irāya

tana paṭṭayam naṭuviṉ maṇṭalam
vaḻutalam pa
ṭṭu cāvaṭi veḷḷāṟṟukku vaṭakku puvaṉekaviraṉ paṭṭaṇa

ellaikkum meṟku veḷ

ḷāṟṟaṅkaraikku vaṭakku vattirāyateṟṟu ellaikku kiḻakku uṭaiūr
ellaikkum cīyappāṭi ellai

kkum teṟku inta nāṉkellaiyum uḷpaṭa tirumuṭṭam ātivarākap
perumāḷukku irāya

yar ṉaivettiyattum neyviḷakkum tirunantavaṉattukkum
tārāpūṟuvammāka tārai v[ār*]ttu kuṭuttapaṭi

ṅkai karaiyile kārām
pacuvai taṉma[m*]paṇṇiṉa palaṉ peṟuvā[r*]

intat taṉmakku akutam paṇṇiṉaper keṅkai karaiyile kārām
pacuvai koṉṟa toṣattile povā[rā*]ka §

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1504 (1582 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the construction of the compound wall
besides attending to other holy services in the temple Ādivarāhamūrthi by
Koṇḍamanāyakkar, the son of Vaiyappanāyakkar.

edition.l: ātivarāka muṟtikku vaiyyappa ṉāyakka[ kra] ṣṇa

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VIJAYANAGARA: SRIRAṄGADĒVA MAHĀRĀYA,
ŚAKA 1504 (1582 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of the village Pirāṇduṟai situated in
Kurichchipaṟṟu in Eṭṭuppaṟṟu of Padinēḻu Paṟṟu for performing sacred bath,
food offerings burning lamps and maintaining flower garden in the temple
of Ādivarāha-perumāḷ by Rāyasam Venkaṭaiyar, son of Kōnamarasaiyaṉ, a
servant of Koṇḍama Nāyaka, son of Vaiyappa Krishṇappa Nāyaka.

l.ta: itaṉmel cellaniṉṟa viṣa varuṣattil tulānāyaṟṟu k

tirumuṭṭam ātivarākap perumāḷukku poṉparappi cūnta patineḻupaṟṟil
eṭṭuppaṟṟil kuṟiccippa

ṟṟil pirāntuṟai vaiyyappakiṣṇa ṉāyakkar kumāra koṇṭama nāyakkar
vācalil irukkum konamaracay-

yaṉ puttirar irāyacam veṅkaṭa • t[ā*]rāpūṟuvamāka taraivāttuk
kuṭuttapaṭi patineḻupaṟṟu naṭṭavarka
kaḷom pirāntuṟai nāṉkellaikkul nañcai puñcainta a

p perumāḷu[kku] apiṣekka kaṭṭaḷaikkum ṉaivettiyattiyattukkum
neyiviḷakku tirunantavaṉattukkum irāya

m veṅkaṭayar naṭatti[na] taṉma inta taṉmattai naṭattiṉaper
keṅkaik karaiyile kārām pacuvai tāṉam

paṇṇiṉa palaṉ peṟuvar inta taṉmattukku akitam paṇṇiṉaper
keṅkaik karaiyile kārā[m*] pacuvai ko

inscription.p:
This inscription records that Koṇḍamma-nāyanigaru of the Kāśyapa
gōtra, had remitted the Jōdi and Virāḍu taxes collected from 38 villages
and gifted to the temple in Bhōnagiripaṭnam-sīma at the instance of
Āchchyutappanāyaniṅgaru, son of Baiyappanāyaningaru. The latter also gave
some villages and groves for building gōpuras, prākāras and maṇḍapas in
addition to the gift ornaments made to the god besides constructed some
tanks in the villages.

mahārāyaluṁgārupenugoṁḍa nagaramaṁdu ratnasiṁhāsanārūḍhulai

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARĀGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE WEST WALL
OF THE SAME PRĀKĀRA.
inscription.p:
This inscription quotes verses from Śrimushṇamāhātmya in
Varāhapurāṇa, which specifically mentions that one should conduct 12
monthly festivals throughout the year and also make provision to maintain
processional vehicles on the festivities to gain the merit.

nityapuṣkariṇī modalaina paṁḍreṁḍḍu tī¨rthālakunnu śrīmuṣṇamahātyaṁ
varāha-

tī¨rthamaṁdu veṁcēpuśēśi puṁgali naivēdya-

ṁmakaramāsaṁ śuklapakṣaṁ paṁccamināḍu braṁhma vāhanaṁ mīda
vēṁcē·iṁpu śēśi braṁhmakapāḷānanu tī¨rtha vāri naivēdyaṁ [dadhyōdanaṁ]
|| lābhamāsaṁ pau¨rṇamināḍu garuḍavāhanaṁ mīda • • • • •

vēṁcēpu śēśi naivēdyaṁ paramānnaṁ | mīnamāsaṁ śuklapakṣaṁ trayōdasi
vāhanaṁ mīda lakṣmīnārāyaṇa tī¨rthānaku svāmini vēṁcēpu śēśi
naivēdyaṁ citrānnaṁ ||

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARĀGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL
•••••••••• ŚAKA, 13[55] (1433 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Stonemissing. This inscription seems to register the construction of a
maṇḍapa in the temple Ādivaraha Nāyaṇār by Ēkāmbaranādar
Kachchiyarāyar.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARĀGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME WALL.
VĪRAPRATAPA PRAUḌHA VIRUPĀKSHA DE7VA MAHĀRĀYA WHO
WAS PLEASED TO WITNESS THE ELEPHANTS, HUNT-
ŚAKA 139[3] (1471 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription seems to register a gift of land to the temple by
Rāya Rahuttamiṇḍa-[Ma]llikārjuṉa-kachchirāya.

l.ta:
ṇṭuruḷāniṉṟa piṟava •••virupākṣa tevamaharā • • piṟu ••••• paṇṇi
aru[ḷā] niṉṟa cakā

kuttamiṇaṭa ••••• ṟāmatiraṉ ca ••••• tiraṉ maṇavāḷaṉ
vāḷaracar maṇavāḷaṉ

•••llikāccaṉa kacciyarāyar tirumuṭṭa muṭaiya perumāḷ āti •••nāyanār
ko •••

nānkellaikku uḷpaṭṭa nañcai puñcai melnokkiṉa maram kīḻnokkina
kiṇa

inscription.p:
This inscription register the gift of 14 villages in favour of the
temple of Ādivarāha-perumāḻ by Achchyutappanāyaniṅgāru, son of
Bayappanāyaniṅgāru, grandson of Udayagiri Timmanāyaniṅgaru, for
undertaking various activities like offerings, processions, festivals, repairs, etc.
Also states that the images of Maṇavāla, Sūḍikkuḍuttanāchchiyār and the
Āḻvārs had been set up in the temple.

inscription.p:
This inscription states that the wall of the temple Ādivarāhamūrti at
Tirumuṭṭam that was constructed by Koṇḍama Nāyaka, son of Vaiyappa
Krishnappa Nāyaka.

l.te: svasti śrī śakābdaṁ 1504 yiṁddumīda naḍacē citrabhānu saṁvvatsa-

raṁ tirumuṭṭaṁ ·ādivarāhamū¨rttiki yī tirumuduḷu kaiṁkaryaṁ
bayyappanāyani kṛ-
ṣṇappanāyani komāruṁḍu koṁḍanāyaṁḍu ceṁcina ttirupaṇi ||

inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of two garlands every day to be
offered to the deity Ādivarāhasvāmin and Ambujavaḷḷi-Nāñchāramma by a
maid of honour who is attached to the harem of
KoṇḍamanāyaniKrishanappa Nāyaka.

kriṣṇapanāya-

ni nagari hegaḍigate kriṣṇavēṇikaśrivatsāṁkaṁ manavāḷa mahāmuni

julacātanu ·ādivarāhasvāmiki ·aṁbujavalli nāṁcāraṁmaku vubhaya

tirumāleluku kaṭṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1916.)
SRIMUSHNAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
BHŪVARAGAHAVA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE-ON THE SAME
TEMPLE.
•••••••••• ŚAKA 1504 (1582),

l.te: svasti śrī śakābdaṁ 1504 yiṁdu mīda naḍaci citrabhānu saṁvatsaraṁ ti-
rumuṭaṁ ·ādivarāhamū¨rtiki yī tirumadaḷukaiṁkaryaṁ bayappanā-

yini kriṣṇappanāyani komāruṁḍu koṁḍamanāyaṁḍu cēyiṁcina
tirupaṇi

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1916.)
KILPATTU, VILLUPURAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A ROCK IN THE TANK NEAR MĀMBALAPAṬṬU.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the measuring rod of 30ft and 20ft.
respectively.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1916.)
KILPATTU, VILLUPURAM TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
•••••••••• ON ANOTHER ROCK ON THE BUND OF THE SAME
TANK
•••••••••• 3RD YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription seems to the construction of a tank by
Kuṉṟamuḍaiyāṉ Maliyakularāyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1916.)
TIRAKKOL, WANDIWASH TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
JAINA IMAGES-ON THE EAST FACE OF THE BUILDER
CONTAINING JAINA IMAGES. [PARAKĒSARIVARMAṈ]-8TH YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is much damaged. It registers a gift of sheep for
supplying ghee to the Jaina temple (Paḷḷi) at Taṇḍapuram in Poṉṉūr nāḍu
a sub-division of Venkuṉṟa kōṭṭam by Eṟanandi alias Naratoṅga
Pallavaraiyaṉ, a native of Nelvēli in Nelvēli nāḍu a sub-division of
Teṉkarai Paṇaiyūr-nāḍu in Chōḻamaṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1916.)
TIRAKKOL, WANDIWASH TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
•••••••••• ON THE WEST FACE OF THE SAME BOULDER.
CHŌḺA, RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAṈ-22ND YEAR.
inscription.p:
This inscription is much damaged. It registers a gift made to Jaina
temple by Gaṅgaśūrapperumpalli at Rā[jēndra]puram.

l.ta: mmāyil keṅkaiyiṭai kumariyiṭai eḻunūṟṟuk kātattum ceytār

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1916.)
TIRAKKOL, WANDIWASH TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p: Much damaged. This inscription registers the gift of gold for a lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1916.)
TIRAKKOL, WANDIWASH TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE ROCK TO THE EAST FACE OF THE SAME BOULDER.
CHŌḺA, PARAKĒSARIVARMAṈ-12TH YEAR.
inscription.p: Much damaged. Purport is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1916.)
TIRAKKOL, WANDIWASH TALUK,
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
VARADHAMĀNA TEMPLE-ON TWO PILLARS OF MAṆḌAPA IN
FRONT OF THE TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the gift of some pillars by Iḍaiyāṟaṉ
Aṭkoṇḍāṉ Māvīraṉ of Arumoḻidēvapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1916.)
TOṆḌŪR, GINGEE TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON THE VINNAMPĀṞAI ROCK TO THE LEFT OF THE IMAGE OF
VIṆṆARĀJA
PALLAVA: [VIJAYA-ŚĪYAPARUMAR] •••••
inscription.p: Much damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1916.)
TOṆḌŪR, GINGEE TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE SAME ROCK.
CHŌḺA: PARAKĒSARIVARMAṈ, ‘WHO TOOK THE HEAD OF VĪRA
PAṆḌYA’-5TH YEAR (961 A.D.)
inscription.p:
This inscription registers the gift of 10 sheep which is neither died
nor aged for burning lamps in the temple of Kiḍanda perumāḷ by
Suramittap-Perumaṉṟāḍi, son of Irukaṇṇaṉ residing at Kiḻvaḻi Toṇḍur in
Śiṅgapura nāḍu.

l.ta: ••••• cāvā mūvāp perāṭu pattu āṭuṅkuṭutto ṉivai koṇṭu ilakku

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1916.)
TOṆḌŪR, GINGEE TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ONTHE BOULDER IN A FIELD NEAR THE SAME ROCK
PALLAVA. VIJAYADANTIVIKRAMAVARMAṈ-6TH YEAR (802 A.D.)
inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription seems to registers the gift of 15
kaḻañjugold by Viṇṇakōvaraiyar. The gift was taken charge of by the ūrār
of Aruvāgūr.
(Published in SII, XII, No. 42.)

l.ta:
kovicayatanti vikkiramaparumaṟkku yāṇṭāṟāvatu ciṅkapura nāṭṭu
kiḻvaḻi
aruvā kūroṅkaiyeḻuttu viṇṇakovayare kuṉṟaṉār ērikkup pati

edition.p: A small cavity is made on the stone that damaged some letters in it.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1916.)
MĒLAICHCHĒRI, GINGEE TALUK,
SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE ROCK CUT CAVE. PALLAVA:
CHANRDĀDITYA ••••••••••
inscription.p:
This inscription states that the (rock-cut) Śiva temple called Sri
Śikhari Pallavēśvaram, was excavated by the king Chandrāditya of
Sinha(Siṁha)pura.
Published in S.I.I. Vol. XII, No. 115.
